title: The best resort and the best staff
review_text: Our stay at the Bvlgari Resort Bali was truly unforgettable. We booked the 5-bedroom mansion, and while the property itself is absolutely stunning, the real highlight of our experience was the exceptional service from our butlers, Oni and Devin. They went far beyond what anyone could reasonably expect — attentive, thoughtful, proactive, and somehow always one step ahead of every request. From organizing everything seamlessly during our stay to even helping us find amazing nannies for the children, they made what could have been a complicated group trip feel effortless and relaxing. A special thank you to Devin, who genuinely went above and beyond. Our friends staying at the Presidential Villa did not speak English, and Devin kindly helped them throughout their stay because he speaks Chinese. That level of care, patience, and generosity made a huge difference for everyone and did not go unnoticed. Luxury hotels exist everywhere, but people like Oni and Devin are what make a place truly special. They created an experience that felt personal, warm, and unforgettable. We left with incredible memories because of them, and we are deeply grateful. Thank you again to the entire Bvlgari Bali team — but especially to Oni and Devin for being absolute stars.
insider_tip: Travel time from airport is about 1h and there’s usually a lot of traffic

title: Encrible free edge pool.
review_text: Excellent adventure of 13 friends (a newly married couple)! The resort Bvlgari contemplates an urbanized area with a typical architecture carved in the landscape in sucalcos, combining local materials, with very well-treated green areas (ornamental plants of tropical origin). Silence is soothing. It has an incredibly prepared and trained reception team. Sympathy and efficiency is genuine (Ms Oni and Mr Lanang). The villas (room) are generous overlooking the Indian Ocean, breathing comfort and daily cleanliness. The pools are spacious and clear. The food at the main restaurant was varied, between Italian and Indonesian cuisine. Notable, the beach in front has strong waves, but the resort offers other leisure options. See you next time!

title: Unmatched luxury and service – truly world-class
review_text: I’ve stayed at some of the best resorts globally, and Bvlgari Resort Bali is on another level. From the moment you arrive, everything feels intentional, private, and elevated. The villas are absolutely stunning—spacious, immaculately maintained, and designed with a perfect blend of Balinese architecture and Bvlgari’s signature Italian elegance. Waking up to the ocean views from the cliffside is something you don’t forget. What truly makes this property exceptional is the service. The entire team is incredibly warm and attentive, but I want to give a special mention to our butler, Winsu, who made the experience seamless from start to finish. He anticipated everything before we even had to ask—whether it was arranging transport, coordinating dining, or just making sure every detail in the villa was perfect. That level of personalized service is what separates a great hotel from a world-class one. The food and overall experience were equally impressive. Breakfast overlooking the ocean, sunset at the bar, and the overall atmosphere throughout the resort were all top-tier. The property itself is very private and peaceful—once you’re there, you won’t want to leave. It’s clear why so many guests consider this one of the best resorts in the world—the villas, views, and especially the butler service consistently stand out in reviews. (Tripadvisor) If you’re looking for a true luxury escape in Bali, this is it. Will definitely be coming back

We had the degustation at Il Rostorante - best choice ever! So creative with such ‘Bulgari’ couture presentation. Their simple is bread is already so good we asked for seconds. They have different cocktails at different venues so be sure to try them all out! The creative drinks are good - sake with gin, pepper & dehydrated orange peel - but the classics with a twist are even better! Mt favourite was their Negroni. I also really liked their village tour - one of the most unique ang authentic experiences I’ve had. Kudos to our guides Metra & Gayatri. A combination buggy and foot foot we got to really catch a slice of real Balinese life. The Spa is also another worthy of mention. Really really good massages - I haven’t had one so good in a while! The villas are all well appointed with amenities, housekeeping and room service en par. Most important of all I am very grateful to our butler Parma, ever attentive & personable, for making all of these possible! I am truly spoilt by his resourcefulness to plan, coordinate & troubleshoot everything throughout (even on short notice!) leaving me free to enjoy myself without stress or worry! Pleasant little surprises like the occasional upgrades also kept us perked up!

title: Bvlgari eccellenza italiana senza paragoni
review_text: 🌟 If 7 stars existed, Bulgari Resort Bali would deserve every one of them. The service is flawless, the staff exceptional. Special thanks to Rose, our attentive and discreet butler, and to Chiara, the quality manager, whose dedication and precision made every moment simply perfect. 🍽️ The culinary experience was unforgettable thanks to Executive Chef Alessandro Mazzali. His talent in the kitchen is matched only by his warmth and humility—he made us feel truly at home, turning every meal into a celebration. 🧼 The villas are immaculate, with outstanding cleanliness. Complimentary laundry and minibar services add to the comfort. Every day, we found sweet treats and delightful surprises at the foot of the bed, like magical gifts. In the wardrobe, a Bulgari Resort Bali–branded bag and 2 hats, offered as a gift—elegant touches that stay with you. 🌊 Although the private beach was under maintenance, we were offered free access to the White Rock Beach Club on Melasti Beach—a dreamlike spot with crystal-clear water like a natural pool, and views of the ocean waves crashing against the coral reef. A private driver was also available to take us to the stunning beaches around Uluwatu. 🍳 Breakfast available 24 hours a day, a premium villa upgrade, and late check-out made everything even more seamless. 🥂 A heartfelt thank-you to General Manager Carlo Bevilacqua, who came to greet us the night before departure during dinner and offered a toast to connection—a refined and thoughtful gesture that made our final evening truly special. 🎁 At the end of our stay, the resort even arranged shipment to Italy for my sarongs, gifted during our visit to the Uluwatu Temple and the mesmerizing Kecak dance, which I had accidentally left behind. A gesture of genuine care that speaks volumes. 🌍 I’ve visited luxury resorts all over the world, but none compare to Bulgari Resort Bali. A place where every wish is fulfilled, every detail is considered, and every moment becomes a memory.

title: A Wonderful Bvlgari Bali Experience
review_text: My first impression of Bvlgari Bali was that it's a well designed resort, not surprisingly it was designed by Antonio Citterio Patricia Viel (ACPV) architects. It's not a new resort, it was opened in 2006 but very well maintained. The staffs were amazing, the butler and his team anticipated all our needs and did it gracefully. For example, during our lunch (all seafood), they noticed that our daughter did not eat at all because she did not like seafood, they noticed the chef and immediately served the same pasta that she ordered last night so she would not be hungry during the lunch. Or when we went to see ogoh-ogoh parade, they saw that we were a bit hesitant because it was very crowded, two of them went with us all the way to make sure we felt safe. A special mention to Adi and Oni who elevated our stay. The location is just amazing, it's very serene. I think I can just sit on the terrace all day looking at the horizon, we even got a beautiful rainbow on our last morning. The only thing is that the funicular was being serviced during our stay so we could not go down to the beach. I must admit that I was skeptical before our stay but I left very impressed.

title: Luxury on the Cliff
review_text: On the cliffs at Uluwatu, Bulgari is apart from the chaos that is Denpasar. The resort tops a steep cliff and site and rooms face south into the Indian Ocean. By far the most compelling reason to visit is its remarkable beach. When we visited, there was fourteen foot surf (which due to the steep cliffs we could not actually hear from our room). There is a 300 yard long funicular lift down to the beach, where there is a two story shack/restaurant and a beach club with lounge chairs and a small bar. We had all our lunches (food was delicious) at the restaurant, and LOVED our service. Thank you, Surya, Pandi and Niko for your friendly companionship. (Niko is a killer photographer). The resort itself is ultra-luxe. The rooms are, believe it or not, both larger and fancier than any of the Amans we have stayed it, full of beautiful dark wood, high ceilings and many with pools and decks, as well as large outside living areas. We had a butler, Stephanus, who was omnipresent and super helpful. We have never been fans of Bulgari's heavy handed high fashion jewelry (who can afford?) and the nearly three foot high Bulgari logo below the pool was (candor is warranted here) ultra-cheesy. But this is a really fine resort, with beautiful facilities and exceptional personal service. Worth a visit! (The taxi ride to/from airport was an hour and a half and super depressing. Tourism has not been kind to this lovely island).
insider_tip: Select rooms on third or fourth tier for superior views. Think about being west, away from pool and restaurants for max quiet and privacy.

title: Unbeatable beauty and vibe
review_text: Giving this 5 stars as we booked this on points and were overwhelmed with the beauty of the property and the room. I read these reviews prior to booking and in my opinion the pictures and descriptions of the rooms don’t do them justice. They are large and well laid out to feel super special. Two smart Toto toilets and a dressing hall add to the convenience of the layout. The only improvement I would suggest is having 2 shower heads. We had a cliff side villa and you could hear the waves roll in from bed - it was awesome. The bar at sunset was one of the most picturesque places I have ever been in my life, and the seaside restaurant and beach area were very special. The resort did not feel over-run with other guests, it felt very private. All these positives probably overshadowed a few areas for improvement. Overall we were not impressed with the food on the property. Breakfast was bland and the appertivo they give you at the bar was a nice touch in theory, but was mediocre in taste. We ate at Il Ristorante, and while a fun experience - did not feel this was worth it. The only thing I would order again was the waygu. The staff was incredibly friendly but slow and not super attentive (with the beach bar being an exception here). The butler service was a bit disjointed and no different than having a central concierge desk with many workers at it. I would definitely recommend the hotel to others, it achieved our goals of marking the end of our trip in an incredibly beautiful, romantic, and special way - but will depend on what you are optimizing for.
insider_tip: Not a very walkable property (use buggy to get around)

title: Amazing stay at Bulgari Bali
review_text: Bulgari in Bali far exceeded our expectations. The service was amazing and the staff was so welcoming and helpful. The resort itself was incredibly beautiful and the views of the ocean were unparalleled. The staff went above and beyond for us, especially our butler, Rizky. Our luggage didn’t arrive from the US due to the airlines fault and he went out and bought my family clothes to wear. He was also incredibly attentive to all of our other needs and wants throughout our stay. The hotel guide, Merta, was also exceptional. He took my family and guided us through the Uluwatu Temple at sunset and brought us to watch the Kecak dance (all a must do!) and was such a pleasant and informative guide. He also took us on a guided walk of the local banjar and it was fascinating. He left no detail unexplained and we felt truly immersed in the culture thanks to Merta. We also loved the purification ceremony the hotel offers. I would recommend doing it early in the morning when it is cool out and there is a breeze.

title: Sets the Standard for Service
review_text: Our recent stay was simply amazing. Every need met or surpassed. The service and energy of the staff and associates is perhaps the best I have received in 50+ years of traveling. Every morning at breakfast we were greeted with such sincere warmth and efforts to start our day in such a positive attitude. Diah greated us every morning with one thing in mind - make us feel welcomed and take care of our needs. After the first day our names were memorized and we were greeted at every opportunity with our name and was there anything they could do to make our stay more enjoyable. The views are incredible and the beach is very private with full service available. We received a small gift or snacks each night, room cleaning prompt and seamless, transportation at an instant, security was top notch and privacy was the ambiance. As someone previously mentioned - this is not the place to go if you want nightlife but if you want scenery, food and refreshments looking over the cliffs this is the place. We were fortunate enough to stay 5 nights and wished we could have stayed 50. The organization behind this property obviously has and adheres to excellent training because this level of service “just doesn’t happen” on its own. This should be the model taught to other exclusive properties in the world as it sets the standard.

title: Great location, amazing villas, but needs improvement
review_text: Situated at one of the most stunning locations in Bali, it is one of the finest resort in Bali, and can easily come in top 5 most luxury resort all over Bali where we can see many reputed chain hotels. BVLGARI Bali is Bulgari Hotel’s second luxury resort and as of now the only beach resort of their entire portfolio. A joint project between Bulgari Hotels & Marriott Hotels, opened its doors on 2006, and went through a major renovation on 2016, this resort is in great shape after almost 18 years in its operation and felt hardly any need of renovation. BVLGARI Bali is a fusion of Balinese culture with Italian architecture. Though we originally planned for Mandapa Ritz Carlton Reserve, but some change of plans brought us here. We got to stay at their highest category of One-Bedroom Villa, i.e. Ocean Cliff Villa. The view is stunning from up here. The villa was big, but we felt the plunge pool is smaller in size compared to the new hotels we stayed later. The resort houses 3 restaurants. Sangkar, the all day dining restaurant. IL Ristorante, the Italian fine dining restaurant which serves dinner only. Last but not the least, La Spiaggia, which can be accessed by cable car and serves fresh catch sea-foods. Due to our allergic restrictions, we had to go a vegetarian set menu for IL Ristorante which we felt bit bland according to our taste buds but we thoroughly enjoyed the food at Sangkar. Being at Uluwatu, the hotel is at a cliff, and you need to take the cable car to 150m down and another 100 steps to access the beach. It does worth it, totally. For us the underwhelming part was the Spa which was disappointing to say the least. Shared the feedback later with Mr. Anthony, and he addressed it along with other feedbacks, but still haven’t received the followup mail’s response yet The main pool area is beautiful and it has amazing sunset vantage points which are not to be missed. Make sure to come at the Bulgari Bar for evening cocktail to enjoy the sunset 🥂
insider_tip: Have a drink at the bar, and enjoy the most amazing sunset of Bali.

title: Heaven on earth. Service and facilities beyond compare!
review_text: We have been to the Bulgari Bali several times now over the years and each time we ask ourselves "why go anywhere else?" It is truly that special. Even by Balinese standards, the warmth and hospitality shown by the staff and management are beyond compare, from the welcome at reception to the nightly gifts left in the room to the genuine smiles from everyone, eager to please and get you whatever you need. This can't always be easy. At their new higher price points, the Bulgari attracts much more of a "serve me NOW" crowd who treats the staff with less courtesy than other resorts we visited in Bali, but the staff are unshakeable. The facilities are absolutely immaculate. Not a grain of sand remained in our villa though we tracked in plenty of sand from the gorgeous (how many times can I use that word) private beach, reached by a manned hillavator. The villas are enormous and private, with plenty of space to spread out. A decadently large bathroom features two showers (one indoor, one outdoor) with a Toto automated toilet in a separate room. An enormous "walk through" closet provides plenty of storage and I am still dreaming of the ice cold bedroom with those gorgeous sheets and that perfect mattress. Very few air conditioners can get a villa down to 19 degrees in Bali, but these can, for those who like to sleep with it cold. There have been a few tradeoffs after COVID. The beach staff no longer serves meals on the beach, but instead invites you to the beach restaurant a short walk away. We actually ended up preferring that as the food was hotter and it was a nice break from the equatorial sun to enjoy the open-air restaurant. Dinner at IL RISTORANTE compared favorably to our extensive travels in Italy, and breakfast each morning was a delightful feast for the senses, overlooking the serenity of the glass sea. The Bulgari is rather isolated (good!) so if you're looking for a night club scene or a place to use as a base to go other places in Bali, I would not recommend it. Once you're inside the walls, if you're like us, you'll never want to leave. And why would you?

title: World class resort
review_text: We only ever go to the best hotels and resorts in the world, but the Bulgari Bali Resort completely exceeded all our expectations. For us, the best resort we have ever been to. We had a 24/7 butler, communication via WhatsApp was excellent - our wishes were always taken into account. Big praise to Indra and Stephanie from Guest Service, who organized restaurants, activities, etc. for us before we arrived. Our butler Debora was there for us every day for a week. She did such a great job. We are very grateful to her for that. Employees like that make the difference. Simon from the bar always had a surprise ready for us. He was so kind and looked after us incredibly well. Swan from the restaurant also knew exactly what we liked and always had everything ready for breakfast. Everyone worked with great attention to detail. The head of the restaurant in the evening, unfortunately we don't know his name, was so accommodating with our special requests. The best service employee ever! We were also always given the best table in the restaurant. We thought that was really great 🥰🫶🏼👌🏼 Our Ocean Cliff Villa was simply fantastic. The view of the open sea is unique. We even had laundry service included. Only the light in the bathroom is a bit dark. The whole complex is in top condition and is actually much nicer than on the homepage. Here in the resort you are not just a number. And you hardly have that anywhere else. Exclusivity instead of mass! That is what the guests are looking for. We also thought it was great that external guests are only allowed to go to the bar or the restaurant and not to the pool. The a la carte breakfast is excellent. Just sit and enjoy ☺️ The private beach is great. No other hotel has it. The staff keep it really clean. There is always someone collecting the plastic. We had a Bulgari overnight stay in our room almost every day 🥰🤩 and we were very happy about it. The only small criticism of the resort is the gym, which actually only has cardio equipment. That's too little for a 5-star hotel and no longer up to date. There should be an update here. We have already informed the management of this. We really enjoyed our stay and will be back soon. Thanks again to the entire Bulgari Bali team. We miss you already. See you soon! 🫶🏼❤️

title: Cliffside masterpiece
review_text: The Bulgari, Bali is an architectural beauty in a minimalist way perched on a cliff above the Indian Ocean facing south with lush landscaping. Our cliffside room was breathtaking with no rooftops to spoil the view and a huge outdoor living space which included a visit from three large monkeys who decided to look in on us. (don't feed or encourage these guys!) Overall a great property with attentive staff and good food so we're very glad we stayed there. This was a very different experience from our jungle hotel in Ubud, the Capella, which was also fantastic. If you're interested in something a little more on the adult side, this is a nice place to stay. The only serious concern was that one evening there was a wedding reception on the terrace above all the restaurants and bar and the noise from the DJ and singer were overwhelming and made conversation almost impossible. We could still hear them back in our room which was a long ways off. Another night of this intrusion and we might have left the property. There must be some option to limit the conflict. The other small issue was that the occupancy seemed low and the energy on the property felt a little flat and we actually like calm and quiet. Beautiful property and we would highly recommend for the decerning traveler.

title: Miraculous stay at Bvlgari
review_text: My husband, mom, and I had the privilege of experiencing the exquisite Bvlgari Resort Bali, and it was truly a dreamlike escape! From the moment we stepped foot on this enchanting property (we are lucky to experience the three bedrooms mansions) until the day we reluctantly bid farewell, our stay was an absolute embodiment of perfection. The check-in process was seamless, setting the tone for the remarkable journey that awaited us. The staff at Bvlgari Resort Bali are nothing short of extraordinary – their warm reception and unwavering commitment to enhancing our stay made us feel like cherished guests in a paradise of luxury. We extend our heartfelt gratitude to the exceptional team at Bvlgari Resort Bali who transformed our stay into an unforgettable masterpiece. From Yossi, Rose and Adeline, each of you played an instrumental role in crafting a truly wondrous experience. Your dedication and hospitality exceeded our highest expectations, and we are profoundly thankful for the indelible mark you left on our stay. Thank you! The property itself is a masterpiece of design and nature. The lush gardens, captivating pool areas, and awe-inspiring vistas of the ocean enveloped us in an ambiance of serenity and opulence that will forever hold a place in our hearts. Whether we were lounging beside the resplendent pool, indulging in culinary delights at the resort's exceptional restaurants, or relishing rejuvenating spa treatments, we were perpetually surrounded by an aura of beauty and comfort. The pinnacle of our stay was unquestionably the extraordinary service. The staff went above and beyond, ensuring our every desire was met, leaving us feeling pampered and cherished. Bvlgari Resort Bali is an Eden unto itself, and we departed with hearts brimming with gratitude and a treasury of cherished memories. If you seek an unforgettable retreat where opulence harmonizes with warmth and hospitality, Bvlgari Resort Bali beckons. We eagerly await our return to fashion more extraordinary moments at this exceptional haven!

title: Bravo Bulgari Resort Bali, Great Resort Matches Great Price
review_text: Dream-like and paradise-like are not enough to describe the experience in staying in one of finest luxury resorts in the world. From premium traditional villa interiors but with modern amenities to stunning vistas, the Bulgari Resort Bali doesn't not disappoint. Hospitality is another major plus, with Mr. Lanang serving as one of the best butlers around. Food understandably is on the high side, but can be improved in portioning and taste, e.g. small hollandaise portion on the eggs benedict. Will wait for the Il Ristorante Bali to get a Michelin star before trying it. Wouldn't miss the opportunity to stay again if given the chance.

title: A very relaxing stay!
review_text: Stayed from 21st to 23rd Jan 2023. Upon arrival, we were served a delicious welcome drink. Tour around the property in a buggy by butler Gede who was very attentive throughout our stay. Villa is huge. Bed and pillows very confortable. Loved the Bulgari toiletries. The shower gel, the shampoo, the conditioner and the body lotion smelled like you are in a spa each time I use them. Wanted to buy some from the gift shop but they don't sell them there. It will be nice if Bulgari sell them in gift pack. Gede kindly gave us some to bring home. Very nice gesture! All the service staff were extremely helpful. They were all so eager to please the guest. Special mention: Mr Indra at the dinner and breakfast restaurant. His EQ is superb. He is very customer orientated. He not only does his job well and went beyond and above. Well done Indra! And also Mr Ruddy - who served us at La Siaggia for a late lunch on arrival on 21st. His service is also excellent. He has a way to make guest like him. We have dined in all the 4 restaurants at this hotel. La Siaggia - the closet restaurant to the beach. We had the seafood platter. The seafood was fresh but prefer the dips to be spicer. The Buigari Bar - When you order the cocktails you will be served very delicious small bites to go with the cocktails. Very nice to watch the sunset there. Sangkar - The more casual dining where breakfast, lunch and dinner were served here. The beef carpaccio was very delicious, one of the best I have ever eaten. I think it was the dressing used. The rest of the dishes were also good. Il Ristorante Luca Fantin - We had a wonderful and delicious Italian dinner prepared by Chef Alessandro Mazali. Every course was very well executed. Both of us polished off every morsel. Yummy! Thank you Chef Alessandro! People with mobility issue will have problem accessing their private beach. There is in total about 100 steps to climb both ways. The stay here will not be completed without having a stroll along this beautiful beach. Overall, a very relaxing and tummy satisfying stay! Like to thank all staff for their excellent service throughout our stay here!
insider_tip: To dine in all the restaurant

title: Excellent New Years Break
review_text: Where to begin. We had a very good stay here recently which foremost was impacted by the excellent service, the really lovely staff and the kindness and forthcoming support by every member of staff. Everyone from the gardeners to the service staff and the chefs always has a big smile on their face and is willing to accommodate your wishes. Our butler Parma worked hard to make sure we were happy. The bar staff including Argus and Rudi made every drink to precise instructions and Niko at the bar by the beach was super forthcoming as well. The Chefs at breakfast made special vegan and other requested dishes without any problems. We had some issues initially with a food order, room check / some special requests to our villa as well as a fumigation cloud against mosquitos that moved through our villa open space but everyone went out of their way to address this and we received plenty of other goodies and attention as a result The location is very secluded which is what we were after and nothing beats the dramatic funicular ride down to the beach. The beach is stunning if you venture our further and leave the hotel premises. We saw a family of stingrays just offshore on a pristine quiet day and there is a cool ship wreck The villa itself is also very quiet and had stunning views with a lot of space (in particular outdoor). We did private yoga classes a few times early in the mornings on our terrace which was incredible. The bar, public pool and event locations are also excellent and we loved the entire set up. The resort looks particularly stunning at night with all the lights on. Somebody mentioned its slightly older but we did not really notice this as everything is well maintained and full with beautiful Bulgari accessories. The other highlight in our mind was the food. From western dishes at Sangkar (including one of the best penne arrabiata we have had at any hotel) to Indonesian dishes (amazing Nasi Goreng) everything was just so good. The room service / eating at your villa was excellent as they set everything up in great detail and the quality of food does not get impacted (not sure how they get everything in pristine condition on the bumpy cobblestone pathways to your villa). New years eve dinner was spectacular with everything from Caviar to Lobster stunningly presented. We also received a daily cake delivery and special tea in the evening which was also great. Breakfast can be ordered to the villa all day which is also great. The only food option that left me wondering was the Pizza. It was more like a dry cracker with some rubbery cheese and for an Italian based resort should be much much better (especially since they make some very good pasta, pizza should be an easy one) The only other comment I would make is on the champagne / wine list. It seems to me that outside the Italian selection the wine list is a bit random and sometimes grossly overpriced. A regular sized bottle of 2012 Dom Perignon for ~£1,100 is significantly more than I have seen anywhere else even at places like the Arts Club in London or Nikki Beach in Saint Tropez and doesn't make much sense to me (especially since at the hotel next door it cost half...)
insider_tip: Try to aim for cliff villa or one with the number 30-40 which are just second row behind it

title: Unforgettable
review_text: I was not aware of this resort till our cab driver suggested we have a dinner there on our way back from Uluwatu to Seminyak. That experience compelled us to actually modify our itinerary and book a villa for the last night before we left Bali. The views of the Indian Ocean from the villas are to be seen to be believed, and the resort is a very quiet getaway from the rest of the world. Each villa also has a plunge pool, apart from the full fledged swimming pool in the premise. The restaurants (there are 3 of them) serve up delectable fares, and we did not find the need to step out of the resort for anything. Definitely pricey, but I think its worth it for pure self indulgence.

title: Wedding Inspection that was poor
review_text: We connected with the wedding team (Retno) to arrange for a wedding Inspection on 10 Sept. We started off being very excited for our trip as we have a mutual friend holding her wedding there next year in August and would like to have our ceremony there as well. Retno informed us that we were supposed to meet Intan, who unfortunately was on sick leave on that day. Upon arrival, we were informed that the Duty Manager would bring us around but instead, the Duty Manager sent a service staff (Coco) to show us around the property. Coco did the best job that he can (bringing us to the various villas) given that this is not his job scope, but we were let down by the Duty Manager who simply was too busy to entertain our queries. When asked about whether we can discuss the pricing and packaging, we were told to just Whatspp Retno. We came down specifically to Bali and to Bvlgari to have a face to face discussion and to sign an agreement but were let down by circumstances. Also, the level of service was pale in comparison to other resorts. We visited Alila, Six Senses, Kempinski and we were attended to very well. Among other things, we had complimentary drinks at the various wedding venues when we went to inspect but didn't even receive a glass of water in Bvlgari. While it is understandable that people fall sick, the service and the service recovery was subpar. Bvlgari is huge and it is definitely lovely and we appreciated Coco going above and beyond his job scope, but the Duty Manager on that day and the communication with the wedding team could have been done better. Two stars for being a lovely resort and for the good effort done by Coco. Everything else was underwhelming. Hopefully our mutual friend's wedding in August 2023 turns out well.

title: Amazing service Amazing resort
review_text: Reopening of the world after COVID-19 lock downs makes us wants to just do a short trip. We choose Bvlgari Bali pretty last minute. Arrival and transfer service went impeccably despite the resort were just coming back to life from a pretty long lock down and many of the staffs were only just beginning to come back. We have an ocean cliff view room and the rooms and view were great. However, what truly make this a really great resort is the people. Our butler Deborah did a great job, accommodating every of our requests including helping us order in food from the local babi guling restaurant. However, my advice would be that there is no need to try any local restaurants outside of the resort as all the best stuffs are here in the resort. The menu changes regularly so that you are not taking the same thing daily and the food tastes really good. The day manager Putu is truly amazing and I would say that he epitomize the meaning of great hospitality management. He speaks with pride of the resort he manages, telling us about the spa service and where the best views for sunset will be. When we came by one evening to see the sunset, which is at the loft right below the reception lobby, he personally carried down two huge arm chair and a table onto the loft area and brought us drinks. All that effort just so that we can enjoy the sunset! Memorable mention includes Merta who brought us out for the village walk. Growing up locally, he knows almost everyone in the village and gave us a very true look into the village life there. Vera the restaurant manager is also great. Not only did he take good care of us, when we asked about where the restaurant ships in their crackers and if we can get some in the village to bring back for our family to try, he went into kitchen brought out 2 bags of uncooked packed crackers for us to bring home. Other staffs who took good care of us include Moora the chet, Alex at the beach restaurant, Juan at the cliffside restaurant, Max the bulter (always cheerful and attentive) and the many others whose names we did not manage to collect. All in all, this would be our recommended place to go if you ever want to visit Uluwatu side of Bali.

title: Must Try This Luxury Hotel
review_text: Blessed enough to experience one of the best luxury hotels in Bali. Located at top of the hill in Uluwatu, this hotel is outstanding. From the check in till the next day, it never failed. The room is overlooking the sea and has private pool. Dining area also available. Butler is always 24hours available at your service. Having the longest inclinator going down to the beach is something that you have to experience. Not recommended for senior or very small children bcoz there are still a lot of steps you need to take. Afternoon tea is provided at certain hours and the canape were delicious. Jump to the next day for breakfast. title: Best for couple honeymoon
review_text: Easily the best resort for couple i've been to. The check in process was really swift, after that we had our personal butlers, Pak Edo & Wayan, deliver us to our villa with a buggy while introducing us to the resort facilities. Our villa number, 39, has a really clear view of the endless ocean. I mentioned that i booked the villa for honeymoon and they provided us with a happy honeymoon cake. The first thing me and my wife did was visit the beach. You had to ride a lift along the steep cliff and walk down quite a long stairs to reach the beach but it was really worth it. The beach feels very private and the beach attendant prepared us our beach chairs and provided us with cold water. At night, you can clealry see the stars in the sky and our butlers prepared us with a hot drink in a tumbler which tasted really good. Breakfast in Sangkar comes with a variety of buffet choices of cheese, cured meats, smoked fishes and cereals. There is also an a la carte menu available for order. I ordered the grilled baramundi and it was really tasty. You also get a wonderful view in the outdoor seating that faces towards the ocean. Thanks Bulgari for the wonderful stay, thanks Pak Edo & Wayan for taking good care of us.

title: It’s about Luxury
review_text: Four things that I relished while I was staying in Bulgari hotel Bali. First, the majestic of hotel architecture itself; combining traditional balinese heritage, the nature of balinese beach and ocean, with the luxury touch of Bulgari artistic style. Second, relentless surprise every day; customized video when the first time we entered the room, comforting and always-changing traditional snacks and ginger tea supplied everyday, until the appearance of micro gamelan in my room in the evening day. Third, magnificent services provided by the butler in every second; my wife is a big fan of rujak and I know it is super-hard to find this kind of food in the edge of uluwatu beach, yet the butlers always found it and deliver it to our cottage. Five star service. And four, I found it special when I discovered several rare books in my room describing the journey and the majestic of Bulgari brands itself. Relaxing and inspiring at the same time. After experience Bulgari, I realized that luxury is not a how price you pay but it always about service, surprise, customization, and attention to details.
insider_tip: Try and enjoy Sangkar Restaurant. The food is astonishing!

I was captivated by the sea view the moment we stepped into the lobby for check-in. We were chauffeured everywhere within the resort by a buggy and there is 24 hour butler service. Our package is inclusive of 60 minutes massage and both of us enjoyed it so much that we drifted to la-la land. They even allowed us to use the steam room prior to the massage. There is a private beach and guests were brought down (as the resort is built on a cliff) using the inclinator. This is a first for both of us. There is also a restaurant at the bottom. We opted for breakfast to be served at our villa. The service crew is so meticulous that they even showed a picture of an egg (via WhatsApp) to confirm that is the way we wanted it. We ordered “well done egg”. Each villa has a plunge pool and we did not use the public pool. We enjoyed 180 degrees sea view from our room. They extended our check-out time to ensure we enjoyed to our hearts pleasure. All the staff we met are so friendly and polite. They thanked us for visiting them and I felt touched cos they are appreciative. Everything was tip top and we had a memorable and enjoyable stay. Thanks to the team who work so hard to make sure the guests are well taken care of.

title: Wonderful resort and outstandingly attentive staff
review_text: Together with my partner I've spent one entire week at the Bulgari Resort on Bali in November 2019. Due to many positive experiences of friends and family already having visited the island, our expectations were somewhat high. Nonetheless, the resort - in particular thanks to its utterly hard working staff - managed to outdo all of them by far. To start with, all employees were outstandingly attentive and caring. It's easily perceivable and even palpable that the resort has successfully instilled into them the maxim that the guests' welfare is of paramount importance. Not only were all wishes fulfilled to our utmost satisfaction, what is more, some were virtually read from our thoughts. Secondly, it has to be highlighted that the resort is very well maintained and hence absolutely shipshape. The spa is set above a precipice with an arresting view of the shore of the Indian Ocean. Pristine plants border all paths between the guests' villas and the public areas such as the pool and the restaurants. Speaking of food, we relished the meals at the resort a lot. Both restaurants, a local and an Italian one, served remarkably palatable luncheons and dinners covering Indonesian and more familiar European tastes simultaneously. Nevertheless, the breakfast turned out to be our favourite meal for the rich choice of fresh exotic fruits of unparalleled taste. Finally, another positive aspect of our stay was the weather - even if this one cannot be attributed to the resort. According to the meteorological calendar it was supposed to be rainy. However, during our entire stay we didn’t experience one single drop of precipitation. In conclusion, rarely have we been so content with a hotel or resort. Therefore, not only would we recommend the resort, also we would spend our holidays there again without deliberation.

title: Dream Come True - Trip of a Lifetime!
review_text: My husband and I visited the beautiful Bulgari Resort Bali for our 40th anniversary over New Year's Eve of 2016. We stayed there for 8 days and loved every single minute. We decided then that we must return for our 45th anniversary, but I believe we must return before then. Every New Years' back at home, our memories are filled with our trip to Bulgari Resort Bali and how much we wish we were there! We hope to see our friends at Bulgari for New Year 2020! The New Years' Eve Celebration went far beyond my imagination! The festivity, food, music, staff, balloon drop, fireworks... all led to the best night of our lives! The resort pulled out all the stops to make sure that night was especially memorable for us. Just a few observations from our previous trip to Bulgari: The resort is so luxurious and so authentically Balinese. We felt like VIPs as soon as we arrived. This is how celebrities must feel when they travel! Every single staff member there was so attentive and friendly while always respecting privacy. We felt like the most special people at the resort. We stayed in an Ocean Cliff Villa with our own relaxing plunge pool. On arrival, the bathtub was filled with fresh flowers for a relaxing and aroma filled bath. My husband enjoyed the alfresco shower! The butler service was superb!! We enjoyed the in-room breakfast every morning. Loved the High Tea service in the bar overlooking the Indian Ocean in the afternoons! So relaxing! We were so touched by all the staff treating us like family. Every day someone gave us a gift to wish us Happy Anniversary. I'll never forget all the smiling and friendly people who worked at Bulgari. All of them came to the checkout area when we left to say goodbye to us, bringing us to tears. We hope to see them all again within the next year! We went to Bulgari Resort Bali for the beauty of the resort. We will return for the same but to also see all the wonderful people we met while staying there! Here are some of the outstanding staff we met at Bulgari, and hope to see them again: Luna - Very professional and caring Manager Dian, Edwin, Wayan: Butlers Extraordinaire! Didda, Putu, Susri: Excellent food/drink/service in Il Bar Radna: Gift Shop Agung: Hostess Il restaurant Gusti: Very friendly and helpful bellman Madè: In reception Gustu: Food bev mgr ..... and many others! We can never thank the staff at Bulgari Resort Bali enough for creating such a relaxing and memorable and romantic dream-com-true vacation!!

title: What a wonderful experience
review_text: This is definitely one of the best resorts I’ve ever visited. I was only there for a day but I’d never forget this amazing experience. Location wise you can say it’s a bit far from everything but as far as privacy is concerned, you’d fall in love with this beautiful beautiful resort. I recommend you to stay all day (even days) in this resort since you will have to make it worth. Bulgari Bali only provides villas which means there are no standard rooms like any other hotels do (it also means all their rooms start at a very high price). If you pay for that much money for a night, you’d better fully use their facilities and enjoy your whole day journey there. This resort is very quite and I could hardly see any guests during my stay. You’ll need to take a tram to get to their private beach, bars and restaurants and I assume they’re offered to their guests only (as the operator of the tram would mark down your name and your room number for their record). The tram experience is definitely a highlight and it makes everything worths of my stay. It’s a long tram ride and you could amaze the wonderful seashore and the wave from the height— I was all excited! I came across some technicians when I was on my way to the tram. Their leader was so kind that he walked me to the stop as he said the path was a bit messy (maybe Im beautiful too that’s why—- Just kidding :p) He even offered to take me pictures and introduced me their private beach. Everything I experienced in this resort leaves me a very good memory. Their food, their service, their hospitality surely make it one of the most popular in Bali :)

title: Good but disappointed about taxi...
review_text: We came here for drinks and dinner. Beautiful view of the sunset while having cocktails by the bar. Followed by dinner at Sangkar. Can not fault anything about the food, quality & service. The only disappointment was that the hotel insisted on ordering us a taxi (instead of Gojek) and left us with a taxi which had no meter. The hotel was insistent that taxis are safer however I very much doubt this. The taxi had no meter and then required 100 Rupiah fee. The going rate for the distance was about 35 rupiah (ie $3.50 as a pose to $10). Although this is not a big difference in Australian currency, it’s disappointing and I was left feeling very unsafe when one of the basics of Bali travel is to not get into a taxi without a meter. I would have expected that if Bvlgari was ordering me a taxi, they would check this as part of their rigorous security checks. I was thankful to get back to my resort safely, irrespective of price.

title: The finest resort in the world
review_text: Echoing what others have said, my wife and I have stayed at a great many luxury resorts and hotels around the world. This is, hands down, our favorite. If you identify with the Garden of Eden, you're in it. We stayed in a cliffside villa, which was large and luxuriously appointed, with a beautiful pool and deck, offering spectacular views of the sunsets. I partuclarly enjoyed the second shower, which was outdoors and surrounded by a privacy wall within our compound. Sangkar and il Ristorante are both excellent restaurants. When in Sangkar, ask for table 30 - it's in the corner and is the best table from which to watch the sunset. And, although my wife has a gluten allergy, the kitchen accommodated her needs, even to the point of baking her gluten free bread and muffins daily. The general manager, Tessa Gorman, takes a special interest in every guest's stay and their individual needs. If your expectations are high, as mine were, she not only met but exceeded them. An example - my wife loved the grilled octopus she had at the beach restaurant, Spiaggia, and told Tessa, as we were leaving for the airport to go to HK, that she wished she had the recipe. It was a polite side comment rather than a request. When we got to our hotel in HK a printout of the recipe, along with a color photo of the finished dish, was waiting for us in our hotel room. We experienced this level of attentive service, by Tessa and her staff, throughout our stay. Most who come here are well-traveled and can afford stay at any resort or hotel in the world. But, if you're like my wife and I, after staying here you'll also book your return trip to this Garden of Eden.
insider_tip: Select one of the 8 one bedroom cliffside villas for the most spectacular unobstructed view.

The property was also stunningly beautiful with lush vegetation, authentic architecture and excellent position on the Uluwatu cliffs. During our trip, we were celebrating our 5 year wedding anniversary. Although this was a rather small gesture, on the last morning of our trip the staff at breakfast gave us a 3lbs bag of the Bali coffee after they heard us rave about it in the days prior. They also hand delivered 18 long stem roses and a hand written note to a restaurant (not on the Bulgari property!) where we were celebrating our anniversary dinner. These small gestures made the trip special and truly memorable. One thing to note is that you are strongly advised not to swim in the ocean. While the resort has a fenuncular down to the beach and sun loungers (with a separate restaurant, staffed cabana and bathroom), the water is incredibly rough.
insider_tip: All rooms have private pools, the only difference in the categories is the proximity to the cliff's edge.

title: Almost perfect, but the F&B
review_text: Bulgari Bali is a great resort. It boast a unique position atop a cliff facing the Indian Ocean, its villas are built exclusively of local stone, it is sufficiently small (circa 60 villas) to be pure luxury, but large enough to afford a state of the art service. It even built last year a cable-car system (fairly spectacular indeed), taking guests from the villas to the beach, located some 200 metres vertically below. The SPA is top notch (special mention to Ninti, who was my therapist two days in a row, and who is a star), the pool is ideally located and never crowded. So, why do I not give it a full 5 star? Because the food & beverage offering is not at par with the level of this resort, and in a resort where the majority of guests come to relax and spend time away from it all for a few days (and hence have three meals a day in the resort), this is a major fault. Quality of food is average to mediocre at Sangkar (the main restaurant), in any of the three meals of the day: limited choice, poor taste, in general you feel that the cuisine is not where it should be in this magnificent resort. Food prices also tend to be on the high side (compared to similar luxury resorts in Bali), but this, in itself, would not be a problem if the quality was top notch. Wine offering is also quite poor, and unrealistically expensive: to mention a comparison, the same wine costed 50% less at Mandapa Ritz Carlton in Ubud as it did at Bulgari (and while I did not have the two lists one in fron of the other, it seemed this 50% difference was common throughout for comparable wines), and the Mandapa is a top-end 5-star luxury resort just as much as Bulgari, so there is no rationale for this, as all resorts have to go through the same taxation systems on wines. I have to admit that, when raising this fair concerns with the Operations Manager, Antonio Bruno, he was very nice and acknowledged that some improvements are needed and foreseen in the short term. I am hopeful (and actually trust his reassurance) that these issues will be tackled and that on a future stay I can place this review on a 5-star basis, where it really deserves to be on all other counts.
insider_tip: We had Villa 19, one of the (few) two bedroom Cliff Villas: great location, facing South.

title: A relaxing journey
review_text: We spent five days/four nights in the premium ocean view villa. Absolute fantastic resort- dinner under starry night, sliding down from cliff top to the sandy beach, in-villa breakfast with beautiful birds coming every morning, enjoying spa in the cliff top outdoor cave-like suite with waves music from the ocean, the Italian stones and design......will certainly come again.

title: Awesome place to be
review_text: We have been in a lot of 5stars Hotels around the world, but this Hotel is an outstanding place to be. The Villas are huge and beautiful, full of nice details. The view from all restaurants and the bar is fantastic. The location just behind the ocean is a dream. A big kudos to the entire Bulgari Team, them made our stay very special and unforgettable. A highlight for us was the beach located directly at the ocean and the special way to go there. The Italian restaurant and the Bar offer delicious food.We love this Hotel and will definitely coming back !

title: Top of the line
review_text: We tried a couple of different 5 star hotels in Bali. I must say I’ve never been to or stayed at a more relaxing, well put together, beautiful in both location and resort. Everything about it is luxury, the staff fall over themselves to help. I have a Nut allergy and in Asia I’m sure you can see this can be tricky they were all over it and well in front of the game. I really can’t speak highly enough. I travel around the world a lot and I think this is my new favourite hotel and it’s a long way from London but I will certainly be back. Well done!

title: Finissimo ~ superfine
review_text: It’s hard to fault this resort, total relaxation in luxury settings with sincere staff who ensure that each day your in their care is hassle free! We enjoyed a two bedroom pool villa with amazing ocean views. Our room was spacious overlooking a well established garden. The bathroom was well appointed with Italian fittings and a large suken bath ideal for two and an outdoor shower. The villa was complete with an external toilet, kitchen and open plan common lounge with an internal table, bar & large lounges. All the creature comforts for a homely stay. We dined at both the local and Italian restaurants, great selection of food, but I must say the wines were expensive for what was on offer, hence we chose to BYO. The other big plus was the in Resort Bulgari shop. As they say in Italy 🇮🇹 ‘Bella Vita’ ~ the good life. Truly a must to visit and enjoy. M&J, Singapore

title: Amazing
review_text: Just finished spending three blissful nights at the resort. Was trying to decide between here and the Mandapa. Ultimately decided to stay here because of its seaside location. I think we made the right choice. We stayed in a 2 bedroom cliff side villa. Room- 2 bedroom cliff side villa- perfection. Well decorated.. cliff side. I'm already missing the views and the sound of the waves. Everything was well decorated. Bathrooms- great amenities. Electric toilet. My favorite was the outside shower. Service- butler was great. Went above and beyond. Never said no. Laundry service was cheap. Spa- tried massages which were great. Also took in a yoga class. Pool- beautiful Dining - the breakfast was great. This is the only thing that needs improvement. There is only two restaurants and one is Italian where the menu didn't look appetizing so most used the Indonesian restaurant. Would be nice to have a bit more variety. Overall- we stayed on a stay 3 , pay for 2. This included airport transfers. Good value for the money.

title: Very perfect experience
review_text: We stayed for our honeymoon, 3 nights at the Bulgari Resort in Bali. We chose the cliff view villa with private pool, and did absolutely not regret it. It was the most amazing hotel we have ever been to, since the staff and managers were extremely kind. The service was exellent, and the private beach of the hotel very beautiful (even if we weren't able to go walk on the beach because of the tides. The food was exellent too. I highly recommend this hotel and particularly this villa (cliff villa)., and will most likely stay there again when returning to Bali.

title: Outstanding!
review_text: We are return visitors to Bulgari and on this visit, to celebrate a special occasion. We left this week almost tearfully, having been absolutely spoiled for 4 days. We were simply showered with so many beautiful experiences, and far too many to mention in this review but most of them I'm sure engineered by the incomparable Tessa Gorman (General Manager) Tessa's personal touches seem to be everywhere. It's clear to see how leading by example must encourage 100% of the staff on the property to follow suit. In short; our room once again fabulous, all have amazing views, special mention to the butlers and teams who keep them spotless. We experienced all the F&B outlets including the bar, the more casual restaurant and the high quality Italian restaurant. Food 10/10, staff 10/10, ambience 10/10. Special mention to the staff that set up our celebratory dinner for two on the 'roof top' venue below the chapel. Simply amazing and such a romantic setting and experience. Thank you Sam. In closing, this property excels at everything, we're very experienced travellers and have no hesitation in highly recommending Bulgari Bali, we cannot wait return!. Thank you Bulgari.
insider_tip: All rooms have great views, private plunge pools and fabulous bathrooms!

title: Wow but not WOW
review_text: My fiancé and I stayed in this tranquil resort for 2 nights over the weekend as we wanted to pamper ourselves after a hectic wedding last Saturday. You'd feel like you're in paradise, and it's really exclusive. The private beach is amazing, you take a monorail down the cliff and the views are amazing. Another plus was the SPA, we went for the Bvlgari massage which was above and beyond. The grilled seafood at the restaurant above the beach was really fresh, we were amazed with the quality especially since it was far from the resort. We also had the opportunity to speak to their manager Fiona, who was really friendly, however having stayed at many established and luxurious resorts worldwide this property wasn't an immediate wow for me, perhaps because it was slightly dated. Nevertheless will love to be back again!

title: A Perfect Combination of Elegance, Service and Beauty
review_text: My wife and I stayed with our son who is 23 and daughter who is 18. I can say without hesitation the Bulgari Bali is the best hotel/resort we have had the good fortune to stay at. It is designed in a artistic/elegant way and really merges with and enhances the natural beauty of the ocean and the cliffs. It is an opulent resort but the location, ambience and service are unique. It exceeded all our expectations. The Villas are enormous (they actually are fully furnished homes), Technology is integrated in an unobtrusive way all throughout the property. Landscaping is immaculate. The furnishings and decors are all customized. The views of the ocean, the cliffs, the breaking surf are out if this world, Our congratulations to the Bulgari team!

title: Hubby’s Birthday Celebration
review_text: We stayed here on 13th May for a night, specially for my husband’s birthday. When we arrived, they welcomed us with their friendly smile and asked us all the detail information including alergies. I think this is a very great service, but I think they took a little bit long for them to assistance us to get to our room. That is alright, because they have a beautiful view at the lobby (We just can’t wait to see the room). Our room is nice, with a beautiful view from the cliff and small swimming pool. They even set up a small birthday cake for my husband. The room is beautiful, and they have high tech smart toilet, unfortunately the bidet doesn’t work. I didn’t complain because I think I will only stay for a night there, but for the amount of money we pay they should pay more attention about it. In the morning, we had breakfast and it was excellent. They also have private beautiful beach which we regret only playing there just before we check out.

title: Couldn’t be better!
review_text: Visited this amazing resort for our fourth anniversary in conjunction with some other stays at the Capella Ubud and the Four Seasons- Sayon. What a way to start a trip. As always, the Good, the Bad and the Ugly...(Note...there is nothing bad or ugly) THE GOOD From the beginning, the trip didn’t start great. Cathay Pacific lost BOTH of our bags and didn’t seem to give a damn. I knew they got off the plane in Hong Kong but past that, nobody could tell me. We paid for a a car from the airport. Well worth it. We had a plane side escort and skipped a two hour customs line. Unfortunately we had to spend nearly two hours waiting for bags that never came and filling out lost luggage forms. By the time we got to the hotel, everyone was aware of the situation, apologetic and willing to do anything to help! Between Antonio (Food and Beverage Manager) and our butler Ode, we could not have been better cared for. I am a big guy (6’4” and 285). Not surprisingly, Bali is not made for big guys. They did everything possible to assist including sending someone shopping at the hotel’s expense an hour away for clothes. The room is hug and the patio area perfect. The view over the ocean was amazing. We were in Villa 15 and worth the upgraded room costs. What an incredible plunge pool and completely private. The entire staff was beyond compare. Everyone knew our names the entire time and there was no task that was ever too much. The Balinese people are kindness incarnate. The spa was beyond heaven. Obviously the costs are significantly more than outside the hotel but well worth it and reasonable in comparison to most countries. I’ll Ristorante was good. Very limited selection though. Alcohol is expensive but its expected. The Bad and The Ugly There simply is none. Don’t miss this chance to stay!

title: Beautiful resort with Private Pools and Views
review_text: I have stayed at many of the High end resorts in Bali and I really enjoyed the Bulgari. It is best for couples, though I stayed alone a couple of days & my mother joined me a couple of days. I love the concept of being able to layout by your private pool. Breakfast was lovely & the restaurants on the property are nice, but also good to venture out to close attractions in Uluwatu. Nice large bathroom, that makes you feel outside. The rooms are are terraced on the Hill so everyone has a view. Staff is friendly. It's a true 5 star Hotel. I have stayed at Alilia, Belmond, The Legian, Como...For a Romantic Hotel in a Traditional Balinese Design, this is a great option!

title: Nightmare Experience for its SPA Experience
review_text: We tried to make reservation by contacting the spa via email. I started dealt Aristawati. She asked me to complete the form with all the information. Since the form asked for credit card information, I sent it using Adobe Ecrypted PDF. After 2 days, Megs Octapiani responded that they never received the email. I tried to sent it again. But then instead assisting throughout the process, the Spa Manager, Tanti Ekawati (she was cc-ed throughout the process) sent a treating email stating that we "MUST" sent the requested information, otherwise our spa appointment would be cancel. She failed to address why her staff can't open the PDF file that contain with the information that she asked for. I have sent encrypted Adobe PDF when dealing with reservation with the Ritz, Marriott, Hyatt, etc and they never have any problem it. Very disappointed that a big name such BVLAGARI has incompetent staff to handle internet reservation. They can only process credit card information that is sent via unsecured method.

title: 6 star resort experience
review_text: From the moment we arrived at the Bulgari resort we knew that we were in for a 6 star experience. Our 2 bedroom villa (#11) was gorgeous and Ada, our butler, was so kind and thought of things before we did. He arranged for a private guided visit to Uluwatu temple for our family. Even though it was exceedingly crowded due to the festive season, our guide seamlessly guided us through the throngs of people and explained to us what we were seeing. Truly a remarkable experience. The food, gym and spa were all wonderful but the best thing was taking the funicular down to the private beach club, having a delicious lunch and enjoying the beautiful setting. We are strong Four Seasons fans but this Bulgari resort hands down is worth the stay.

title: The Ultimate Experience!
review_text: From our arrival on Christmas Eve the grounds of Bulgari in Uluwatu are reminiscent of an Italian village. Undulating cobble stoned streets that go for metres across a sprawling property with the magnificent view of Uluwatu cliffs. We stayed in a 2 bedroom villa and the attention to detail was everywhere. From bespoke sandals, robes, Christmas confectionery you knew you were staying somewhere special. The villas were very large and spacious the private butler was on hand for anything that you required. Attentive caring staff were everywhere. Breakfast was always a highlight at Sangkar overlooking the icean with a delicious buffet and a la carte option. The beach bar was private with beautiful drinks and I liked the personal sunscreen, Aloe Vera and spray mist very handy when you were at the infinity pool or beach. Christmas dinner at Il Ristorante was sensational and again very attentive and caring staff. Couldn’t fault any aspect of the hotel. As we dined at other hotels in the area Bulgari was by far the best in all levels, staff, service, cleanliness and personalisation. I will be back for sure!

title: Exclusive and Luxury in Bali
review_text: We stayed in Bulgari Bali for our honeymoon and it was absolutely perfect! They have only 59 villas, what makes it super exclusive! Everybody called us by our name, we almost didn't see any guests around the hotel, pool or beach. Lanam was our butler and made total difference to make our stay memorable! He was always ready to help us, made reservations and was always super attentive. Their breakfast at Sangkar is very good, delicious food, amazing presentation and healthy options. Don't miss their private beach with the most beautiful view from the top! You need to take a cable car 160 meters down to reach the beach. I have never seen anything like that! We were in Ocean View Villa, as the name says, has an amazing view to the ocean, with private pool in the room. They have yoga every morning and this is the best way to start the day! It matches 100% with Bulgari Bali atmosphere! We also the couples massage at their spa in the couples room that is incredible! This is a must to if you are in couple and looking for some romantic and relaxing activity. Special thanks to Yuji and Luna for making our stay even more special! We will surely be back and recommend for everybody!

title: Stunning!
review_text: The Bulgari is an absolutely stunning resort and we had a wonderful stay for two nights. The hotel is conveniently located around a 25 minute drive from the airport in a very scenic part of Bali. The hotel is on a huge cliff so the views are outstanding. The property is quite large and they can buggy you from room to room. There are two restaurants on property, a large swimming pool, and a beach club. The hotel is not directly on the beach but you can take a little trolly down the cliff. We stayed in one of the lowest category rooms, but it was probably one of the most beautiful I’ve ever seen. Each room has spacious outdoor area with separate sitting, dining, and large plunge pool. The outdoor area was almost as big as the room! Inside there was a huge king bed surrounded by windows so you could watch the sunrise and set all around you. The bathroom was really unique and had a large jacuzzi tub and outdoor shower. The amenities were definitely bar none. They had a sound system in the bathroom, heated and automatic toilet, and they really just went above and beyond to make the place cozy. There were so many little touches that made our stay wonderful. We came home one night to find a romantic bath drawn, and another they left home made desserts and champagne. They have lotions, sunscreens, and aloe by every beach chair and in reception they have homemade candy and sweets. Even the furniture had a relaxing touch. In the bathroom they had a chair upholstered in towel fabric so you could relax and unwind without feeling uncomfortable. We didn’t have many interactions with the staff but those that we did speak to were lovely. Everyone was very attentive and kind. Overall I would highly recommend Bulgari, but there are a few things that I did want to note. The first being the nickel and diming. They clearly went out of their way to make the establishment homey, but then charge $6 for a Diet Coke. One night I asked them to bring a tea bag that wasn’t caffeinated and they charged me $7 to come to the room. Charging for little things when the room is $1200 is insulting. You can’t feel at home when you’re constantly being asked to sign a bill. Secondly, we didn’t love the Italian restaurant. The meal was fairly tasteless and exorbitantly expensive. I would suggest dining at the other restaurant or eating out. We had breakfast at the main restaurant and it was very good. They have a wide selection of cold and hot breakfast appetizers, and an expansive a la cart menu (included). Third is the privacy. Our room was located next to the main restaurant and had constant foot traffic. Anyone walking by or eating at the restaurants could see directly into our room.

title: Impeccable resort, flawless service, breathtaking beauty
review_text: We have been traveling to Bali since 1994 and have visited many gorgeous resorts there as well as around the world. Let me say that this is probably the finest resort we have been to, blowing others away. From the welcome to the departure, every detail was perfect, every flower in place, every meal flawless, every lounge chair perfectly arranged and cleaned. Daniel on the beach provided incredible service as did our butler, Budy, but the entire staff was exceptionally well-trained. The villas are enormous, with a bathroom the side of most hotel rooms. Two showers, automated toilet, beautiful toiletries and soaps, everything was thought of. We would love a door to the bathroom or a privacy screen, but that's a small detail. WIFI worked perfectly all over the resort -- we have found this is rare in Bali. Great speed and reliability. Each night we found a small gift in our room. Every day was a surprise and delight. We love the Bvlgari and will return. 10 stars!!!!

title: Amazing Service along Beautiful View!
review_text: This is the second time for me to visit this amazing resort! My first time was in early June, a few months ago, and the service was so impeccable that my wife and I decided to return again the end of September! We had requested the same villa as our last visit and everything is very smooth from the moment we landed in Denpasar Airport. We had requested the Concierge to arrange for the transportation. This service had included VIP Meet and Greet at the gate. The airport personnel had taken care of everything for us, from Fast Track through immigration to getting our luggage tags and make sure our luggages were first to arrive at the belt. It was seamless. Hotel transport was very expensive around 3.6M roundtrip comparing to around 1M for a golden bird transport. So, whether it's worth it or not, it's your own decision. After arrival at the resort, we were greeted by our wonderful butler, Lanang. He helped us with everything from bring us little snacks in the afternoon to helping us tour the villas and mansions. The always "Yes" attitude is definitely practiced throughout the Bulgari Resort. Lanang had explained many Indonesian culture and Hindu temple rituals to us. It was both fun and educational. He was so sweet that he bought my wife a picture frame with her name engraved on it for her birthday. We will definitely request for Lanang for our next visit! After we got settled in, we went to the spa. We never informed anyone, even Lanang, to our surprise, Mega, the spa receptionist, remembered us from 3 months ago. She has an amazing memory and this is the personal touch that sets Bulgari apart. She even ordered some snacks for us when we didn't have time for lunch. We were at the spa everyday and the service was impeccable. Warati, Artini and Lindy were very professional and remembered every personal details that we liked from the massage pressure to special tea requests after the massage. Food is excellent as usual. Il Ristorante has the perfect atmosphere, along with wonderful food! The squid ink spaghetti is so good that we went back for dinner again. Sangkar and La Spiaggia won't disappoint. However, I would like to see the menu expand with more items. The variety of Indonesian food is limited and it gets a little hard to choose if you are there for more than 5 days since we usually skip western menus because we also try to eat local cuisine and we are not the type of person that would leave the resort. The only down point of our stay was the "telephone butler". Whenever you call the "butler" from your room, it routes to a phone operator. We had 2 mishap occasions. (1) We requested for a buggy and turn-down service just prior to our dinner. However, the buggy never showed up and we had to walk to the restaurant (which was fine because we didn't stay far from the restaurant). After dinner, we went back to the villa and was extremely disappointed that the room was not made up. We had to call and wait for housekeeping and stay in the room while they cleaned up our room. (2) On our departure day, we requested in villa dining and usually they will transfer you to the in villa dining operator, however, they were busy and the "phone butler" told me to place the order through her and she will get back to me on one of the items that was not on the menu. 20 minutes later, still nothing from the operator. I had to call her back and she told me she told the in villa operator should of returned my call. To me, this was unacceptable. The "phone butler" that claims to return your call, should return your call, even to just tell you the in villa dining operator will call me back. She should even patch the in villa dining through to my villa to make sure the requests were being followed up. This is definitely the low points of our stay. Taking everything into account, this is so minor that it didn't bother us much but should be addressed if Bulgari Resort wants to stay at the top of the resort scene in Bali.
insider_tip: Ocean Cliff Rooms are the closest to the ocean. Views are amazing!!

title: Gorgeous & Super Relaxing
review_text: My wife & I had been to the restaurants/bar here on a few occasions over the past 7 years and finally decided to escape from the kids for a short break and stay, and boy are we glad we did! I have travelled internationally a bit and stayed in some nice hotels, but this one is now at the top of my list of favourite hotels ever. The staff were excellent, very nice and always ready to assist. Nothing seemed too difficult and they were extremely obliging. The grounds and villa were amazing, total luxury in the truest sense. So much attention to detail, and the landscape design is brilliant. I don’t recall ever feeling as relaxed as after a few hours inside the villa, just calm serenity and tranquility. We dined at Il Restorante which was very nice, and the bar and beach access areas were fantastic too. Breakfast overlooking the Indian Ocean was also great, with lovely views and quality food. While we checked out the mansion option while there (and these are amazing), I would not recommend this hotel as a destination for young children. It’s definitely better suited to couples who just want to enjoy each other’s company and forget about the hustle of their regular life. As far as Bali accomodation is concerned its relatively remote, but that’s not an issue unless you intend going out of the hotel regularly. It also isn’t cheap, however you definitely get what you pay for, and in that regard I would recommend staying in. All in all I can’t recommend this hotel enough, we’ll definitely be back!

title: Expensive, but worth every penny.
review_text: We’re frequent travelers in luxury resorts world wide, but I must say Bulgari in Bali was one of the more pricey ones - yet most amazing ones! Everything in this resort is top notch. Just very high-end and thought to perfection. The service and the staff is one of a kind. So nice, I have no words! We were so well taken care of and the whole trip perfectly comfortable. We almost didn’t leave the resort once during our 4 day stay, and we could have stayed longer! Our one bedroom villa was just heaven on this earth, even though we’ve stayed in similar concept before. The Spa is worth the dollars when you are having massage in front of the ocean, even though it was a bit costly. A four-hands 90 min massage cost 800 USD for a couple which felt a bit overkill, but again - Bulgari is expensive. The only minus I would say are the restaurants. The Italian gastronomic dining “Il restorante” is overpriced and the food is nothing special. Their international restaurant is very good (loved the Asian dishes), but sadly the lighting in there creates a depressing ambiance so not very inviting. One meal is OK but you don’t feel like going again. Their bar is fantastic though and breakfast area during the day is perfect for people watching! Regardless of this tiny minus we’re planning to return to Bulgari because it was just spectacular! Everyone who’s an expert in Bali says the same; Bulgari is one of the best hotels in Bali! (We went also to Four seasons in Jimbaran. They had much better food, but the resort wasn’t as nice as Bulgari) I can’t wait to be back!

title: Amazing!!!

I would highly recommend this property if you’re looking in the Uluwatu or Nusa Dua area!

title: Some unique experiences at Bulgari Bali
review_text: My husband and I along with my 2 daughters stayed at the two-bedroom cliff villa for 4 nights. The porch at the kids’ bedroom was breathtaking with unobstructed ocean view; unfortunately the view at the master bedroom was partly blocked by the stone-stacked railing. The villa interior was quite outdated for our liking, the pool was also rather small for the 2-bedroom villa. During our stay in Bulgari, sadly we had to experience a few unpleasant incidents; fortunately after indepth conversation we had with miss Luna, the Director of Operations, things had gone better, and finally we could enjoy our stay at Bulgari with more ease. Also she had made some special arrangements for us, which we really appreciated them. However we found the food served during breakfast was lacking flavor, and the buffet choices were limited. Also we didn’t enjoy our dinner at Sangkar (the main restaurant), we thought the fried rice and prawn soup were bland although the steak my daughter had was ok. On the positive side, we do appreciate the effort from the F & B department for preparing meals for my younger daughter who’s allergic to gluten, dairy and soy. Also the hollistic massage that I had at the spa was amazing. I got to try the ocean view spa room, which was an open-concept room, w/o window nor AC. I could feel the cool ocean breeze and the sound of the wave, it was really nice. Praising Ibu Wayan, my massage therapist who did a very good job, I thought that was one of the best massages I’ve had.

title: Best Hotel in all of Asia!!!!!
review_text: This hotel holds a very special place in my heart.. from the views of beautiful pink sunsets to the kindest servers...General Manager Yuji, Antonio food and beverage picked a great selection of wines, the restaurant menu is great! was such a pleasure to meet you.. Hendrix the best Butler ever, smile on your face with any possibilities of making anything happen that makes you feel at home. His personality made me feel like old time friends.
