title: Bad Experience
review_text: I was in Bulgari Bali for my honeymoon in April 2018. We chose this hotel to end our trip with a flourish, since the infrastructure seemed to be amazing. We chose a vila with front seaview but our problems started on the very first day. Our villa stood right beside a house that was rent for wedding parties during our stay. I went to Bulgari looking for peace and quiet, but during 4 days I had to put up with loud Withney Houston, Shakira and PitBull songs until 11pm followed by fireworks. As the nights were bad, we tried to enjoy the days. The worst part of my stay though was while laying naked by my private pool, when I saw a drone filming me and my husband in our privacy. We immediately called the lobby and they apologized for the situation but they said there as nothing they could do to prevent this type of incident. I dont work in the hotel business so I cant tell what should Bulgari have done for me. They only thing I can do is writing a review to let people know about this embarrassing situation so you can think twice before spending a bulk of money at Bulgari. For the records: restaurants are very good but breakfast is VERY poor with a lot of bread types but small offer os fruits, cheese, yogurt. For eggs, sausages and other you have to right to choose 1 option a day and they will bring it to the table. To be honest: Four Seasos is WAY BETTER in every aspect.

title: One Of The Finest Hotels in The World
review_text: My wife and I had the great pleasure of staying at the Bulgari as part of our honeymoon. The two of us are avid luxury travelers and have stayed in some of the world’s leading hotels. We have never had such heartache saying farewell to a property and staff. The grounds themselves are truly akin to paradise, immaculate in their attention to detail and breathtaking to behold. As if the sheer beauty of this property wasn’t enough, the service from the dedicated staff was above and beyond anything we have ever experienced to date. The concern, doting and ever-present smiles of every single staff member was incredible to behold. You could truly feel their sincerity in their mission to make sure that you will feel more pampered and appreciated than you ever have in your life. As a wonderful example, we would bump into random staff throughout the day and they would somehow invariably greet us by our exact names! For anyone looking for the experience of a lifetime in a property that is the closest thing to paradise that one can imagine, the Bulgari is an unparalleled choice.

title: Way beyond my expectations! Paradise.
review_text: Husband and I went for our honeymoon -- this was the last stop in our 2 week trip to Bali + Sumba island. I wish we'd stayed here the entire time! The hotel is gorgeous, peaceful, and has a spectacular view of the ocean. There are so many reviews describing this wonderful place so I'll focus on a few specific aspects: #1 - The staff is amazing. Everyone we spoke to by the pool, at the restaurants, main lobby, gym, and beach were amazing. The drivers who shuttled us around were fantastic as well! Intan, Gusti, Ricky, Ferry, and Yana were particularly helpful as were Yuji, the General Manager, and the general managers of the restaurants. Merta was a fantastic tour guide when we went to the Uluwatu temple! Whenever the staff could go an extra mile, they would. When we arrived I mentioned to our butler that I had 'bali belly' and when we returned from lunch, there was a pot of hot ginger tea with honey and lemon in our room. #2 - The food is awesome. We dined both at Il Ristorante and at Sangkar. Everything we ate was spectacular and the staff was so kind. Definitely try the breakfast specials at Sangkar - they are out of this world. The food at Il Bar is also great and we enjoyed hanging out by the beach and pool and ordering off the menu. If we go back to Bali we will DEFINITELY look to stay at Bulgari. Can't recommend it enough!

title: Excellent service!
review_text: My friend and I came to visit to do the spa. And the treatmant option that we do is a holistic massage for 60 minutes. We are in clifft and in a place with a view of the high seas. From the beginning of the reservation through the phone service provided is very good. We are advised to come early in order to enjoy other facilities before massage treatment is done. Such as swimming with sea view, then after sauna for 15 minutes before massage. How the reservation staff, hotel staff, and Desa who is of the spa therapis is very smart and friendly. They are give a excellent service! I recommend anyone to come here though not stay at this place. For the price to be paid is very fitting with everything that get in there. Thank you Burgari Resort Bali.

title: Luxury short escape
review_text: I came to Bulgri Resort for their Spa not for stay. I really love the way they handling the customer. They located us on the ocean room so we can hear the sound of the wave while they pamper us with the treatment. I choose scrub package and my partner choose reflexology. Both of the therapists, Wayan and Ninti always explain the products first to us before start the treatment. Even after we finished the treatment, they gave us a ginger and little treats while watching the sunset from the cliffside. The best treatment I ever had and humble therapists so far.

title: The Mansion Experience - An Update
review_text: Introduction This review is a supplement to the review I did last year on the Mansion experience at the Bulgari Resort on June 27 titled "Mansion Experience Fabulous" ( SEE BELOW), parts of which I have included at the end of this updated review. Due to space limitations, I have had to cut much of the 2017 review in order to cover all the topics which I wanted to emphasize in this update. It is reported that there are more five star resorts on Bali than are in Paris; however not all five star resorts are equal with a vast difference between the best and the worst. Residing at the pinnacle of the five star resorts are only four or five resorts with the Bulgari being one of those resorts. Within the Bulgari are two main categories with the villas representing the most commonly occupied accommodation of which there are 59 villas. The most exclusive accommodation within the Bulgari are the Mansions with 3 three bedroom mansions and 2 five bedroom mansions for a total of five mansions. The experience at the villas represent a luxury vacation while the mansions represent a decadent luxury vacation (DLV) which would make even a Silicon Valley tycoon blush with envy. There is difficulty in finding enough superlatives to describe the experience at the mansions. The mansions are separated from the villas with their own entrance so that those at the villas never get to see the mansions, thus, providing an amazing level of exclusivity for the mansion experience. Since I covered the actual mansion experience in my last review, I will concentrate on what makes this such an incredible experience in this review, which is the people at the Bulgari Resort. If your idea of a luxury vacation is to join the heaving, thronging masses on Kuta beach, then you should stop reading at this point since this review is not for you. Reservations Your experience will begin with the reservation team headed by Satria. Most of my interaction this time was with Satria and Cumpu (rhymes with shampoo) who arranged the dates of stay and payment, but also arranged transportation to and from the airport, expedited immigration, and meeting my butler at the airport. They also arranged tour experiences and connected my with Feny at Air Bali to arrange for a helicopter tour. Feny used to be an employee at the Bulgari which helps with making the arrangements. So the reservation team at the Bulgari functions not only in making reservations but also as a concierge service. Since most of your contact will probably be by e-mail, I have included pictures of Cumpu, Satria and Lydia so you can match the face with the name. You can also specify who you want as your butler since you have a full time butler assigned to you during your stay. If you like, the team can assign a butler for you since there are three butlers who are primarily mansion butlers. Satria, Lydia or Cumpu can help you with all of the arrangements prior to your arrival. I checked my bag in at Los Angeles and never touched it again since the expedited immigration agent collected my bag, took it through customs and handed it off to my butler who then unpacked my bag upon arrival at the mansion so that it was a truly seamless, stress free experience. On my departure , the same happened in reverse. The CAS agent met the car, took me through primary airport security, had me wait in the comfortable CAS waiting area, went and waited in line at the airline, obtained my tickets and checked my bag, took me through the secondary airport security, had me wait a moment and went into a back room and returned shortly where we bypassed immigration, and then took me to the airport lounge; thus avoiding the crowds. Satria, Lydia and Cumpu can make this happen for you. Transportation As noted above, Satria/Cumpu can arrange transportation for you and I would very strongly advise that you avail yourself of their services. The Bulgari uses a contract service, which provides the best cars for transportation, but more importantly, the best drivers and in particular a driver who is simply the best driver I have ever encountered on Bali. If you have never been to Bali, then you will not understand the utterly chaotic nature of the roads and driving experience. Most Balinese use mopeds and many of the Balinese do not have driver licenses. Those who have driver licenses have only rudimentary driver training and the driver examination is equally rudimentary, thus the road is filled with a mass of drivers with little or no understanding of how to properly drive resulting in utter chaos on the road. All is not lost since Suwitra (pronounced sweet-ra) will come to your rescue. He is the finest driver I have ever encountered on Bali and has nerves of steel, lightening fast reflexes and eyes out of all sides of his head. He is 56 and is in perfect physical shape since he jogs each day. Driving in Bali requires great physical endurance and stamina to face the rigors of navigating the roads. Suwitra is the Olympic gold medal winner of the Balinese chaotic road driving competition. The contract company the Bulgari uses probably provides the most luxurious cars on Bali with a BMW750 and Toyota Alphard. The new Alphard probably exceeds the BMW in terms of luxury and sits higher so that you can view the panoply of Balinese life as you pass by in utter luxury and more importantly, safety. Mansion The manager for the mansions is Dewa who makes sure the mansions are in proper maintenance. You may never see Dewa since he is one of the many people working behind the scenes at the Bulgari making sure you have a fabulous time. I previously described the mansion, the layout, and the amenities, so I will refer you to my previous review. Butlers The head of the butlers is Bintara and he can assist you in choosing a butler for your stay if you have special needs such as someone who is good with children. Your butler will be the person you interact with the most and is the one who will coordinate the entire Bulgari team from food service, to housekeeping, transportation, and so forth. Your butler will ensure you have the decadent luxury vacation of your dreams and do everything in his power to make whatever you want happen. As such the butler is your go to person while staying at the mansions. My butler during this stay and my prior stay was Lanang, who has been working at the Bulgari for approximately five years but as a butler for just under two years. During my stay, I have never gone to the Sangkar restaurant for breakfast since Lanang finds out what I want, goes down to the restaurant picks up the food and waits on me at the mansion so I have never have to interact with the morning buffet crowd. Lanang has accompanied me on all my expeditions outside the mansion to the restaurants for dinner, to the spa, and the beach. When I have gone outside the Bulgari on my extensive expeditions such as tours, Lanang has accompanied me to make sure there are no problems. He has accompanied me on a shopping trip which was a tremendous assistance,so your butler is there to make sure you have the most enjoyable experience possible that is also free of problems. On his day off Lanang has taken me to the north side of the island to see Lovina, Singaraja, Gitgit waterfalls, and the local museum in Singaraja, since I love visiting museums. In other words, whatever you want, your butler will do all in his power to make it happen. Lanang is not just a butler but a good friend, as well. We e-mail each other several times a week to see how each is doing and he gives me updates on his children. To say Lanang has provided tremendous service would be a gross understatement since the service has been so amazing that it is hard to express in words my gratitude for the what he has provided in making this another wonderful trip or a DLV. Housekeeping These are very secretive and mysterious people . Dirty towels disappear and clean towels magically appear in their place. I have never seen housekeeping since Lanang whispers magic incantations into his two way radio to make them appear when I am a gone and then makes them disappear as if by magic. I had to to have Lanang take a picture of these very shy people before I would believe they existed. They dress in brown, so if you see someone dressed in brown quickly take a picture since you may never see these very shy, mysterious people again. They do such an incredible job keeping the mansions clean and well stocked with all the amenities you need for a comfortable stay. I have included Lanang's picture of these secretive, but incredibly important people. Security As I mentioned the mansions have their own entrance which is guarded so only mansions guests may enter. Outside intruders including villa guests are not allowed into the mansion area. The entire resort is patrolled by a most diligent and friendly staff of security people who protect and keep safe the guests under their care. Although the resort is somewhat isolated, safety is not an issue due to the stringent security force surrounding the resort and the mansion complex. I have included a photo of one of the many security personnel you may encounter. Grounds keeping The grounds of the Bulgari are some of the most spectacular I have ever encountered and it takes a veritable army of grounds keepers to maintain the property to such a level. You will occasionally see the men working to keep the grounds. These are local farmers who supplement their income by keeping the grounds of the Bulgari. They work silently and diligently at keeping the grounds. They are appropriately dressed in green and deserve a great deal of respect for the hard work they perform to keep this beautiful property maintained. RESTAURANTS There are three main restaurants at the resort with the most formal being Il Ristorante, which is modern Italian cuisine. The most informal is down by the beach and is La Spiaggia. Perched on the cliff overlooking the ocean is Sangkar. Also near the Sangkar and Il Ristorante is the main bar. Antonio is the food and beverage director and is frequently seen at Il Ristorante helping greet the guests while Gusto is the assistant director of food and beverages. You will frequently see Gusto at the Sangkar making sure the guests have a great time. Il Ristorante You will be greeted by Dessy or one of her colleagues with a big smile and a warm welcome to one of the finest dining experiences anywhere. After you are seated, a variety of staff will wait on you including Chandra who is the junior sommelier and who will explain the wines to you. Chandra will also help serve you making sure you are well taken care of. Antonio generally stops by to make sure things are going well with you and keeps a close watch over the service. Sangkar The manager of the Sangkar is Hadi who will try to greet you at some point. As I was mentioning above under transportation, the roads in Bali are chaotic. Hadi just returned to work after a moped accident in which he fractured his left forearm. In spite of his painful fracture and the presence of pins and plates, he is his usual beaming, happy self and will make sure you are well taken care of by his outstanding team. Since you are a mansion guest, you will have your choice of seating. I typically have been waited on by Ari who is 25 years old, plays guitar and surfs in his spare time, and has also spent one year in Colorado. Ari will spend the time to explain the dishes on the menu, and if there is something you might like that is not on the menu, he will see if the chef can prepare it. All you need to do is ask and Ari will do all in his power to make it happen. He also will explain Bali culture and religion so he is a wealth of knowledge and incredibly fascinating to talk to. He is originally from Ubud and so can give you tips on what to see and do in Ubud. I typically let him choose the menu items for my dinner since he remembers from last year what I have had and not had. Ari is occasionally assisted by Diah who is, in my opinion, the most beautiful woman on Bali with an absolutely radiant smile. I call her Diah with the dimples. Her smile literally lights up the room. The Bar Although I have never spent time in the bar, Ari typically has the bartender make mock-tales for me out of ingredients that I like. They are a wonderfully creative team and like the rest of the food service staff, are friendly and willing to do what it takes to make your meal special. If you want a drink not on the menu, no problem since they will make whatever you want, all you have to do is ask! Music From 6 PM to 8 PM two musicians are playing on the Gamelan which is like a bamboo xylophone. They play outside the bar but the music wafts throughout the restaurant/bar complex providing a wonderful background to the meal. The music they play are old songs the farmers would play after a hard day in the rice fields and so provide an otherworldly and beautiful backdrop to your meal. They say music helps the digestion and this music provides a wonderful stimulus for the digestive process. La Spiaggia and beach Although the restaurant is informal in appearance, and the menu simple due to the location, the food is superb. Sudhi, Adi or one of their colleagues (depending on scheduled assignments for the day) oversee the restaurant and greet you on your way down to the beach offering food or drink with a smile. Down at the beach Nyoman generally greets you, offering you a lounge chair to relax on. Since you are a mansion guest you get first choice of lounge chairs. Nyoman has been with the Bulgari since it opened and is one of the nicest, kindest persons you will ever meet. He will make sure you have plenty of water and if you want a drink from the bar at La Spiaggia, he will order it for you. Everything is pretty effortless for the guests at the beach. All you have to do is waddle down and back up to the inclinator and admire the spectacular views on the way. Nothing could be nicer than a morning at the beach with the friendly, helpful staff. Spa Wayans (pronounced why-ans) is the goddess of the spa. Under her touch those screaming, aching, jet-lagged muscles will relax so that you will find it difficult to even stand since you will be in such a transposed state. She and the rest of the spa team will make sure that you have a truly memorable experience. If you want a treatment not on the menu, no problem, just ask, since the spa team is there to make this a DLV for you. Administration Luna Bajracharya is in charge of the personnel including the butlers. She is a petite person bursting with energy and originally from Nepal. You may only meet her once during your stay but she keeps in constant contact with your butler making sure you are never neglected, but well taken care of. Yuji Tanaka is the general manager (GM) and looks like a cool, elegant Japanese movie star. The first time I met him, I was baffled why a movie star staying at the Bulgari was speaking to me until I realized he was the GM! Like I said, I am a common person pretending to be sophisticated. Mr. Tanaka has a difficult balancing act in meeting the needs of the guests and maintaining the respect of the employees and from all accounts he does a superb job in accomplishing both. Approximately 450 employees are under his care and it is a heavy responsibilty, which he handles seemingly without effort and incredibly well. The mark of a true professional. Summary I hope this supplementary review to my prior review on the Bulgari mansion experience provides a little more insight into the sort of experience you will have. The mansion is a great deal of money and takes a leap of faith to book such an expensive stay without having some insight into what you will get for your money. I have tried to provide a little more information for those considering such a vacation and reassurance that it will be money well spent. The Bulgari staff will make sure you get the DLV of your dreams. I have enjoyed this experience so much that I have now booked twice and will be staying a third time next year. The choice is fairly simple, do you want to stay in some hot sweat box on Kuta beach listening to the party animals until the early hours of the morning, or do you want to wallow in excessive but casual luxury? The Bulgari can make your vacation dreams come true. June, 2017 MANSION: I stayed in the largest of the mansions which was a five bedroom mansion and has a total interior space of 2400 square meter (approximately 27,000 square feet). Each of the mansion has virtually an identical floor plan and furnishing with the only differences in the presence of an extra two story building containing an additional two bedrooms, the view of the Indian Ocean, and the size of the lot the mansion sits on. The pool size may be smaller in the other mansions as well. Only the largest mansion has a small detached gym, which I used a few times during my stay and contains an elliptical and a treadmill. Each of the mansions has a Porte cochere with a double door entry. The mansions are each two story with a split level staircase with the stairs leading up going to the living room, bar, dining area, and kitchen, while the stairs going down go to the master bedroom and one of the guest bedrooms. A second stairwell leads to the third guest bedroom and breezeway connecting the main mansion to the other two guest rooms, which are in a separate, semi-attached building. The Bulgari mansions are probably the only residences in all Bali providing elevators. The elevators connect the living room to the lobby for the master bedroom and guest bedroom on the lower floor. FUNICULAR: Not much has been written about the funicular, which is surprising since it is one of the most incredible features of this incredible resort. Admittedly not many of the guests seem to use this feature of the resort which is unfortunate, since it is one of the highlights of the Bulgari and very unique. I believe there is only one other funicular and that leads to a public beach with a disco!?! The Bulgari funicular is private and is available only to the hotel guests. The funicular leads to one of the most spectacular landscapes in all of Bali with thundering waves and soaring cliffs. At low tide, the beach is approximately one kilometer long with virtually no other people on the beach since the only other access is a rather treacherous stairway which a few of the locals use. As indicated above, there is a small bar and restaurant just below the end of the funicular and further down the path is a viewing ledge which has loungers for relaxing and a small attendants cabin and toilet. Nyoman oversees the beach area and is vigilant both to the safety and comfort of the guests, quietly and discreetly providing water and towels as needed. All in all, a very relaxing and enjoyable way to spend a few hours.
insider_tip: For the most luxurious experience choose one of the mansions

staying at the mansions my butler during this stay and my prior stay was lanang who has been working at the bulgari for approximately five years but as a butler for just under two years during my stay was mentioning above under transportation the roads in bali are chaotic hadi just returned to work after a moped accident in which he fractured his left forearm in spite of his painful fracture and the presence of pins and plates he is his usual beaming happy self and will make sure you are well taken care of by his outstanding team since you are a mansion guest you will have your choice of explain bali culture and religion so he is a wealth of knowledge and incredibly fascinating to talk to he is originally from ubud and so can give you tips on what to see and do in ubud i typically let him choose the menu items for my dinner since he remembers from last year what i have had and not had ari is occasionally assisted by diah who is in my opinion the most beautiful woman on bali with an absolutely radiant smile i call her diah with the dimples her smile literally lights up the room the bar although i have never spent time in the bar ari typically has the bartender make mock tales for me out of ingredients that i like they are a wonderfully creative team and like the rest of the food service staff are friendly and willing to do what it takes to make your meal special if you want a drink not on the menu no problem since they will make whatever you want all you have to do is ask music from pm to pm two musicians are playing on the gamelan which is like a bamboo xylophone they play outside the bar but the music wafts throughout the restaurant bar complex providing a wonderful background to the meal the music they play are old songs the farmers would play after a hard day in the rice fields and so provide an otherworldly and beautiful backdrop to your meal they say music helps the digestion and this music provides a wonderful stimulus for the digestive process la spiaggia and beach although the restaurant is informal in appearance and the menu simple due to the location the food is superb sudhi adi or one of their colleagues depending on scheduled assignments for the day oversee the restaurant and greet you on your way down to the beach offering food or drink with a smile down at the beach nyoman generally greets you offering you a lounge chair to relax on since you are a mansion guest you get first choice of lounge chairs nyoman has been with the bulgari since it opened and is one of the nicest kindest persons you will ever meet he will make sure you have plenty of water and if you want a drink from the bar at la spiaggia he will order it for you everything is pretty effortless for the guests at the beach all you have to do is waddle down and back up to the inclinator and admire the spectacular views on the way nothing could be nicer than a morning at the beach with the friendly helpful staff spa wayans pronounced why ans is the goddess of the spa under her touch those screaming aching jet lagged muscles will relax so that you will find it difficult to even stand since you will be in such a transposed state she and the rest of the spa team will make sure that you have a truly memorable experience if you want a treatment not on the menu no problem just ask since the spa team is there to make this a dlv for you administration luna bajracharya is in charge of the personnel including the butlers she is a petite person bursting with energy and originally from nepal you may only meet her once during your stay but she keeps in constant contact with your butler making sure you are never neglected but well taken care of yuji tanaka is the general manager gm and looks like a cool elegant japanese movie star the first time i met him i was baffled why a movie star staying at the bulgari was speaking to me until i realized he was the gm like i said i am a common person pretending to be sophisticated mr tanaka has a difficult balancing act in meeting the needs of the guests and maintaining the respect of the employees and from all accounts he does a superb job in accomplishing both approximately employees are under his care and it is a heavy responsibilty which he handles seemingly without effort and incredibly well the mark of a true professional summary i hope this supplementary review to my prior review on the bulgari mansion experience provides a little more insight into the sort of experience you will have the mansion is a great deal of money and takes a leap of faith to book such an expensive stay without having some insight into what you will get for your money i have tried to provide a little more information for those considering such a vacation and reassurance that it will be money well spent the bulgari staff will make sure you get the dlv of your dreams i have enjoyed this experience so much that i have now booked twice and will be staying a third time next year the choice is fairly simple do you want to stay in some hot sweat box on kuta beach listening to the party animals until the early hours of the morning or do you want to wallow in excessive but casual luxury the bulgari can make your vacation dreams come true june mansion i stayed in the largest of the mansions which was a five bedroom mansion and has a total interior space of square meter approximately square feet each of the mansion has virtually an identical floor plan and furnishing with the only differences in the presence of an extra two story building containing an additional two bedrooms the view of the indian ocean and the size of the lot the mansion sits on the pool size may be smaller in the other mansions as well only the largest mansion has a small detached gym which i used a few times during my stay and contains an elliptical and a treadmill each of the mansions has a porte cochere with a double door entry the mansions are each two story with a split level staircase with the stairs leading up going to the living room bar dining area and kitchen while the stairs going down go to the master bedroom and one of the guest bedrooms a second stairwell leads to the third guest bedroom and breezeway connecting the main mansion to the other two guest rooms which are in a separate semi attached building the bulgari mansions are probably the only residences in all bali providing elevators the elevators connect the living room to the lobby for the master bedroom and guest bedroom on the lower floor funicular not much has been written about the funicular which is surprising since it is one of the most incredible features of this incredible resort admittedly not many of the guests seem to use this feature of the resort which is unfortunate since it is one of the highlights of the bulgari and very unique i believe there is only one other funicular and that leads to a public beach with a disco the bulgari funicular is private and is available only to the hotel guests the funicular leads to one of the most spectacular landscapes in all of bali with thundering waves and soaring cliffs at low tide the beach is approximately one kilometer long with virtually no other people on the beach since the only other access is a rather treacherous stairway which a few of the locals use as indicated above there is a small bar and restaurant just below the end of the funicular and further down the path is a viewing ledge which has loungers for relaxing and a small attendants cabin and toilet nyoman oversees the beach area and is vigilant both to the safety and comfort of the guests quietly and discreetly providing water and towels as needed all in all a very relaxing and enjoyable way to spend a few hours

title: Top notch
review_text: We braved a 2 hour long car ride from the airport to get to the resort all thanks to a bus breakdown which blocked the road. But from the moment we reached the resort, the breathtaking view of the Indian Ocean at the arrival reception area just took all the bad mood and grumpiness away. We were deeply appreciative as Luna, the Director of Operations, was also present to welcome us. We were promptly whisked off for check-in. Every staff was professional and courteous, making us feel like exclusive treasured guests in the resort. Our first dinner at Sangkar is nothing short of amazing. The view once again took our breath away. The service staff greeted us by name and took effort to remember our names. This to me really means going the extra mile. The indonesian cuisine at Sangkar was authentic. When night fell, the entire sky was lit by stars. Just brings to mind this particular song lyrics - look at the stars, look how they shine for you. Our short stay at the resort did not require us to leave the resort at all as there were plenty of activities to keep us occupied. Breakfast variety at Sangkar was fantastic, good mix of international options and local delights. After breakfast we proceeded down to the beach. Once again proving the point that this resort’s location is why it costs so much to stay for a night. Lying on the sun-beds and watching the fearsome waves crash onto the shores during high tide was very therapeutic. At the pool, attention to fine details such as providing sunscreen and facial mist, alongside serving iced cold water and even going as far as serving as delectable snacks (popcorn chicken), once again goes to show how the resort pampers its guests. Dinner at La Spiaggia satisfied our craving for seafood. The seafood platter is a must-try. Being so close to the ocean while dining is yet another wonderful experience. Every staff whom served us, greeted us by name. And everyone had interesting tidbits of the hotel or or Bali island to share. Staff always made sure our needs are catered for. Customer service really runs in their blood. We loved the tranquility and top notch service at Bvlgari. We certainly hope to return soon. Only improvement we could think of would be the use of keys for the villas. Given that the resort has yet to go through any major revamps since opening in 2006, perhaps its a good time to upgrade to keycards. At certain points of the day, calling a buggy also requires 15-20mins wait time.

title: Heaven on Earth
review_text: Here in Bali for a weekend and stayed 2 nights to enjoy the clift view from beautiful Bvlgari Resort Bali. The welcoming session was very delightful. We arrived in the evening just right after the sun set. We were escorted to Premier Villa which is 300m2 and we were so lucky to have a fantastic clift view. The layout of the villa has many outdoor space and lounge. But the best is the bedroom... with 360degree window. The bed is really comfy. Slept for more than 10hours for the first night. In the morning prior breakfast I spent time having the complimentary Yoga session around the spa area. The view is fantastic. After delicious breakfast we walked to the clift and the private beach. Which opening hour starts from 9am. Be ready with your walk, must pass over 100 steps downhill after the electric lift. Overall experiences were fantastic, the service is superb with 24/7 buttler. I was so pleased to spend the weekend here. Many instagramable spots for picture collections. And very very private... Highly recommended.

title: good but not sublime
review_text: the resort was great I mean it is Bulgari after all. My wife and I stayed here bc i proposed to her at the Bulgari Milano. So to start we booked Bulgari, Ayana, and Amandari for our most recent trip. Having been to several Bali resorts I would say Bulgari had some of the best views on the island. Bc we booked through amx we were upgraded to a cliff villa. Stunning views. However several things bothered me, the bed was not comfortable, the pillow had no support. The housekeeping from the first night was lackluster, we found strands of hairs on the outdoor sofa, and the housekeeping staff was perhaps short during our stay because the room was always serviced with a delay. the last day our housekeeping was def on par. I mean don’t get me wrong it’s a stunning hotel, but when you are spending a lot you kinda except a lot more. I would say Bulgaris service is more like the Maui andaz than an aman. We also never got that little beach bag everyone seem to have... I feel left out. Morning yoga is great, in villa breakfast and pool staff is awesome. But overall I would say book a regular pool room and enjoy the public pool area.. But if you r looking for an amazing service at the same price go to an aman resort.

title: Heaven on the Island of the Gods
review_text: If you are looking for the ultimate luxury hotel south Bali, don't look further! The Bulgari is located in Pecatu (South Bukit) on a cliff, therefore you are above the Ocean and access the private beach with a funicular. Yes, Bulgari has its own private beach, this is just exceptional for a property in Bali. The hotel has only private pool villas (around 50) extending wonderful privacy, and high professional butlers to help you spending the perfect stay. Villa Check-in was smooth, taken in charge by an agent and then introduce to our butler who propose to show us the resort and then escort us to our villa. The villa is sumptuously decorated with a deep Balinese feeling and also an Italian twist and elegance. Large bedroom facing the pool and the Ocean, very large outdoor living room and a beautiful bathroom with outdoor shower available. You could stay in your villa all day long but we decided to explore the beach and the pool. Beach & Pool Both offers a breathtaking view on the Ocean. We were very well attended by the staff, get really good food and nice crafted cocktails. Beach is accessible only for Bulgari guest with a funicular. At low tide, the beach I super large, offering long stroll. Restaurants We took all breakfast at the main restaurant, large choice of fruits, pastry and anything you need. They also have “a la carte” with eggs, local dishes, Asian options and delicious pancakes or French toast The highlight is Il Restorante. Italian restaurant with Italilan chef named Fabricio. We finally spent our dinner at Il Restaurante as the food is tasty, refine, wonderful service and the chef was happy to accommodate our request.. in fact very few request as the food is just the best one we get in Bali. Spa Beautiful settings. We didn’t book a Spa but we participated to a morning complementary yoga session. The teacher was really patient with us and he was conducting his session based on our level. We met with the General Manager while at the pool, a real gentlemen. All the staff we interacted with gave us a wonderful and genuine care. We can’t find one little glitch in our 3 days spent at Bulgari, the all experience was an absolute bliss. We will be back!

title: Stunning property, but needs more polishing
review_text: Don't get me wrong - I think this is a beautiful resort, but definitely not worth the money we paid for it. First, let's talk about what was amazing - the beach and restaurant tucked right above it, into the cliff, was fab. As was the service and hospitality. Breakfast was yummy, and the villas are a great space to relax. Loved all of this! What wasn't great for a luxury property like this one - the beach is only open from 9 am-5 pm, so even if you want to sit at the restaurant and enjoy being close to the beach, that isn't an option because the inclinator shuts down even before the sun sets. The staff is great but the very limited knowledge in English led to frequent miscommunication - for instance, I wanted a shower cap at the hotel pool, but over 40 minutes later, I didn't have it. Turns out they delivered it to my room :/ Also, when we made the booking, we weren't told that the main pool is under renovation and that we have access only to the pool in the Bulgari villa (no doubt it was beautiful, but definitely worth calling this out when customers make a booking). And finally, just wish there were more restaurants. The cliff restaurant was only open for lunch, the Italian restaurant (Il Restorante) was super expensive, and that only left us with the breakfast restaurant as the final choice. For a resort that's tucked away at the fag end of Bali, a few more activities and eating options would be a good idea!

title: Paradies
review_text: The Bulgari Bali is situated on a cliff overlooking the Indian Ocean, it’s design merges perfectly with the tropical nature and plants where it is embedded. It offers breathtaking views over the wide ocean from most of the public facilities and villas, simply wonderful... The villas (each of them offers more than 300 m2 of privacy) with private pool and panorama view are offering everything you are looking for in a luxury hotel and the surroundings and views of it will make you feel far enough away from everything to really be able to switch off and relax. The restaurants and bars provide great quality food a mix of local and western cuisine. The Italien Restaurant “il ristorante” will excite you with creative creations, a fusion of local and Italian carefully selected ingredients. Also the breakfast leaves no wishes open and the cocktail at the bar are highly recommendable. One of the greatest things this place provides is its amazing employees. They were extremely friendly, helpful and sweet from the day of arrival until we have left. At this point I’d like to thank all of them who made our stay not only special but memorable! title: The most beautiful, cozy, relaxing and friendly beach resort ever.
review_text: From December 2017 to January 2018, we have decided to spend the festive season in Bali, coming from Brasil. For 9 days, the New Year's week, we stayed in Bulgari Resort Bali, located in Uluwatu. Obviously, choosing a hotel like this one you expect simply the best. However, they surprised me delivering a sort of things that made one of the best places I have ever had. Let me start talking about the staff; you can count on your private butler to make choices and arranging activities all day long, in our case, was Mr. Chandra Wibawa, who took care of all our needs and, every time, trying to understand if we were feeling well about facilities, massage, dining, beach or, the comfort of our villa. Mr Yuji Tanaka, Mr Ida Kaniaka (Gusto), Mr Andrew Skinner, Mr Matteo Giurdanella and Mr. Guiri, all of those guys taking care of our meals and effectively surprising us with drinks, starlight dinner but, above all, every day with a unique smile and care, passing by to have a chat, providing gorgeous histories about there own lives and experiences, making the time we spend there, apart from the amazing and outstanding meals we have gotten, a picture that we are going to remind forever. Let me make an special highlight for the gala dinner and the party we had in the New Year's eve; an unique menu of very well and smart choices mixing international cousine to Bali's environment - amazing feeling to take part of it. Secondly, I would like to highlight the beach; You go to a beach resort in order to enjoy the beach and that, in Bulgari, is quite awesome, specifically because the resort is in a cliff of over 150m high and, you need to take an inclinator to get the beach and, just the trip of 3 minutes down along the cliff can worth with an amazing view of the Indian ocean and the coast. Down on the beach you have Nyoman and John taking care of you, with sunblocks, repellent, drinks and big smile to find you. The beach, on low tides, can be as long as 1,5mile to walk and to collect small shells of several colors. Thirdly, I would like to highlight the villa; It is completely astonishing. You can find a villa with small pool, privacy and views that hardly you could find somewhere else in Bali. But the small gifts they provide every single night were touching. In our case, this season, we have received small wooden musical instruments connected with the local culture. Obviously we took them to Brazil, along with the pictures to make a perfect remind of this trip. Finally I would like to thank you guys, from manager to staff, including Ari and Wayan from the restaurant, for the amazing trip you provide to me this time. I Definitely recommend anyone who are interested about getting Bali, to enjoy the beach resort I have experienced this time.

title: Bali Opulence
review_text: As I globe trotter, I have lived in Australia for 24 years but never been to Bali. Last year, I chose Bali as our summer holiday destination with accommodation booked at Bulgari Resort, Bali. During our stay in the resort we enjoyed the Bali hospitality with Italian make over. The very coexistence of rich Balinese heritage culture with sophisticated Italian influence is an unique concept that celebrated people, culture and heritage. We enjoyed our days tasting Italian pastries made by in-house chef from Europe or walking alone the Bulgari maintained beach or watching from our villa magnificent sunset in the Balinese sky. The high standard of customer service with mouth watering Italian and Balinese and several luxury swimming pools had definitely kept us busy everyday.The opulence of Bulgari resort , Bali is admirable and we would love to go back to the resort again and again.

title: Return to Bali. It is safe and beautiful as always
review_text: Let‘s face the reality. Bali tourism is deeply suffering from the news about Mount Agung. Exaggerations of media have resulted in cancellations and non-bookings. However, 99% of the island is safe and 100% of the hotels. In a worst case scenario there will be flight delays (and there are worse places in the world to extend a stay for a few days). Hotels like Bulgari will make everything possible to provide opportunities for well organised departures. We have enjoyed for years the hospitality of Balinese people, now it is up to us to show that we are caring for them. We have just spent 7 nights on Bali, 3 in the wonderful Bulgari. Location, architecture and service are amazing. Our butler Lanang was not only perfect but als warm hearted. We felt very positive about visiting Bali now and hope that those of you who love Bali won‘t get misdirected by media headlines.

title: The name is synonymous to Luxury
review_text: One of my most luxury favourite properties in the world. Set up on a cliff this state of the art hotel has amazing food, amazing rooms and villas. I had the chance to stay in one of their premium villas and from the private pool facing the beach giving you the illusion of being in indian ocean with open sitting and sun beds..the prompt service to the welcome dessert from your private butler’s kitchen, the milk and rose petal bath drawn to bvlgari toiletries and perfume in your bathroom.. bang and olufsen music system to the shack like high ceilings. Everything is a dream. There spa is just amazing and the food served is excellent. The small little novices like having so many shoes of all sizes in the gym for you to wear and work out or a capsule lift taking you from the top of the cliff to water letting you enjoy the view and stopping in the middle of the cliff to have a shack bar giving you the illusion of hanging in the air middle of nowhere overlooking the vastness of the sea rumbling on the cliff while enjoying your drinks or a fresh snapper is simply ecstatic. Take the lift to the bottom to get a super mini beach constituting of just 4 sun beds but a staff ready to take orders and handing you towels and sunscreen just reflects the completeness of the resort. The only two downside if i can call then downsides at all is 1 monkeys which can come to your open area thus you cannot keep anything out in open or let windows be open. (Humidity can also be an issue but thats an issue for any beach place) and 2 its far from main city to go to kutta or semniyak area it will be a relatively long ride. But to relax and take a break from the world and pamper yourself this is the place for sure!

title: Unforgettable property & unparalleled service
review_text: My wife and I spent the last 3 nights of our honeymoon at the Bulgari. To say that we saved the best for last is an understatement. From the moment we arrived until the moment we left, EVERYTHING was taken care of by the amazing staff. Our lead butler, Ada, met us at check-in, knew and remembered our names, answered all of our check-ins and ensured that we didn't have a care in the world. The rest of the staff, everyone from the gardeners to hotel manager, took the time to say hello, asked if we needed anything, and ensured that we had an incredible experience. The staff was always close, but not overbearing. They anticipated our needs, whether it was a ride back to our villa from breakfast or a new glass of water at the beach, and they were incredibly accommodating. The property itself is breathtaking. The views are incredible, the villas are luxurious, the food was delicious, and the drinks were clearly made by an experienced bartender. We can't recommend this property enough - it's worth every penny (which is a lot of pennies, but worth it). We've been fortunate enough to stay at some incredible properties. Properties that have won worldwide acclaim for their service, etc... Those hotels can't hold a candle to the Bulgari. We'll be back (and hope that Ada is still working there when we return)!

The view from the bar was exceptional, full 180 degree views out to the endless ocean. Amazing complimentary snacks were brought out, we ordered a pizza and two bintangs which they poured into chilled glasses in front of us, the waitress was the most friendly, professional, happy person we met in Bali. Try the heated toilet with the air dry it's an experience. Add this to your list of things to do in Bali, you won't regret it.

title: probably the nicest hotel experience you'll ever have
review_text: I think Bulgari Hotel Bali has ruined future hotels stays for me. Everything in my mind will be compared to this one. I've stayed 5 nights here in the Ocean Cliff Villa and hardly left the place as it's designed to cater to every possible need. The place itself is so beautiful, day and night, almost unearthy! I'd totally recommend this particular villa because it has an amazing view on the Indian Ocean and the sunsets here are just something mandatory to experience in a lifetime. Moreover, people that work at Bulgari have this great quality of passing unobserved, respecting your privacy while also making sure everything is running smooth, everything looks impecable and everything is taken care of. However, you'll find that they are wonderful people with deep knowledge and love for their country, people that are genuinely happy to share with you and to put a smile on your face. This goes for absolutely everyone that we have encountered, fro Roni and Chandra, to the Ladies from the spa, people from the Reception, Restaurant, really everyone. You can't help but feeling humbled by this. I'd also recommend the Spa as the cherry on top of a very delicious cake. My experience there was supposed to be an one-hour body wrap but it turned into a 2-hours special package (curtesy of the director of Operations which arranged this for me just because she is awesome) with the Balinese massage as well, which is the best massage I have ever gotten (Ayu has magical hands). This, along with the rose decorations and the flowers and gifts received after, honestly made this one of the best days in my life. Overall, I feel like everything is over the top here. Whatever you might want from a hotel, they make sure you get 110% of everything. Can't wait to come back!

title: Feel at home with a new family !!!!!!!
review_text: Blu and I just had our wedding on 7/7/17 in Bulgari Resort Bali and spent 3 nights there. Words cant really express how elated I was. Bulgari really made it. Everything was so perfect and beautiful. All preparation was done perfectly without even any prior visit to Bali. Very great great thanks to Adrian Pontoan, the event manager who really helped us alot from the very first contact and for every single details. He was very amazing and sincere in helping us, despite our fussy requests, he accomadated all those and we also were amazed by his instant respond for everything at anytime 24/7. We didnt use any WO, but by having him we feel like get eveb more than what we can get from WO. The moment we arrived in the airport we were picked up by Budi, hospitality staff, who was very friendly and kind. It was very nice to talk and know him. And our villa buttlers Chandra and Merta were very kind and helpful to help us with everything that we need anytime. Thank you to Tanaka san, Luna, and Antonio for all your great hospitality and very great team to make our wedding went perfectly. We tried the Italian food and Asian food and all was incredibly delicious. We like the exclusive concept of the resort with a great ocean view from the villa. title: wonderful experience!
review_text: We had an incredible 4 night stay. Bulgari was our first hotel in our first trip to Bali and what an introduction! The facilities, service and food were all wonderful. I was a bit nervous that it would be stuffy but I was very wrong! The staff was friendly and accommodating to us during our stay. There were children there with other guests, which I liked, and it was still very relaxing and peaceful. The villa was beautiful. We stayed in a cliff side villa and the views were stunning. It was also very private. Buggy service was excellent too and made it easy to get around before we knew where we were going! I would come back in a heartbeat and recommend the hotel as well. It is very luxurious and romantic. Great details and attention to even the smallest nuances. An expensive resort but they make it worth it and I believe you get great value for what you pay for at Bulgari. Location is not great, but that all depends on what you are looking for. We did a trip to Uluwatu but otherwise mostly lounged before moving to another location in Bali. The location is serene and peaceful. Very relaxing. it seemed like other sights were at least 15-20 minutes away driving. I don't think that is bad though.

title: Fabulous Beyond My Wildest Dreams!!!!
review_text: Words can't express my life changing experience at Bulgari! The Balinese have stolen a piece of my heart forever! We just recently stayed at the Bulgari and the only negative is that I will never be able to stay anywhere else ever again and be happy....they have spoiled me forever! The property itself is stunning! The villas, food and wine are all over the top. The spa is stunning and my massage was amazing! The service and people are what make this place so extra special. No one can do enough to please you and they go above and beyond in every capacity to make sure you are happy in every way. There is no such thing as no and it all comes from the heart. The general manager Yuji and Luna have done a great job with their team there. Antonio the F and B Director and Gustu, the food and beverage manager both are fantastic additions to the property and we had a lot of fun with them talking food and wine. They both took amazing care of us and made sure we had everything we needed. Even when I asked if it was possible to eat by the pool, Gustu set up a private table in the blink of an eye, no questions asked, and it was the most magical candle lit poolside dinner under the sparkling lit tree and stars ever! I will never forget it! I am forever spoiled by Chef Ngurah's delicious food! I felt like I had my own personal chef and he created whatever I wanted and each dish was better than the last! He is truly gifted and talented and I miss his food dearly! Hadi the manager at Sangkar also spoiled me rotten with his incredible service with a smile and a desire to fulfill my every wish. Dewi the assistant Sangkar manager is an absolute delight! Dia and Ari In Sangkar are the best! Adi the bar tender at the pool bar is first class. Daniel the night manager took perfect care of us after our long dinners late at night. He made sure we were home safe and sound with everything we needed. Edi and Wayan, our butlers, were ahead of us every step anticipating our needs before we even had to ask. Edi even came on his day off to say goodbye and brought us very special bracelets from his temple. His thought and effort forever touched my heart and we are still wearing the bracelets and they are full of powerful happy and magic karma!I Suastika was an excellent driver and made himself available at all times. Everyone at Bulgari feels like family and I miss them all already! Bulgari is truly a one of a kind experience that I guarantee will always hold a special place in your heart as it does mine. The entire staff works as a team and everyone should be commended! Thank you from the bottom of my heart for everything! Looking forward to seeing you all again soon!

title: My Favorite resort in Bali
review_text: It was hard to decide which one in Uluwatu to stay, given the ranking of hotels on TA I nearly booked some other hotel but after some research I decided that the cliff pool villa here is what I would go for. I found other nice hotels with good reputation have only limited sea view from one bed villas. I come here with a lot of doubts, and mainly wonder will this be an instagram hotel only and mainly look nice in photos but not so whilst close up, as a few other guests had indicated? Will this only suit honeymooners who splurge to experience a fancy and trendy brand but not usually very well travelled? Is this hotel expensive only because it's popular with people more into the big names but not the services itself? Once we arrived, all worries were gone and luckily the hotel was refurbishing villas, in the cliff pool villa we stayed everything look shining, not any issued found. If any and being snobby they could have put auto detected light under the bed table so that it would turned on automatically if anyone needs to get out of bed in the middle of the night. The layout of the one bed room cliff pool villa is very comfortable and the bath room is superb pleasant. The pillow at the bath really helps. The rest of the villa has ample storage, size of bed room could have been be slightly bigger though, however with the open unobstructed sea view in front of my bed the size of the bed room is fine. The open air living room is great for travelers during high(dry) season, the plunge pool, to my surprise, it's not as small as it looks on the photos. There was occasion curious money walked on the wall on front of the plunge pool. As long as you don't feed it, it won't get too close or call his entire family to party at your living room. All the staffs we met were attentive and professional. The butlers we had were also very smart and proactive. They remembered our habits and definitely provided us personal services catered to our needs. I would say having travelling to many nice resorts, this resort is out of many resorts which I have stayed offers butler services, but this is one of only three which provides real butler services. The other two actually costed a lot more. Forget about those ' so called ' butler services at some other countries such as St Regis, not comparable. I like almost everything about this hotel,though food can be slightly improve to make it all perfect. Other than that, I am very picky and usually not very generous at rating a hotel, as I said, the whole experience had surpassed my expectation, not only because of the design of the hotel amenities, but also the great services. I believe those negative reviews referred to the none refurbished villas. Once the hotel refurbish all the standard villas I am sure no more complaints about the room quality. For the time being, the cliff villas have all been refurbished and don't hesitate to book it, the view from cliff villas is definitely better than the standard villas too. All in all we had a fabulous stay here.

title: Yoga at Bvlgari Resort Bali
review_text: Did a yoga session with Bvlgari Resort Bali. Session started at 9am. The venue is a lovely small and cosy pavilion with Manduka Yoga mats, bottled water and towel prepared in advanced. The pavilion is among the greenery but it has a view of the ocean. Lovely ocean breeze too. The pavilion can accommodate up to 5 people at a time and anything more would be too squeezy. Yoga instructor arrives promptly on time and is knowledgeable in his instructions. He is well prepared in what he wants to do and makes the whole session enjoyable yet able to bring out the core essence of the session. However, there are spots in the pavilion whereby the sun shines directly on the participants and could make it very hot as though its a sun tan. But overall it was a complete experience to the senses.

title: A very special place - True understated elegance and luxury
review_text: From the moment you enter the gates to Bulgari there's a sense of understated elegance and luxury. The architecture is a tasteful and authentic nod to the Balinese heritage. As you make your way down to the main part or the hotel the cliff top setting is simply breathtaking. The grounds are stunning with the smell of sweet frangipanis in the air. I have never seen such an amazing, unified and gracious team at a hotel. I'm not sure how they have managed to foster such an incredible culture but every single team member goes out of their way to make sure your stay is the best possible. Thank you to our butlers Merta and Hendrix who both did an incredible job looking after us over our 3 night stay. The villas are a luxurious sanctuary overlooking the impossibly blue, never ending ocean. We barely left our room during our stay. Definitely try the spa and book and ocean spa villa for your treatment (no extra charge) the sound of the ocean adds a new dimension to your relaxation. Yoga at the traditional Javanese pavilion in the spa is also a must. If you can, go for a room closer to the cliff for the best views and privacy. Everything in this place has been engineered to perfection from the turn down service, to the luxuriously appointed rooms (bang & olufson entertainment), Bulgari toiletries, to the heavenly sheets and towels. They have carefully crafted not only a destination but a unique, signature experience and atmosphere. There's no 'roboticness' though, the staff show a lot of intuition and initiative to add the element of surprise and customize service to your requirements - in fact they relish in it. All of the restaurants are great, try the 7 course degustation at Il Ristorante for a special treat. The breakfast is next level, delicious pastries, eggs, fruit - everything. Those seeking total relaxation and to recharge in a quiet retreat away from the Balinese chaos will love this place. This is not a place for those seeking big concrete jungle resorts with tacky entertainment, blue drinks and crowded buffets. There isn't beach swimming available year round due to the strong current but the beach setting is stunning and you can venture down the lift to enjoy a beach walk and explore the rock pools and dramatic scenery of the cliffs. Get a cab to Sunday's beach club (15mins away) for access to a great beach. My husband and I have stayed in at least 50 luxury hotels in the last 3 years alone, all over the world and around 20 hotels in Bali in total over the last 8 years. Not many are special enough to tempt us back and deter our adventurous nature to try a new hotel. I can undoubtedly say that we will return to Bulgari Bali and also try their other locations.

Thank you for everything Bulgari Bali!

title: Luxury in a suburb location
review_text: The resort is stunning, and walking around I was in awe of what Bulgari had achieved. Huge stone walls and outdoor corridors, infinity pool, imposing enclosures of volcanic rock with seating for meals, and the list goes on. We stayed in a cliff-top villa which was amazing. Small infinity pool, outdoor lounge area, indoor and outdoor shower, massive bath and all the amenities you would expect from a resort of this ilk, it was beautiful and the butler noted that it had been recently renovated. The food at the Sangkar Restaurant was authentic and amazing and we enjoyed every bite. Breakfast can be delivered to the villas and this is a real treat - the butler brings and arranges everything for you to enjoy overlooking the outstanding scenery that the cliff-top villas provide - great for a sneaky champagne breakfast. The beach is gorgeous and the views just getting there are breathtaking. Note though that conditions at the beach are usually quite rough with very big waves (4m breaking onto a coral shelf) so swimming is not usually an option. Opt for a low-tide walk along the water looking for shells and celebrate with a glass of wine or sangria upon your return. Staff were of the highest standard and always very friendly and welcoming - most greeting us by name. A personal thank you to Antonio Bruno for following up on an issue we had promptly and with professionalism. Thanks also to Mr & Mrs Smith for the recommendation - you spoil us and we love it.

title: Exceptional views and service but villa due for renovation
review_text: Stayed here for one night with family with high expectations. Quite disappointed however as we felt that the resort did not live up to the hype. Yes, the resort definitely exuded luxury and elegance but the condition of the villa we stayed in was a bit run down and has definitely seen better days. Coming from Mandapa Ritz Carlton the night before (which was literally a breath of fresh air) we found the Bulgari ocean villa too heavy and dark for our liking and needs more lighting options. We were lucky however to view the renovated villas the next day thanks to our friendly butler who toured us around the resort. We all agreed it is a big improvement from the old ones with a lighter color scheme and more updated fixtures. The two bedroom villa was the best! Definitely ask for those or better yet, wait until they finish with the renovations. On a positive side, we enjoyed the food from Sangkar, where we ate lunch and dinner. Service is above par and the views are simply breathtaking. In fact, I would go back just for the views! While you're there, don't miss out riding the funicular to the beach, 150 meters below! It is an experience in itself.

title: Three days of bliss
review_text: My partner and I spent three nights here and it was wonderful. While the property is now 11 years old, the villas are being renovated and ours feels brand new. Service is top notch. We had several great meals at the Sangkar restaurant (the prawns are to die for) and one fantastic dinner at Il Ristorante, which is now helmed by Luca Fantin, who also runs a one-Michelin-star establishment in Ginza, Tokyo. The location is a little remote so getting out is a bit of a pain; Seminyak is about 1+ hour away by car and Ubud, about 2.5 hours -- not the best choice if you are intending to paint the town red. But on the flip side, it's perched on a cliff on the southernmost edge of Bali, which means cooler temperatures from the sea breeze. Our favourite spot is the private beach, reachable only by funicular, 150m below the resort. Because so few guests visit -- we saw four come and go during the two hours we were there -- you really feel like the place is all yours.
insider_tip: The standard villas 21-24 should give an unobstructed view of the ocean, but spring for the cliff-side villas for the most amazing vistas.

title: Service at its best!
review_text: This was our third hotel we visited during our trip to Bali (we visited a total of three different luxury resorts within 9 days: 1. the St. Regis is Nusa Dua, the second was Mandapa resort in Ubud and the 3rd one Bvlgari Resort in Uluwatu). High on the cliffs, over the beach, thrones Bvlgari Resort. When we arrived late in the afternoon, there was already a greeting committee at place, as well as a parcel from St. Regis Resort. They sent me over a forgotten purse with all money and cards inside. Nothing had be taken away - thank you to the honest people at St. Regis Resort! We have been greeted by the chef butler, who explained the resort in brief. We remarked construction sites and have been told, that Bvlgari was renovating approx. one third of the villas. You can imagine, that we haven't been happy at all. Having booked an ocean view villa and remarked, that we could see the construction site, we wanted to change, since we have enough construction going on in Zürich and did not want to ruin our holidays... We therefor asked to change the villa and announced, we would also be willing to pay more, if they would give us an upgrade to premium view or cliff villa. After a short while, the chef butler arrived and offered us an upgrade to a wonderful cliff villa complementary. Thank you very much for this generous upgrade! We have even been told, that we should call, whenever we hear any noise from the construction site, they would stop it immediately. But the construction site was well managed, so we did not hear anything during our stay. The villa was newly renovated and spotless clean. It was totally different from the other two villas at St. Regis or Mandapa: cooler and the interior more spartan, a chic Italian style. The pool was smaller than at Mandapa, but the site right at the cliff was awesome, as well as the sight over the sea! Whenever you visit Bvlgari Resort Bali, book a cliff villa. It's worth the higher price, even though the villas are all the same. But it is, like selling or buying a property, all about the place. And the place at the cliff is awesome and worth the higher price! The biggest adventure is going down to the beach. Since Bvlgari resort is on a cliff, high over the beach, you have beach access with a tiny lift (max. 6 persons), which brings you approx 200 m altitude down to the wild beach. Even though I worked part of my life in ski resorts and I'm used to steep slopes and lifts, this lift is awesome and the beach very wild and secluded. A must see! Breakfast buffet was relatively small, but with the à la card menu, you could get, whatever you wanted. The quality of the food was very good and the waiters very well trained. All the restaurants within the resort are great, please read my restaurant reviews. Working in travel industry and travelling quite a lot, I always try to stay at nice hotels. I therefor can tell many things about service quality. I have to admit, the staff at Bvlgari Resort is the best trained staff I've ever met. When I met at my second day the top executives from Bvlgari, I became a fan of their philosophy and personalities. I talked a long time with Luna, the executive assistant manager rooms and Renan, the director of food and beverages. Over our talk, I discovered, that they have hired and trained so many staff members from other hotels I know and estimate all over the world. It's always great to meet such capable, devoted and hard working people like Luna and Renan! To make a long story short: I will come back for sure to indulge myself with the luxury of the resort and the proud, capable and very friendly staff of Bvlgari Resort Bali. Thank you very much for this most memorable stay!

review_text: Stayed at Bvlgari for 3 nights as a last destination hotel (out of 4 hotels in Bali) before traveling back home, and I wished that I booked more nights here, it's just everything, the breathtaking place and sea view, the ultimate service provided by every single one, and the rooms with a huge bathroom area (you have an extra shower outside if you want to shower in the nature), the private infinity swimming pool (will put a picture), the unique experience how you go to the private magical beach. Yes it is pricey but it is worth every penny, I paid the same in another hotel in Bali but it was not worth and I'm never going back there. But I would definitely come back to stay at Bvlgari. The main swimming pool was good too, we enjoyed it and the life guards were amazing. We dined at the bar the first night, we actually wanted to dine at Il Ristorante (The Italian Restaurant) but since our 4 years old daughter was under the accepted age in the restaurant which is fully understandable being a fine dining in a luxury hotel, so the staff offered us to sit at the bar and order from Il Ristorante menu and everybody is happy. So great service at the bar, I'm glad I couldn't dine in Ristorante the first night just that I get to meet the guys I'm going to mention (I got into Il Ristorante the next night, thanks to Chef Nicola) ) Alex at the bar heard me having a hard time sneezing and blocked nose agitating me, all of a sudden there comes a cup of lemon tea ginger magic tea on the house! that itself made my night, talk about small things make a big difference, Thanks Alex I feel better now, just so you know. Andrea at the bar too was very professional and friendly, a guy you will love to interact with, great man. The bar is a must to experience at Bvlgari with its great staff, the ladies were very friendly when we passed by going to breakfast. OK, so one of the best expierience was also Il Ristorante, huge thanks to Chef Nicola, he gave us the green light to come with our daughter the next day instead of dining at the bar, my daughter was sleeping all through out the dinner so no "harm" for other guests dining, thank you for the attitude being humble and passing by the table making sure all good, thanks for the great dishes, I tried the risotto , LOVED it but also Signore Nicola makes the best Tiramisu EVER, it was so good that the next time I went I ordered two just for me, I came back home and went to Armani Cafe at Dubai Mall, the tiramisu was nothing close to the one in Il Ristorante. Molte grazie Nicola, you are the best. Also got to meet Chef Jatu, great chef also humble and sure made my steak exactly how I wanted even though I ordered a complicated way of cooking, but it was spot on. Desa at the spa was great, excellent view from there but it was expensive, a little overpriced. Also met Mr. Graha at the main pool, Room Division Manager, he just approached us to ask about the stay and if all was good, great gesture, true hotelier who cares. Forgive me for the long review, finally I want to thank Ari and Guday too for the excellent service. See you soon

title: If you looking for a relax place, don't pick here!
review_text: I am here with my fiancé for a relax vacation well looking for a place for our wedding. we picked bulgari for the first stop. This hotel is very unforgettable in unfortunate way. 1. Villa experience: When we arrived the our 1st villa, the roof was leaking water, one of the lamp was broke, sofa cover had half feet long stain. so we have to change to another room. The second room's sofa cover is acceptable for a 2 star hotels defiantly meet the standard of luxury. 2. Facility service: Staffs are really nice and friendly, I give credit for that. But there's no facility. Unlike other resorts I been before that provides some water sports like kayak, paddle board, etc. Bulgari has nothing, not even board game. Yoga only on Thursday and Sunday morning. 3. Beach and pool: the beach is locate under the cliff, you need to take cable car to get here. There's no other way instead of that. So due to the rain, they close the cable car, which means the beach is close as well. So I didn't get a chance to check out the beach. And for the pool, high noice pollution! Too many Chinese came here and they don't know how to educate their kid and themselves to care about others. Everyone are yelling and shouting to each other. I guess they are to lazy to walk to each other to speak to each other in normal voice. They people next to my chair is watch some kind drama or video without using headphone... not relaxing but more exhaustion. 4. Dining: there are two restaurants. Both are very small. They Italian restaurant request dress code, but it connect with the buffet area, that leaves no quietness and privacy. With this tiny place and lack of privacy, I don't think it is necessary for a dress code requirement. It just doesn't meet the standard of dress code restaurant. The breakfast buffet is same place where you have lunch or dinner. Small and less variety. It is just like holiday inn's continental breakfast. 5. Room service: this is the reason i give 1 star instead 0 for the rating. Thanks for the room service, i finally can enjoy the quietness in the room and look at the stars with my fiancé. But once a while I can hear the airplane... but still better than people screaming everywhere. 6. Spa: expensive! Don't waste your money for the spa, because you won't be relax with all the noice anyway. Conclusion: this is the hotel you want to avoid if you are looking for a relax vacation!
