title: Worst resort ever -twice!
review_text: Beware I spent $7,000 aud rip off for 3 nights! This hotel is absolutely a let down, disappointment and genuine rip off. This is the second time I have given this hotel a go in a year and the following accounts may make you think twice about your stay. On the first trip we were food poisoned as the minibar drinks were 2 years olds d expired. We were charged for our stay even though we paid in full at time of booking. The food was bad and the service even worse. We were given a Voucher for two nights compensation by The second time round after 7 months of trying to use the vouchers in vain and then them telling us that they were expired. Never the less I proceeded to book again to give it a second go, what a disappointment and waste of money. Good points: View from bar The landscape Bad points: Overcharging, was overcharged by the spa which is ridiculously over priced, my wife got 4 hands massage I got single massage as was the same price? Just over $1000aud for both of us!! This is no mistake and there is nothing special about the service at all in actual fact as a spa owner this was below par. The flies, toads, wasps, centipedes, monkeys that steal your food before you can eat. The servers are extremely slow, for one drink from the bar it takes about 15mins! Coffee from breakfast restaurant 25mins didn't bring couple of times Room service will take hour min to arrive and then 20-30minutes for butler to set up literally eating cold foods and hot deserts Front office wanted to charge $300 aud for vehicle for 3 hours! Same vehicle from same company golden bird cost me $100 aud for 10 hours, I'm all for hotels to make money but this exorbitant. To get anything to happen at least plan non 25-30min delay. Ocean view villas are blocked by the other villas roofs! Don't pay the extra! It's the same view. Also drones flying past video recording you swimming, I was one of 10 rooms complaining about this nothing got fixed!. It was still occurring. As for the room condition: Pool is dirty and filter stopped working, complained everyday for 3 days nothing done pool was murky. Bathroom stinks, had to complain everyday for air freshener they have sewerage problem in the hotel and it smells everywhere. Bathtub cracked multiple places, shower leaks, Lights turn off by them selves. Outdoor shower sprays everywhere including back into the loft, floor of outdoor shower rocks from side to side, major smell and drainage problem also. Doors are old and do not seal properly allowing insects in. Pool water feature is loose and could fall on someone, outdoor pool umbrella does not stay up as is broken, outdoor celling fan doesn't work. Was given a normal pedestal fan. Fridge switches off by itself which ruined my medication. Pillows are stingily filled and blankets are not good quality. The TV that they rave about is too small ugly and has major reception problems so you can't watch anyway. The stereo does not work. Curtains are soiled ripped and stained through out. (Also is manual operation not automatic). Floors creak badly and whole cupboard rattles when walking on floor. Rules of the restaurants is a joke, no collared shirt no entry into the Italian. I was turned away and told they can lend me a shirt!!! When I refused as I was on holiday and only packed smart casual t shirts. They told me to order from the other restaurant and eat Italian, guess anything for a profit! Btw it was the worse Italian I have ever tasted! Even though this place has a nice view, it does not justify the price. I would appraise it as a 3 star if that. Mt Santiago (GM) will just do his usual auto reply to this feedback talk and make promises however the resort are cheaters liars and lazy many more resorts that will treat you better are cheaper and have better features! This is no Oberoi, no Intercontinental, no Grand Hyatt, no Jw Marriott, no Ritz Carlton, No Kempinski, no Mandarin orchid, no Shangri-La, no Four seasons and all of the fine establishments above are cheaper too! It's more of a Best Western.....just a lot slower and more run down. Btw best Western is much better value if your comparing! Save your money stay else where. You have been Warned!!!

title: Our welcome to Bali
review_text: We were not exactly sure what to expect from the Bvlgari Resort, which is often a good thing. Perched high on a spectacular cliff, the resort enjoys truly amazing views and is built into natural surroundings with a wonderful mix of Balinese and Italian architecture. We stayed in a cliff villa, which meant that there was nothing between us and the waves crashing below, well except for some greenery and a few of the local monkeys which we enjoyed having pop past every now and again. The villa is large with its own private rim flow pool (I think all villas have this). We entered the resort pretty tired and stressed, and left feeling rested, relaxed and ready for the rest of our holiday. Highly recommend the Spa and we both enjoyed our yoga session. The private beach is a must visit and offers prime photographic opportunities. The staff are genuinely gentle, kind and caring and really do try very hard!! Thank you to all of the team for making our stay a wonderful memory. They have buggies that get you very efficiently from A to B, however we preferred to walk. The resort is currently undergoing much needed maintenance, our room did have several leaks and signs of wear and tear. Lastly the main swimming pool is pure bliss, however the resort does allow tour groups onto the resort for tea. title: Beautiful Property but Price Unjustifiable to me
review_text: I've stayed at a couple of designer hotels previously (the Missoni in Kuwait, which has now been taken over by another hotel brand and the Armani in Dubai) and so I don't think justifying high prices because of the designer name is sufficient because those other hotels, and non designer hotels generally that I stay at were much better value for money. In my opinion, this hotel is missing too many basic special touches that regular luxury hotels at the $400 to $500 designer mark have down pat. The Bvlgari in Bali came across as quite a stingy offering for the price paid. If you check my other reviews you'll see that I don't have an issue paying for high end hotels. But when I'm putting down money and genuinely don't feel I'm getting something fair in return, well frankly that sux. The best part for me was the spa, also ridiculously expensive but the service was consistently good. Room: Villa style with large outdoor area and pool although the private pool was really quite tiny, smaller for sure than the one I had at a regular hotel in Seminyak. The amenities were five snake fruit and four pieces of local dessert. No chocolate on the pillow at turndown. Actually nothing at turndown. One set of Bvlgari amenities in the bathroom that were tiny. I just found it really difficult to comprehend how this was an offering worthy of the $1000 per night room rate. Stay at other hotels which aren't even Bvlgari which stock Bvlgari amenities in the bathroom (Grosvenor House Dubai or Four Seasons Ammam for example) and you'll get larger sized bottles and two sets. All for 25-30% of the room rate the Bvlgari charges. I kept asking myself why the high price! Breakfast: Not the hugest range in terms of variety but certainly enough to keep you happy and staff are very attentive. One of the better aspects of my stay. Bar: Drinks are expensive for Bali but overall a pleasant spot to take in a drink and staff were really great. Il Ristorante: A surprisingly pleasant experience. The aesthetic of the restaurant is really proper fine dining. Food was very good. Although another reviewer has commented that the restaurant was expensive, I considered it to be pretty much the only part of the stay that was decent value. Compliments to the chef and gratitude to the staff who were so delightful. Pool area: Pleasant with beautiful infinity spa pools overlooking the water. My relaxation by the pool was somewhat marred by a large group from China taking a lengthy photoshoot (I left after half an hour and they were still going, shouting down instructions for poses) without having much consideration for the peace of the other guests. Spa: I had a lovely lady look after me for four and a half hours of spa and salon treatments. I felt truly relaxed afterwards. There was a fantastic open treatment room with views out to the ocean. Unfortunately construction noises started up after that so I asked the therapist if she could turn the music up and she offered to transfer me to a quieter room without a view but I said it was ok. She stuck her head out the door, said something to a colleague and eventually there was no noise. I found out later upon check out from the spa that the spa had arranged for works to stop. I really felt that was going above and beyond. The spa team was fantastic. Buggy drivers: These guys got you from point A to B and their English was fantastic. They were friendly and a positive of my stay. It's clear that for me, value is important. But I do acknowledge that there will be a lot of people around for whom money is genuinely no object. If you are in that lucky bracket, then this is probably still a fine choice. Also for celebrities in this bracket who value privacy this would be a good choice. If you're an average joe saving for a special trip or someone comfortably placed financially who looks for something exceptional if you're putting down large sums of money, I would recommend the Maldives or other luxury hotels in other locations instead.

title: Doesn't Get Any Better!
review_text: Spontaneous stop off for some quality refreshments at Bulgari Uluwatu on our way back to our Seminyak villa. Uluwatu was a deconstructed reprieve from the crazy build up of shops, cafes and tourists - and the TRAFFIC! Here one finds themselves travelling back to the 70's when Bali still had its innocence. The rugged cliffs atop the twinkling ocean transports one to topography that draws you in and invites you to stay longer. Unfortunately, we were time poor and opted for a refreshing indulgence from The Bar ( Bulgari). Every element was beautiful and the service was impeccable. The views by the pool with its infinity edge dropping down to the sea, the crisp cool mixed drinks and the tapas - my colleagues had to literally drag me away and back into the hire car. I plan to come back and book in for a night. It will be worth the investment!

title: Great Place, Amazing Staff!
review_text: Bulgari was the last stop of my 2-week long Bali trip. What a great way to end my very first Bali vacation! The moment I stepped into the reception hall, I felt at ease. The hilltop view was amazing. The checkin process was very easy. My villa wasn’t ready, and they arranged afternoon tea at the seaside bar for me to enjoy while I waited. They saved the best seat for me and 3-course afternoon tea was something to be remembered. Little did I know the best part of the stay was still to come. Once I checked into my villa, aside from the personal welcome letter, the customer service representative also wrote a note to thank me for my patience with a two jars of cookies and roasted nuts as a “thank you” gift. The view from the villa was breathtaking, infinity pool, huge bathroom. The next morning, I was having my breakfast at Sangkar, Bulgari’s Asian fusion restaurant. One of the waitstaff came over and chatted with me, and made sure that I got everything I needed. And the casual chat became a friendly conversation. Her name was Alice Yoon. She’s very attentive and knowledgeable. We talked quite a bit during the next few days during breakfasts. When I asked her if she knew some local attractions, she wrote a nice note with a long list of places she liked and told me their particular appeals one by one. Wow, even the concierge at other hotels were not this nice. This kind of personal touch was unheard of. At my last breakfast, we bid farewell like friends. During my dinners, I befriended with Hadi the restaurant manager, he’s full of local knowledge. he told me a bit about the hotels on the island, and how they evolved over the years. And then Juni, a waitress from local village. She taught me some Balinese and told me about the festival they were going to have. She even invited me to join them during the festival. I also enjoyed the SPA tremendously. Again, the service was impeccable. I liked the welcome tamarind drink, and they promptly delivered a whole jar to the fridge in my villa. Overall, the staff at Bulgar went way above being courteous and friendly. The treated me as their family and took care of me. That’s unique and different from any other hotels and resorts I’ve stayed at.

title: Amazing service
review_text: Bvlgari is simply an amazing facility, its layout and design is par excellence. Notwithstanding, the staff recognise that Service is their point of difference and they offer perhaps the best level of service I've ever experienced. GM Yuji Tanaka made an effort to meet us, understand our needs, and make us feel welcome- this friendliness carries through with his team. The food is amazing, not as fancy as some of the other resorts, but focused on quality produced cooked and presented in an authentic manner. Bvlgari is probably best for couples, notwithstanding, our 7 year old daughter was treated like a celebrity and formed a genuine bond with several staff - thanks Cynthia and Asda ;) Some staff do try to 'kill you with kindness' but I'd rather have that problem than the opposite approach. The boutique might also benefit from stocking more items that suit the resort lifestyle e.g. Only one pair of Men's sunglasses for sale . The Spa is prohibitively expensive, about twice the cost of other 5/6 Star resorts in Bali and other major resort destinations. The beach is a bit disconnected to the Hotel but the amazing views from each Villa quickly assuage any desires to leave your room. This is our second trip and we're already planning a return.
insider_tip: 2 bedroom Villas have the bedrooms separated which is great for older kids but not young children you want within ear-shot.

review_text: I havn't stay in Bulgaria before but I had my lunch few days ago at Sangkar Restaurant. I still remember the amazing experiences in Bulgaria. When I arrived in lobby, Henny and Rachel showed their special attention on me although I am an outside guest without any accompany. They invited me to the sofa area and asked me about my plan at Bulgaria. I said I would like to go to the SPA and also get some information about travel in Bali. They called their colleagues Elson and Jae to pick me up at lobby with buggy. Elson showed her special care of me and introduced lots of activities in Bali for me. She also recommended me to enjoy the SPA with 3 hours treatment which was amazing. She also took lots of pictures for me as memory. She helped me a lot with all the information I didn't know and she made me feel that I have to bring my parents here in Bulgaria to enjoy the service. She was very nice, professional and she treated me as a family. Although I was travel in Bali alone, she brought a warm sense to me when I was in Bulgaria. I have to say that Elson is a special employee in Bulgaria and she really payed her special attention on each guest. She tried her best to help me enjoy my stay since I have met her in lobby when I had my lunch with my girlfriend yesterday. She is a great employee in Bulgaria and I need to say that the Bulgaria is so lucky to have such an employee which really deserve future development in Bulgaria. Elson is the model of hospitality industry in Bali and she definitely will be a future leader in the hospitality field. The gentleman who was driving the buggy called Jae, he was also very nice, he always smile and have lots of patient to answer my questions. He was well trained and has the potential to be a future hotelier. There was a gentleman called Rifky, he drove me to the lobby to meet my friend although the rain was very heavy. He was worried about me and borrowed me an umbrella. I was really touched by those small things which were unexpected. The Bulgaria Resort in Bali is the best place to visit and I will definitely come back again with my parents and friends. I hope I could see those lovely employee next time and wish them all the best of their career development. Thank you.

title: Could be better
review_text: This is a stunning luxury hotel located on a high clifftop which enjoys amazing sunsets. The highlights include, incredible reception building, great cliff top bar, very good restaurants and private pools within villa compound. The lowlights include a general lack of maintenance (below expectations), the constant (as in every night we stayed) weddings which use up most of the bar area just when you want to have a sunset drink. For us we had a broken air-conditioning unit which maintenance failed to fix, so we endured two terrible nights sleep with what sound like a jet engine over our heads. Not good enough for this sort of money. Finally got shifted to a cliff top villa which was nice. Overall this hotel is disappointing. Disappointing because with a bit more effort and maintenance program in place it could be stunning. Also, it is some distance from activity outside the hotel, but that was fine with us.
insider_tip: Get a villa close to pool. Clifftop villa's not really worth the extra money as the view is the same.

title: It's a 50/50 kind of Hotel
review_text: 50/50 referring to the 24 hrs we stayed at the Bulgari Resort and Spa in Uluwatu. We got there by noon, 3 hrs prior to our check-in time. We were greeted nicely in the very welcoming and luxurious reception. The views of the resort are breathtaking. After that we were invited to have lunch either at the restaurant or at the bar. We chose the bar for it's closeness to the pool and the view of the ocean. Food was delicious! a 10/10. Service was also impeccable. Sadly it stops here. Around 3 pm we were called to be taken to our room. The private villa was very deceiving. It looked outdated. the materials were worn out, the curtains stained with a few holes. The bathroom was nice but too dark. everything lacked of light and freshness. We requested our room to be fumigated as my wife is easily scared with bugs. Although they assured us it was fumigated we still found a few unpleasant visitors in one afternoon. The tv was too small, and far away from the bed. The windows were not opening and closing properly. We still decided to give it a chance and spend the night. But that was it. The room was not comfortable and we did not enjoy our night there. We were supposed to spend 3 nights at the Bulgari but had to shorten our stay to one night as we were not happy with the room at all. 50% + for the resort / 50% - for the room
insider_tip: After we left the Hotel I heard the Mansions are much better than the deceiving villas! Worth taking a look as the resort is beautiful.

title: Beautifully Decadent
review_text: We initially came to Bulgari for coffee in the late morning and we were absolutely spoilt with the view and the friendly bar staff who attended to us most diligently. The atmosphere and grounds at Bulgari are absolutely 5 star they have truly made the resort feel like a touch of paradise. With their own unique special blend arabica coffee we enjoyed some of the best coffee outside of Australian and definitely by far the best in Indonesia. We enjoyed our coffee here so much we decided to come back for dinner. As an Australia we are spoilt to have Barramundi readily available at most restaurants but here at Bulgari we probably had the best Barramundi we have ever had. The fish was absolutely divine as it just fell apart and melted in your mouth. Prices here are not for the faint hearted but you certainly do get a lot for what you pay. The cocktails are delicious and the complimentary bar snacks are unique and tasty. Bulgari is most assuredly worth visiting for either a coffee, a sunset cocktail or a special evening meal.

title: A place to get away from it all
review_text: Me and my husband just returned from our honeymoon trip to Bulgari. It was the third Bali resort we visited, and we were so glad we saved it for last. If you are looking for privacy, quiet, and relaxation- there is no better option. While it is a bit further from the activities and a bit remote, you are only a short car ride away from anything you desire. Our butlers Yadi (and Wayan in the evening) were so helpful and attentive. When Yadi found out I had been bitten by a few mosquitos and that the bug spray wasn't quite working for me he made sure to provide us with mosquito patches and hydrocortisone. I really appreciated the concern. The villa is very well equipped and planned out. While it was not the largest villa we stayed in while in Bali, it was perfect and well planned out. I have never seen views like we saw off the cliffs of Bulgari. It really was transporting you to another world. We dined at il Restorante and had a wonderful meal. Being one of just a handful of tables really gave it a special feeling. Service at the pool was fantastic- they had a great pool setup. They even moved a lounge chair to an odd location just so that I could get the "best sun". Breakfast was the best. The staff kept bringing out special dishes just for me and my husband that were off the menu. They somehow got word of our dining tastes and things just kept showing up on the table every morning. That attention to detail is why Bulgari is so amazing. Little gifts in the villa every night was also very personalized. We felt extremely taken care of. The beach was beautiful- however the tide was too high during our visit so we couldn't stay too long. There was just something special about Bulgari- the resort was completely booked while we were there but we still felt like it was just us. Thanks again to the wonderful staff who only aim to please. We can't wait to return.
insider_tip: The higher the better views. I believe all the villas are the same besides the location.

title: Too bad
review_text: We decided to stop for dinner on our way back to the airport and made reservations. He was extremely knowledgeable about the hotel and friendly....you could tell he was a happy, contented man who enjoyed people and his job. In fact I would say he was the highlight of our visit. The Sangkar was ok, ok food, nice view, decent service, no towels in the restroom. I would not return for numerous reasons. Probably the biggest reason for me is not being able to walk around. One takes an elevator to the beach, and the topography is awful all around the resort. I find it hard to believe people have not gotten hurt as safety precautions are certainly lacking. It is built on cliff which obviously makes for lovely views.....but how about railing when going down stone deep steps? Seems like commonsense.

title: World class service at world class resort. Absolute best experience in Bali!
review_text: This was my second stay and it was for my own wedding. We had the resort nearly booked up for my wedding so all staff were very attentive to me and my guests. Every detail from the moment we checked in to check out was handled perfectly with great efficiency and service. The resort is great for couples trips, families, and groups of friends. There are many beach clubs and such to explore 20 min drive from the hotel so it's a superb choice for a luxury stay in Bali. Here's a brief break down.... Rooms/ resort: the beautiful resort has a jaw dropping view of the cliffs in Uluwatu and the layout and architecture are just so exquisitely composed, it was very easy to navigate around the resort and by the end of my stay (3 nights) I felt like it was my home! I was put in a cliff view villa which had an infinity pool and an amazingly gorgeous view of the ocean and cliffs. Couldn't have asked for more. Facilities: The infinity pool, poolside cabanas, the poolside bar are amazing and sufficient to make you want to relax there all day, my guests had a wonderful time swimming and drinking champagne by the pool all afternoon. There's also a great spa which I got to enjoy once on my wedding day, it was very relaxing and well done, a must during your stay. There's also a gym which I didn't get to use but I'm sure it is just fine too. F&B: all restaurant options are superb, especially Sangkar, it's sort of refined Indonesian restaurant, absolutely tasty and we went there a few times during our stay. Italian restaurant was also great with several menu options. It was a beautiful dining area suitable for couples or even big groups. Breakfast options had a great variety as well and all of which were so delicious! Service: throughout my wedding weekend, the staff went above and beyond to accommodate our needs. Our butler was always there for us whenever we needed his help. Everyone was so attentive, professional and patient to us. I am sure that even if it was just a regular stay, they would carry out the same standards. Very impeccable hotel team, truly impressed.

title: Thank you Bulgari. You are loved, and you are love.
review_text: It has been three months since I left Bali. Usually, I review a hotel immediately after my visit. In the case of Bulgari, it has taken me a long time. This is for good reason. I was so overwhelmed by the staff at the resort, I was speechless — unable to put into words how wonderful these people are. The hotel itself is on par with the most beautiful places in the world. Overlooking the Indian Ocean, on a cliff, the grounds are mix of Bali’s natural beauty with Italian style. I spent many hours simply walking throughout the resort in awe of its beauty. And the service at Bulgari is understated at five stars. The care and elegance with which every single staff provides service is better than anywhere I have ever stayed (and I have been all over the world at exceptional accommodations). But, to focus on the breathtaking landscape or the best-in-the-world service would be to miss the true heart of this hotel. The people who work at Bulgari are the kindest, most genuine you will ever encounter. Ongki was my butler, but I would prefer to call him my brother. This man is an exceptional person who made my feel the true essence of peace whenever I saw him. Aida and Rony were the same. Also my butlers, I was taken in much the same way — their energy and inner peace is a true offering that is not listed on the hotel’s website. Hana and Jay in the restaurant are no different. Their zest for life is infectious, providing not just meals but emitting such wonderful vibes. Jay is a superstar, extremely personable, and I would be fortunate to count him as a friend. These people are special. This hotel has every amenity you would ever need. I could not do justice to the beauty of my villa if I tried. But again, there is something much more about Bulgari than the typical resort. Ari was my driver for my week, and anyone looking for the hardest working, most pleasant and professional man should ask for him by name. And… I have saved the best for last. Astrid works in the restaurant and epitomizes what Bulgari is all about. She is smart, beautiful, classy, kind, and the most peaceful human being I have ever met. To meet Astrid is to experience Bulgari in its fullest. This woman, like her colleagues, make a stay at Bulgari more than a holiday. To meet Astrid, and stay at Bulgari, is to be alive, to feel earthly powers bigger than yourself, to have your life touched by energy that will never leave you. This is not a resort, it is a place where the world’s best people work and therein a gift to all of us. Thank you Bulgari. You are loved, and you are love.

title: Bvlgari, gem of Bali
review_text: Bali is always one of the most romanic places in the Globe. That's why my wife and I visit it quite frequently. In any of their cities, Nusa Dua, Kuta, Ubud, Seminyak...,you can find a unique and delightful experinence. But, of course, picking the right villa is very crucial. For Bvlgari Villas, honestly, because, the reviews on the net show contrasting opinions, so we have stayed in few other villas in Uluwatu before checking it out. Its splendous view of the Indian Ocean and the thundering splashing waves is unsurpassable. The villa is built for lovers. We like their floorplan, unlike some other villa that is huge but with poor overall arrangement, Bvlgari Villa is quite compact and it plays much emphasis in putting up a cozy ambience, the lighting, the packed living area, the romantic bathing area and, of course, the marvelous outdoor chilling zone. Yes, there are negative points: the wooden structure leaves the villa susceptible to humidity change, so sometimes the doors cannot be easily open or closed ( the butlers always help us in this, spotaneously! ); the 'pool' is petite, ( relax, it is still great to soak in it. As for swimmers, the main pool will definitely satisfy your need); there is not indoor fridge; as mentioned, and like most Balinese Resorts, their clientele are mainly honeymooners and couples so you can say that there are lots death traps if you are really sarcastic: its proximity to the dangerous cliff, the dim lighting, those sharp corners...well, remember, the villas are meant to serve adults; infestation of natural lives, e.g. monkeys and mosquitos can be problematic but understandable because we are the actual intruders in the area so we have to respect them and Bvlgari has tried their best in keep the harmony. In addition, Bvlgari does provide us with plenty of warnings and advices; finally, the resort is quite worn out, but, apart from some flattened cushions, things are still in good conditions, the aging simply adds more charisma. The two restaurants are truly great: the surrounding, the food, the structure... And like other guests have mentioned, the staff there are indisputably their Aces, they treat you as family members, memorize your names, your preference, your stories, cater your every needs. Hadi, Astrid and Shinta from the Sangkar and a pretty thin girl in Il Ristorante who served us on 3/12/2015 are one of those showing superb performance impressing us most. All other staff are fantastic too, thankyou so much. Some may argue Bvlgari's breakfast choices are quite limited, ( others resorts may even provide fine dinning style dishes, numerous choices, a lavish serving of food as breakfast, yeah, we like those too ), but, remember it is just the breakfast, the Bvlgari provides us with fresh fruits and pastry, besides, the staff will always surpise you by bringing you tailor-made dishes. Sometimes, we have to beg them not to spoil us so much. For the Spa, the experience is average. The ocean cliff spa room's water pressure is too weak, water just drips from the shower, and without air conditioning in the make-up area, under such hot weather, it will drive you sweat through your kimono. Luckily, the spa treatment itself is awesome. Oh, we must not forget the Bvlgari Beach, it is the a private beach and is one of a kind, though you can't swim there, just go there, you won't regret it. There are lots of weddings going around in the period, luckily , apart from just longer waiting time for the buggy, the services of Bvlgari are not much affected,. At last, we must express our gratitude to all those butlers who are always smiling and helpful. They all have tried their best to deliver us a home feeling environment and in this aspect, they excel all other villas. As for those poor comments, they mostly arose from unrealistic expectations, language barrier, cultural difficulties. We have to be fair to the big name too. Bvlgari, definitely, Balinese Style with Italian Spirit, will surely give you an unforgettable balinese experience. Do go there, it worth its title and my wife and I will undoubtedly visit it again.

title: Average experience overall, but poor experience if you consider the price
review_text: Bulgari is supposed to be a 5 star exclusive luxury hotel. I have stayed in many 5 star hotels in Bali (been there 4 times in the last 2 years), and Bulgari is a letdown. Let's break it down. Location: On the far south of Bali island. Yes, good if you like to just see cliffs and some waves. But it is far from everything else in Bali. Beach: The beach is very poor and inaccessible. You need to take a cable car down to the beach. After which you need to climb down ALOT of stairs (imagine the climb back up later). Not suitable for elderly people or those with children. Very small beach and not much area for tanning. Service: Service is good. Pretty standard for five star hotel in Bali. Food: The Italian was pretty good. However, the breakfast buffet has very limited selection. The place doesn't feel exclusive at all because of the small buffet area. Room: One word: DARK. Everything in the room was dark and I had to strain my eyes to see everything. I am 30 years old and my eyesight is 20/20, yet in the hotel it felt like I was visually impaired. Turned on ALL the lights and it was still the same situation. Booked a vila and the view was to the sea. If you like seeing the big ocean (and nothing else) then this is for you. Overall an average trip and definitely not worth the money. Will not be back.

title: Hustled!
review_text: I visited Bulgari Hotel for a third time yesterday evening. My first visit was in Oct 2014 when my friends and I enjoyed drinks at sunset followed by a lovely dinner at Sangkar. Also in Oct 2014, I visited BH for lunch, also at Sangkar, followed by a heavenly spa treatment in the afternoon. I returned for a third visit yesterday afternoon, full of anticipation but what a let down! Although the complimentary taxi service, provided by Blue Bird, was 30m late (due to traffic), the girls at the spa kept my 90 mins appointment for my husband and myself. I had planned to end my evening with another lovely dinner at Sangkar. Our waiter, Eric, was very polite and attentive. However, our nightmare began with the hostess when we tried to settle the bill using the same VISA card we had used at the spa earlier in the day. We were told that it was not accepted because we did not have a PIN number. We explained to the hostess that Singapore banks do not issue PIN numbers for their cards, but all of this fell on deaf ears. We even told her the hotel spa had no issue with our same VISA card earlier in the day. We were going back and forth on the bill for at least 20m. As it was already late in the evening and we had a long ride back to The Legian, not to mention the complimentary taxi was already due to arrive, we decided to settle the entire bill in U.S. dollars. The hostess gave us the hotel's applicable exchange rate but upon receiving the cash (2 pieces of hundred dollar bills) we were told that a more 'unfavorable' exchange rate had to be applied as one of the U.S. bills was dated 2009. This is the most ridiculous excuse I have heard; the U.S. notes are either legal tender or not. If so, there can only be one applicable exchange rate on the same day! Needless to say, it is unfortunate that we had to end our lovely afternoon on such an unpleasant note with this hustler! I am truly surprised that a hotel of this standard even employs such staff; it will only tarnish the hotel's good name and all the good service/work of all its other staff members. It is a real shame!

title: Stunning but not perfect
review_text: Overall this place is absolutely stunning, an amazing photo everywhere you look! However, our one bedroom ocean view private villa was a little tired (in parts) and needs a bit of maintenance to bring it back to the high level it should be for $1,500 a night! The resort staff are amazing and call you by your 1st name (even when you arrive) so you are made to feel very special in every part of the resort. Il Restaurante was ok (not amazing). Went to better restaurants in Seminary (eg. Matise) We are from Melbourne Australia so we have very high standards because our Melbourne Restaurants are world class. We actually preferred the food in the less formal Indonesian Restaurant and in hind sight would have given Il Restaurante a miss. The resort guests are predominantly Asian (70%). A handful of European and we were the only Aussies (in quite a while I think). Beach club amazing, very relaxing! Lunch at the beach club restaurant was a little disappointing but the view was too die for so lower your expectations with the food and you'll enjoy it. We had lunch at the Indonesian Resuarant at Alila Resort (just down the road) which was probably the best food we had in Bali. The Resort is also breathtakingly stunning, but I would prefer to stay at the Bulgari because it's a lot more private. Overall, this is a stunning place and you will not regret coming here for a total relaxation. Sherren from Melbourne (Australia)

title: Tourist Trap for Chinese
review_text: We went for tea and lunch at Bulgari. The view and surroundings are nice, but the service is atrocious and food was average and overpriced. We expected that the food would be expensive and bland, given the location and brand. What was unbelievable was that, in the end, they actually even tried to overcharge us for an additional afternoon tea and an additional bottle of champagne, thinking that we wouldn't check the bill when paying. Only after we pointed this out to them and debated multiple times did they agree that they had 'made a mistake' and would not charge for those extras. Of course, no apology or explanation. It's outrageous that a five-star hotel treats their guests this way. Given the number of Chinese guests there while we were (most of our group was also Chinese), it seems they've become too accustomed to ripping off people that don't have a look before paying. Seems the hotel also specializes in Chinese weddings, wonder what prices they charge them!

title: A beautiful hotel with some disclaimers
review_text: We stayed at the Bulgari for 10 days last month. There were so many positive about the hotel but largely it was the service that made the Bulgari memorable. The staff are genuinely friendly and very professional, they couldn't do more for us, including the chef offering special off the menu dishes. Our villa was lovely and spacious. As some other reviews have mentioned it may be time for some updating but overall very good. We had several treatments at the spa and they were excellent. The disclaimers for this hotel is the number of Asian weddings that take place and include large entourages of photographers and assistants that disrupt the serene environment and turn it into a photo shoot. The brand name attracts mainly Chinese and Korean clients who only stay 1 or 2 days so there was a constant turnover and a less relaxed approach to vacationing with a focus on taking photos in every possible place of the hotel and way too many selfie sticks for our liking. Fortunately we could retreat to the sanctuary of our villa. The location of the hotel is quite isolated and unless you want to take a 50 minute trip to Seminyak your restaurant choices are limited to the those at the hotel or at Alila, having said that, the food was excellent at the Bulgari restaurants. Overall a lovely hotel with incredible views and exceptional staff.

title: Top hotel in Bali, among best in the world
review_text: Anyone complaining about this hotel should be rewarded with a vigorous beating at the hands of the vast majority of the world which will (most unfortunately) never afford to stay here. Whenever anyone on the island heard which hotel my wife and I were at, they'd say "Ooooh, Bulgari, top hotel in Bali" and stare as if we were billionaire unicorns. The only reason it doesn't completely dominate the rankings here is because it'll never have the same volume of visitors. I've had impeccable service many times before and I know what 5 stars is like. Bulgari in Bali...wow...even advanced torture techniques couldn't achieve such consistent quality and dedication from so many personnel. I could provide examples but it wasn't any single thing, it was demonstrated in everything. I mentioned there was a mosquito somewhere in our villa, we came back to find an elegant mosquito net strung up. I mentioned my wife liked the tarts they make for breakfast, we came back to find the recipe and a signed card from the executive chef (Andrew Skinner) and his team. With that said, no hotel can be everything to everyone. There are only 59 private, secluded villas (can't see in past their high walls so get freaky), each with an assigned butler (ours was Parma and he was great). Staying at the Bulgari is like being marooned on a luxurious private island; only thing missing is Richard Branson cracking jokes. If you want to completely unwind with a significant other, come here. If you want to party or do lots of activities, then you're better off staying somewhere less remote. There's a helipad nearby if you want to use that to get around but driving tours around Bali can be tedious. if you do need ground transportation, I recommend hiring balicab.com and having them standby; it's only $50 USD for 10 hours.

Unfortunately were the drinks prices and food very expensive, so it has withheld wine, cocktails or similar to drink. However, there were evenings always happy hour at the 50% on all drinks. I booked with breakfast and in the evening I had to dine at Il Ristorante. You will be electrically transported within the resort. Very friendly staff. Extremely tidy complex for certain service. Clean pool area with beach beds. Very spacious different Villas. Beautiful spa area. It is based on the slopes of Uluwatu location with spectacular views of sunset. It offers a variety of different restaurants and bars such as Il Ristorante and Sangkar Restaurant and La Spiaggia. To summarize it was a good hotel for my stay. If the focus is to see the whole island, is in my opinion is to plan a good trip, since Bali has a lot to offer and the means, despite the small islands are actually very long.

title: Breakfast with a View!
review_text: I absolutely loved this resort. I stayed in the lowest room category which was an Ocean view pool villa which was stunninh. The dark wood tones with stunning bright blue ocean backdrop was beautiful. The resort is influenced by both Bali and Italy, with Balinese styled villas linked by meandering cobblestone footpaths reminiscent of an Italian Village. The grounds of the property are lush, colourful and simply breathtaking. The pool area is home to a number of sun lounges and cabanas to relax in, with the pool staff on-hand to provide magazines, suncream, cooling mist, aloe vera gel and of course bar service! The resort is located high on the cliff edge of Uluwatu and it has own funicular to take guests down to the Beach Club. The Beach club has sun lounges, restaurant, a bar and staff to wait on guests. The ocean is not recommended for swimming due to the rough rocks and tides. The service at the hotel was fantastic, they could not do enough for me! I enjoyed the sunset and dinner at the bar which was delicious and breakfast at Sangkar Restaurant which was a well thought out and stylish buffet. The view from my balcony table at the restaurant for breakfast was a peaceful and truly magical way to start the day.

title: Great stay but expect more
review_text: We stayed here in 2014 for two nights. Upon arrival, we were greeted by smile and friendly staff. The welcome drinks were served as we enjoyed the great view that the lobby had to offer. Then, came in the buggy to escort us to our villa. During the escort, you could immediately feel the serenity, quietness and exclusivity of the resort. Our villa was an ocean view villa. The villa had a private swimming pool with a separate outdoor dining area and a bale chair. The bedroom itself really spacious with Balinese style decor with separate walk in closet and bathroom. The bathroom area also had separate toilet, "gold" bathtub, shower and outdoor shower. The king size bed and the fluffy pillows were also very comfortable. The amenities were as you would expect in a luxury resort. The breakfast we had here was by far one of the best we had so far. They served variety of foods from western to full set Japanese meals. The food at restaurant here was also fantastic. You will enjoy having your meal with a "to die for" cliff edge view of the restaurants. ANd now the negatives, the size of the private pool here was relatively small compare to other resort villa that we had stayed in. The furniture of the room need major refurbishment. We found some of the furniture had worn out. The outdoor shower was dirty. The view from the outdoor dining room was nothing special (our villa was quite far from the cliff). Unfortunately, we felt that we expected more from the price that we paid for the villa. FYI, As the resort is quite secluded, you would find looking for an outside restaurant or cafe quite difficult. Also, be careful with the transport here. The prices they charged here seemed to be higher than what you would expect around the seminyak area because you are a little far from restaurant and cafe.

title: Wonderful service and ocean view
review_text: This was my fourth time to Bali. I have stayed at Ayana, Four seasons, Royal pita maha, St. regis and Banyan tree . This time I chose Alila and Bvlgari. Before I came to Bvlgari, I have read many blogs and reviews about the negatives of old facilities, mosquitoes, one course breakfast, and small private pool, so my expectations were low, but to my very surprise, it turned out I loved Bvlgari more than Alila. I wonder how manager did that, every staff I met no matter from restaurant, bar, reception, spa, or beach was so helpful and courteous, one Chinese lady from reception was so polite that I felt I was like a royalty. I have stayed many luxury hotels, I know it is hard to achieve that level, but Bvlgari did it . Special thanks to Budi from Sankar, impressed by his passion for customer service. I also love the serious fine turn down service dessert and local gift. There was a bonus that you came home with a complementary beach bag and a lot of Bvlgari toiletries. During my stay, there was no leaking on the roof, the door was not that hard to open, one course breakfast had become buffet style, I could sleep through the night without harassment from mosquitoes. Maybe I was lucky (I overheard guests talk about the leaking from their villa), but with wonderful service, awesome beach inclinator, and magnificent ocean views, I would be fool not to stay Bvlgari. But there were drawbacks of course, the small private pool couldn't compare with the pool from Alila and Banyan tree, and a lot of trash on the beach, they had to pile it up and burned it on the beach causing air pollution.
insider_tip: worths the money to upgrade to a premier villa that is higher and gets better views

title: Second Home
review_text: Bulgary is like a second home for us. For the past 3 years we have always try to come back and stay at Bulgary for our birthday or anniversary. The level of staff attentiveness is one of important factor of why we always love Bulgary. The bar was my husband's favorite chilling out place and something that he looks forward together with the honeycrumb ice caramel, which no other property seems to have. We love the wagyu burger as well as it is worth the money and the quality is one of the best that we have tried so far. Sangkar is also one of our favorite breakfast restaurant. For the past years, sangkar had an ala carte breakfast style which has been changed this year to buffet due to influx of guests from Asia. We were worry in the beginning that quality of food might have dropped as buffet is a mass production but again sangkar with its exceptional level of personal service managed to win us again. They are willingly and happily prepared special menu if requested by diners. They proactivelty looked after all diners to ensure everyone is satisfied with their food. The villa as always was delightful and has a lovely smell of bulgary's fragance and has an amazing view. They always generous with amenities and very unique with the design. This is one of the property that does not cut their cost by serving less to their guests. Keep it up! 1 thing for an improvement and it has cought our attention for the past 2 years: - it is about time for bulgary to change the traditional ceilig of 'alang - alang' (hay) to a proper solid ceiling to ensure guest's safety and comfort. The use of hay looks beautiful on the overall look, however not suitable for long period and especially on rainy season with the location of the estate which is in mountain area with strong wind. For the past 2 years during our stay, there were water leaking from the ceiling all over our bedroom, the strong wind and the rain have made holes through the ceiling. The wooden floor became sliperry and we almost felt down in the middle of the night. Not only the leaking from the ceiling, but it also caused drama with mosquitos, insects and bugs that dropped to the bed and floor from the ceiling. We were explained that any changes with the materials have to be approved by Bulgary's head office in overseas, maybe it is a good idea to invite the decision maker to experience themselves the level of discomfort to stay in the villa during rainiy season.
insider_tip: Be careful of water leaking and insects from the traditional hay's ceiling.

title: Beautiful wedding reception, but disappointing service during our stay
review_text: We recently stayed 3 nights at Bvlgari. I read some reviews in trip advisor prior to the trip and was particularly concerned with the comments re mosquitoes issues. Below is a quick summary of my experience of this resort: 1. Check in -no one greeted us, we didn't get any special welcome even we arrive with the bride n groom. A staff told of husband to collect our friends passports for her as if he's a tour rep which annoyed him. She told me this is a 'tropical island' when I told her I am very allergic to mosquitoes. (I've been Bali for five times let alone other tropical islands. I know some resorts do have more mosquitoes vs others). 2. Dinner at Sakra (their casual restaurant) - restaurant was very busy and food came late for our group, they missed my side order. Quality of food was average. I don't think a indo fusion menu work well. A waiter dropped a plate and spilled everything across our table. 3. Room Deco - very clean, no issues with mosquitoes as long as we closed the door all the time. Pool we never used as its too small, monkeys run around on resort so we can't in the villa in case we get disturbed or leave food around. But very dark lighting and furnished. Furnitures Need an upgrade as they started to look worn. 4. Buggy transfer - took 20 mins to arrange a buggy to get to the restaurant on our first night. The operator made excuses as any staff made mistakes usually do. 5. Private beach - staff refused to take us down while there's a little rain, it's nice to explore on the beach, but you can't swim in it as there were lots of rocks. 6. Pool - pool attendant didn't let me take a picture until after I told him which villa we stayed. Pool was nice to swim. All of the above happened within half a day that we arrived. We discussed this with their operation manager day 2, she told us there was no excuse and very sorry for what happened. She arranged an hour massage at the spa for us which was a kind gesture. But, if we could choose, we'd very much prefer paying for the massage without having to deal with issues during a short and expensive trip. The staff was more aware of our needs after our chat with the manager, we didn't have more issues with the buggy and checkout was efficient. For the wedding, 'the water wedding' (as referred by the hotels website) by the bar area was absolutely beautiful. The ceremony was elegantly done. It's ideal for under 100 guests. We were blessed by the good weather. A firework before the reception dinner was amazing. Dinner venue was indoor which was good as rain started half way. Their band did a great job. Everyone had a great time dancing and drinking. There was also a fire show half way during dinner. The bride and groom had their own crew of photographers, which they spent the next morning taking more photos. We spent our last night dining at their Italian restaurant, food was very good and service was good. Last thing I want to add is about the location, it's very far, which makes it a very quiet and exclusive resort to stay (perfect for wedding), but it's not for people who want to eat out and explore around town unless you want to deal with the travelling.

title: A league of it's own in Bali
review_text: My sister and I stayed in a 2 bedroom villa. Well in Bali we stayed in a few different hotels, including the notorious W hotel. Bulgari is simply in a league of it's own. Situated on a cliff, with the most stunning views of the Indonesian ocean, with a private elevator down the hill side to the beach, were monkeys frequent your doorstep, it's amazing. The day spa, the infinity pool, the low lighting and ambience, the breakfast selection the dinner options, the gourmet lobster menu, the Bulgari shop, the security gates and guards with dogs, the foyer overlooking the resort, the cocktails. All simply amazing. What's more stunning than all of that is the staff. Second to none in service, just absolutely attentive, helpful, genuine and quick! With three flowers floating in the pool they insisted on cleaning them out, which was a little bit funny. Well it is expensive to stay, the price tag is well matched with te facilities rooms and service. You buying privacy, space, access to facilities and service with no wait, and your counter parts staying from around the world are all there for the same thing. No reason to leave the resort, drinks delivered to your villa, nothing is impossible there. To people complaining about Mosquitos, guess what, you are in Bali! No money gets rid of those! If I ever get married it will be at this hotel! No doubt in my mind! I'm a huge critic of hotels anywhere I stay but my sister and I loved this hotel! It leaves Palazzo Versace in the dust ! A huge and special thank you to One of the assisting managers during our stay, Jeffery Xu, you ad your team you managed were just absolutely amazing and it's because of you that we will be returning in the future! Thanks, Jevan

title: An amazing resort!
review_text: We stayed at one of their fabulous villas for three nights. The villa had an open air sitting area, a dip pool and a view of the ocean. It had a well-stocked fridge and coffee & tea making facility. The bottled drinking water was replenished daily. The bedroom itself had strong air conditioning, which was great, a comfortable bed and wonderful pillows. Glassed in on three sides, you could view the sitting area and the dip pool from the bedroom. There was generous closet space and drawers, and one could easily place two big suitcases on the luggage area. It was a welcome treat from the less than sturdy foldable racks in most hotel rooms and certainly well-appreciated to not have one suitcase on the floor! A safe was available in the room. The huge bathroom was heavenly. Separate enclosed toilet area, a large shower area both indoors and outdoors, a wonderful tub in the center of the room, two sinks and a makeup table. Needless to say, all the amenities were available. If there is one drawback, I would say the lighting was a bit dark. The entire villa was very clean and really a pleasure to stay in. All the staff was very polite and always ready to help, the food was delicious, the spa very relaxing, the entire resort was a pleasure. There is a funicular that can take you down the cliff closer to the ocean where there is a place to sit, have a drink, and enjoy the view. It is located quite a bit away from shops and restaurants and you will need a vehicle and some time to reach the busier areas, but if you are looking for a truly relaxing retreat, you will love this fantastic resort.

title: Bulgari Bali
review_text: We just stayed for a week in the Bulgari, Bali. The service was excellent as was our accommodation. The landscaping is unique and beautiful and there is no reason to venture out in the mad house of Denpasar or Seminyak. Everything was perfect or so we thought. The only very annoying thing is that the hotel are building a chapel on the grounds next to the reception and villas. Construction commences from 9 am in the morning to 9 pm at night. The noise was relentless and can be heard throughout the grounds of the hotel. Despite requesting on several occasions for the noise to stop before 5 pm and the staff attempting to fulfil our request this didn't happen. So if you want peace and quiet and you dont want to feel like you're staying next to a construction site DONT STAY THERE FOR AT LEAST THE NEXT 6 MONTHS until works are completed and then stay in one of the best hotels in the world!
insider_tip: All the rooms are great however stay as close to the main reception as possible as the landscaping is quite steep elsewhere and you will have to rely on a buggy to get around. Much nicer to walk!

title: Rustic, Luxurious
review_text: We were in Bali and simply had to try out this luxury hotel for a night. We arrived at night, and we felt overwhelmed by the sheer luxury of this place. No stone was left unturned in creating a super-exclusive atmosphere. Even the security checks were longer and they even had guard dogs, which made us feel that the resort was closely guarded and thus very safe. Check-in was quick and uneventful. We absolutely loved that everything was Bulgari-branded. It was quite an experience to live within such luxury. We were brought to our villa, which was gigantic. We could not see the cliff view as it was at night but we could hear the sounds of the waves, which was very peaceful and relaxing. The villa's style is very wooden, which would be nice to those who like it but we don't really favor the rustic ambience. Floorboards creaked softly, creating an extremely rustic feel. The bed was very comfortable, and we spent most of the night at the bar having drinks and snacks, listening to the waves. In the day, we took the glass elevator down to the beach and had a few pictures, but couldn't really walk on the beach due to high tide. It's a very secluded beach and there is another beach bar on the cliff owned by Bulgari. Certainly a great place to chill and read a book if you are staying there for a longer time. Overall, a GREAT and EXTRAVAGANT hotel. 4/5 only because we don't enjoy rustic ambiences, but otherwise it would have been perfect.

title: Nightmare experience in Bulgari hotel Bali
review_text: We came to Bali with alot of expectations tht many of our friends said how beautiful Bali is. I asked a travel agency to find the best villa in Bali as I had the best experience with Shangrila villas in Borracay, Philippines. As soon as I and my girlfriend arrived Bulgari hotel Bali, I realized the hotel facility is not as good as Shangrilla Borracay, while Bulgari hotel cost alot more. Since this is my first time to Bali, I thought Hotels in Bali are more expensive than the ones in the Philippines. Below are our experiences and nightmares: Day 1 We arrived Bulgari around 5pm It was Villa no.18 with ocean & cliff side view. I could see the ocean view was beautiful, we got inside the villa, we found out tht the bathroom size is twice as big as the bedroom and has a small TV, and the Butler was proudly mentioned that the TV brand was Bang & Olufsen, but the TV was too tiny to watch the soccer game while the quality of picture is not suitable for watching anything. The swimming pool was so small, its only approximately 1/3 of the size of private pool in the Shangrilla villa Borracay we stayed. The butler even reminded us to be careful not to fall off the cliff. I then ordered food from room service as we both really hungry, it was 5 different dishes. The food arrived and we barely touched it as each dish tasted terrible. By the time we went to the bed, we realized that the bed was not comfortable and trying to find a comfortable spot on the bed made the bed feel small. The pillows were too soft as if it was filled by just a wisp of polyester fibers. The atmosphere of the villa was eerie as the road to the villa was very narrow and dark at night. The villa in general was dark except the bathroom lights framing the mirrors. These lights are too glaringly bright, which hurts our eyes. My girlfriend feels that the villa so spooky that she was afraid to go to the bathroom alone. The sounds in the master bedroom is muffled while the rest of the villa echoes. Maybe because of the fact that the master bedroom had a cheap curtain completely around the room. Then there was the problem of mosquitos. As we layed down to sleep thats when you hear the mosquitos buzzing around your face. We had a very hard time sleeping as mosquitos buzzing on our heads a whole night. After getting up the next morning we had a dozen or more bites particularly on the back of the legs, hand, face and backside. Day 2 Sunday. Took a cable car 150 metres down to the beach and was told by the staff that the last cable car was at 5 pm. If we missed it, we would have to climb up the rocks to the hotel. He was not joking and seemed serious. So we went up back to the hotel early and went to the swimming pool. There was only two men in the pool so we didn't stay long. The small pool at the villa did not seem sanitary as there was moss on the edges and there was a ugly shiny frog in the pool. Villas tend to be places where quiet and seclusion is desired so that one can spend some quality time with your significant other but the villa at the Bulgari does not evoke romantic feelings. In fact, once in the room one can't help but feel a bit eerie, with the combination of the morbid curtains, drastic lighting differences in the villa, and the strange way that sounds sounded in the villa really puts a strain on emoting romantic feelings. In fact, both my girlfriend and I sensed that the room was actually making us feel like we did not want to talk that much to each other, let alone the romance. .. Day 3 Met my friends outside as they stayed at another resort and spa. Their resort and spa was much brighter and breezy. Plus, they have so many water sports activities like para sailing, jet sky, flying fish, seawalker etc.. I then decided to stay at their resort and gave up two days left at the Bulgari. It was a great decision and no brainer for me. There were no mosquitoes there, the food was delicious, they have great stunning beach and awesome spa where we had great massages...and most importantly, I was able to finally get a real nice nights sleep. The best suite room I stayed there only cost me less than 1/2 of the cost I paid in Bulgari hotel, but I and my girlfriend feel much happier and stress free staying there. I left Bulgari hotel 2 days earlier than my booking, they dont refund the remaining unused room charges I paid. We even were told that we will have to pay an extra for a late checkout. We were told that a 4pm checkout would cost 50 percent of the room rate and a 1030 pm checkout would cost a full days rate. So I actually paid a full 5 days (@US $1300/night) and stayed only 3 days. I would rather given up US$2600 than continued staying in Bulgari, its just how bad the experience was. Don't get me wrong, Bali is one of the nicest places I have ever visited, I and my girlfriend will definitely go back there again. Spending $1, 300 per night at the Bulgari in Bali was a bad choice. I should have checked the trip advisor first and took my friends first recommendation. After all, I have learned alot, one of them is that expensive stuff does not mean is the best, particularly in this case.

title: Heaven exists, it is in Pecatu, Bali
review_text: Service, food, environment, good taste, beautifully kept gardens, amazing ocean view, fantastic rooms (suite), great beach and pool, sophisticated spa, and Monkeys, these are the ingredients you will get at the Bulgari resort. Don't miss their bar and have a fantastic cocktails before dinner at one of their two restaurants (classic italian and modern indonesian). Early mornings by the beach are just the connection between human and sacred. If you are lucky enough, you can see dolfins playing and enjoying sun rising. The only way to reach the beach is via an elevator (funiculario). Don't imagine yourself swimming in the ocean, at this point currents are strong and dangerous. The swimming pool is perfect and our villa had its own. If you are afraid off monkeys, you might get scared stiff, but omnipresent but discrete staff will rush to help you. Enjoy it!
insider_tip: all rooms must be high standard, you have golf carts driving you through the huge property, nonetheless, I would pick my room closer to the bar/restaurant. area. You can feel very lonely ...

title: Beautiful views.. but bugs are a real issue.
review_text: I'll start with the good.... The Property is beautiful... breathtaking, as are the villas. It truly is memorable and unique and that's really why this hotel is so highly rated and so over priced. Food was pretty as were the restaurants, no complaints on the service. Now the bad... The hotel is in a remote part of Bali on the southern tip making it out of the way, but obviously that's a logistical reality of having such a breathtaking view from the entire hotel. However, being in such a remote place with the set up of the entirely outside villa hotel has it's challenges, and the management clearly has not figured it out, and for that, you should stay away. The biggest problem: mosquitoes. I have left and am now at the St. Regis. I just counted, I have 17 bites, noticeable ones that itch. Arms, Legs, Ankles, Forehead, Back of Neck, etc. Before coming to Bali I was very aware of mosquitoes, I wore repellant the entire time I was here, including to sleep at night in the villa since it was obvious there were mosquitoes INSIDE our villa (#34). It was miserable, it felt like you couldn't get away from them. I was both happy and sad to leave, but more happy. When you pay $700+ per night to a hotel, you shouldn't leave with bug bites. The management of this hotel needs to seriously consider what it can do. Every AM their breakfast brunch is great, except i am a big fan of Muesli and both times I had the brunch there were ants in the Muesli. Another representative issue of how the bugs are a real problem here and management hasn't figure it out yet. To contrast I am at the St. Regis. It's not as breathtaking but is still beautiful, has better access to the beach and is considerably cheaper. The room is equally as nice / beautiful, just smaller, but still incredibly spacious. Most important, there are no mosquitoes and now I am applying calamine lotion trying not to let the bugs not bother the rest of my Asian trip. Really, this hotel should refund me for jeopardizing my entire trip. Fix your mosquito problem. Stick the torches up that keep the bugs away. Maybe I am oversimplifying the issue, but you have to do something about the bugs. I may post pictures later of all my mosquito bites but I figure I'll spare the TravelAdvisor community and you'll take my word for it.

title: Beautiful view
review_text: Very good location on a clif, with a mountain elevator to the beach and the sea. Villa’s with private butler and swimming pool facing the sea. Don’t leave food outside as the monkeys will invade the terrace and the outside area. Shower outside and very good bathroom. Spa and massages in an outside area facing the sea. Maybe the most relaxing one that I ever had. Couple of restaurants inside the property so for some days we didn’t leave the area. Private transfer from the airport to the resort.
insider_tip: Villa’s with private butler and swimming pool facing the sea

title: Everything you expect from a luxurious brand.
review_text: From the moment you are greeted to the moment you leave, you are in safe and caring hands. Every attention to detail is looked after and you are made to feel as special as any celebrity. The views are beautiful and the only sounds come from the ocean. The villas are very spacious in all areas, the room, the bathroom and the outdoor area. The only thing i would suggest is to include fly spray like other hotels. An insect repellant would be a nice touch too given the amount you are paying per night. Luckily we brought our own. Each room has a private pool surrounded by a lot of foliage, which could be part of the reason there are so many mosquito's. I would recommend this hotel as an experience, but there are plenty of other hotels that are just as nice for half the price. Also the buggy's that drive guests around can be quite noisy. There's not a lot to do outside of the resort, so best to make the most of your stay and enjoy the hotel and the Italian restaurant.
insider_tip: Ask for a room furthest from the lobby ensuring less chance of buggy's driving by. They are also building a wedding chapel, so try to stay away from that too due to noise. The chapel is behind the restaurant.

title: Style with wow again
review_text: This was my second time at the Bulgari. I had a wonderful stay last time but this time I was a little apprehensive. I had such a great visit previously that I was wondering if the hotel would live up to expectations again, especially as some of the recent Tripadvisors hadn't been great. However, any misgivings were quickly dispelled on arrival. Once again, I was wowed by the jaw dropping view from the arrivals area and the fantastic staff who did not miss a beat throughout our 2 night stay.We were in villa 37 (ocean view) which was great. Spotlessly clean, fantastic bed and the most amazing bathroom for 'ultimate bathing pleasure' according to my wife. My only brickbat would be the decking around our plunge pool which was showing signs of wear and tear and was in need of some maintenance. After this stay I decided there are 5 must do's when staying here. Number one is taking in the view and my most favourite spot is from the main pool. Swimming up to the infinity edge and looking out over the Indian Ocean is simply stunning. Number 2 is a leisurely breakfast. Try to get to breakfast early to get a table for 2 on the upper terrace and enjoy coffee and croissants while you are watching the waves. Number 3 is lunch at La Spiaggia on the cliff (access via the inclinator - very cool). It is only available to guests and there are only 2 tables (plus a lounge area below which is great for drinks). Simple but fantastic food (loved the grilled seafood) and even better views. You can hear and see the waves crashing on the beach. Number 4 is sunset at the bar with a cocktail or juice. It is also a nice spot to socialise with other guests. Number 5 is to have a spa but request a treatment room along the cliff for the sound of the waves and the birds in the trees. Combined with some fabulous therapists this makes for a memorable treat. My only other suggestion would be a late afternoon walk on the beach or dining at the Italian restaurant Il Ristorante which I missed out on this trip but enjoyed last time I stayed. One last tip: check out of your room and pack a day bag then go to lunch at La Spiaggia followed by a last swim as the pool area has shower and changing facilities (with Bulgari toiletries, of course!). It makes for a great end to the stay.
insider_tip: If your budget allows, opt for a cliffside room - they look amazing!!

title: There are so many better options in Bali!
review_text: My wife and I just back from a trip to Thailand and Bali. Of the 5 hotels we stayed(all in the same price range and service level of bulgari), Bulgari was our least favorite and most expensive. Our room would constantly leak every day. So, when we left in the morning there would be a puddle of water in the bathroom. When we woke up in the morning, there would be a puddle of water in the bathroom. Every day for 3 days. In addition to this, there seemed to be more mosquitoes in our room than outside our room, I have to assume the water collection everyday had something to do with that. Lastly, the bed spread had blood on it. The blood was not ours and unfortunately we found it in the morning after sleeping with it the night before. In addition to the room, there seemed to be a lot of misunderstandings with the hotel staff. Granted, we speak English and this is probably a second language to most of the staff, but there just seemed to be a lot of things they didn't understand or miscues on their end that could have easily been avoided. Lastly, the overall price at the hotel was a lot more than all the other places we stayed at(2 4 seasons, peninsula and a COMO property). There are so many other options in Bali, that would just avoid Bulgary altogether. There beach isnt anything special, you cant even swim in their portion of the ocean. The only redeemable thing is their views, but you can get that same view at a few other hotels in the area.
insider_tip: Make sure you room doesn't leak, if it does you will be bombarded with mosquitoes.

title: a little less than expected, but still a very good resort
review_text: We stayed at villa 29 for two nights, pretty good view to the ocean, right beside the reception building. very clean room, and the bathroom is super big with a bathtub, outdoor and indoor shower rooms. a little hand written welcome card was placed on our desk, very personal and delightful. the staffs were all friendly, helpful and polite, they greeted us by our names. buggy always came fast to pick us up anytime. our butler (Mr Ode) was very hands on to our needs. wifi connection covered almost the whole villa area, fast, free, and reliable too. if i can mention the downsides were: - doors in the villa are hard to open or lock (maybe i just didn't get the trick to do it) - the lighting in the bedroom is too dark for me - mosquitoes buzzing in my ear for the two nights we stayed there, even after applying the mosquito repellant - the sound of transporting buggies was heard pretty loudly from our room even after we closed all doors - the breakfast food taste and varieties are so so. would expect more for such a big name Bulgari - room air conditioning suddenly not cold enough at night (happened twice) one more thing; on the second morning at the breakfast place, my wife was approaching to the empty table at the corner of the patio. but she was stopped by a waiter telling her that the table had been taken (there were no reserved signs on it). so we took the table right behind it. and after the 35 mins+ we were there, nobody came and the table remained empty even after the breakfast time ended. we were just wondering why we weren't allowed to sit there. anyway, it's a very good resort if you want to stay in and enjoy the quite and be in touch with the more natural side of Bali
