title: Unexpectedly a nightmare stay & worst experience ever
review_text: Without any prejudice or discrimination i am honest with my feedback on my experience staying in Villa 47 Bulgari Hotel in Bali. It has a stunning views and food which cannot be dispute but the whole experience were a nightmare for us with it's service. I have been traveling around the world staying in the best hotel around the world and this is the worst ever that i could imagine the hotel staff can't wait throwing us out from the room.I am so upset till i have to make such feedback on my experience. The room were full of mosquitoes which i am not sure any regular fogging has been carried out. Mosquitoes were buzzing around my head in the whole night even after spraying the mosquitoes repellant. Many switches in the villa were spoil. Air conditioning is not working and i was sweating the whole night and kept waking up till the next day i woke up feeling dizzy and terrible where i have to be on my land in Bali working with the Land officer & focus under the hot Bali sun for hours and hours. Woke up in the morning make a quick call to the reception for a late check out due to i have to leave the villa immediately because the land officer has already arrived at the land without any prior notice, Bulgari reception instead of understanding client’s need she insisted pushing us out by 12pm even having said that I suggested to her that I might not come back on time and it will be safer to extend till 3pm instead of 12pm or 130pm because I am being realistic in time for me to come back and take a quick shower and pack which will only takes 10mins. She said to me our people can come in to your villa and pack all your stuff for you before you come back & leaving it at the concierge if i am unable to pack my things in time and you can shower at our outdoor area or spa area that sounded to me like throwing us out from the villa totally without considering our position at that point of time. So rude and not interested to empathize with client’s needs for that few hours of check out time. This is not a standard business city hotel , it is 7 star client experience hotel. I have no choice but to leave the villa immediately , when I left the villa i saw 2 empty wine bottles , wine glasses and broken glasses by the monkey in the morning were all over the floor including the roof top leaves mess on the floor. As expected we came back late due to the measurement, whole body in a mess walking in the bush, sweaty, headache, no energy and hot after measuring the land under the hot sun and hungry. We then stop by the restaurant and we ordered food and the reception at the restaurant came to us and start mentioning to us as in rushing us out from the villa after we have been super tired and hungry. After lunch we immediately go back to the villa , arrived looking at the mess outside the room 2 empty wine bottles, broken glasses on the floor, wine glasses and roof top leaves were all still on the floor meaning that no one actually came in to make up the room the whole morning. Went into our room everything is in a mess with 4 workers inside keeping everything , bedsheets all over the place, in the toilet collecting towels and so on treating us invisible with no care about us coming back into the villa whether we need privacy in order to have a quick shower or change & pack in 10mins, while we were brushing teeth the staff was standing in front of the basin not moving at all, went to the toilet , toilet papers were kept , went to shower left a bottle of conditioner on the floor and keep all the shampoos and body shower gel, Everything is in a mess that I could not recognize where my belonging were left before I leave the villa in the morning in urgency. We were very upset and requested to see the GM and he came to the villa with a sour face as in we were wasting his time after we specifically asking him to come so that we could feedback such a first time experience with Bulgari for their improvements, for a 7 star hotel which is more of a standard 3 star business city hotel , instead of like other 5-7 stars hotel that we have been around the world which provides solutions and suggestions comfortably to accommodate client’s needs if it is really inconvenience the clients. 4 days after returning back home I just realized that I have a very important book that I left on the side of the bed and due to the mess created by the house keeping around the bed area the book was covered underneath bedsheets and left behind without being returned to the lost and found dept. I called and someone by the name of Andara picked up the call and told me he will call me back after checking with lost and found department. The book was clearly left behind in the room when i left and it was a very expensive finance book that i have been doing a research on . With no initiative to further check in a few minutes time the staff called back immediately and just say sorry we did not have any lost and found reported not even checking with me the location of the book or being responsible for the mess that has been created in the room where all location of my things that I have left in the room before leaving the villa in the morning were not easily found. I asked someone to call us and make refund because of the mess they created and no one even care to call. It means no one does any checking as well on the villa before the guest leaves . This is a totally unacceptable in a 7stars hotel and it brings an embarrassment and brand damage to such a luxury brand “BULGARI” . I only wish if they could be more responsible for what they have caused at least making an effort to purchase the same book and refund it back to us. It is not about the book but the responsibility and effort that one make in a service and hospitality business to gain trust among clients with the hotel and leave with a good image. I am speechless with unexpected nightmare stay with Bulgari in Bali. I just hope to get my book back

title: Brilliant staff and location. Less than ordinary after that!
review_text: We loved the resort, position, position, position, HOWEVER all was not as promised. The staff were so so good, as only the Balinese can be, but surely management have to take responsibility for the many negatives that arose during our 4 night stay. I have been reading tripadvisor reviews for months about maintenance issues in the villas, we had doorknobs falling off, shower leaking, toilet constantly flowing and floorboards so badly warped that barefoot walking was a constant threat. Obviously management don't give a rats backside about guest reviews. Spa attendees are advised to arrive 40 mins early and spa menu is advertised. I arrived at 7.55 for a 8.30am appointment and waited till 8.20 for staff to arrive, no apology and during 2 visits I was not offered the menu to peruse. We were advised that late checkout was available as we had an 8.30 pm departure from the resort but all members of our group were turfed out of their rooms at 2.00 pm. We were told by many staff that occupancy was dropping considerably as our group used more than 50% of the resort, why upset paying guests for nonexistent ones??? It is only because the staff were so amazing that this review is offering a VG rating. If I return to Bali I will not be staying at Bulgari!
insider_tip: Make sure you door knobs aren't falling off!

title: Good but could be better!
review_text: We stayed at Bulgari during the Easter long weekend. We were glad that we used their airport transfer service as it is our first time to Bali and didn't know our way around. Bulgari staffs took care all of these for us. During our stay there, Bulgari staffs tried their best to make sure that we are happy and satisfied. One thing to note that is the buggy did take about 10 minutes to come. Maybe Bulgari should have a few more buggies to minimise the waiting time. We were running late for a few appointments because of this. I also really enjoyed the food served by both italian and Sangkar restaurants as well as in room dining. We ate at the Italian restaurant and were given complimentary breads, starter, sorbet ( to clean our palate) and chocolate truffles and turkish delight. Food was amazing and yummylicious. However, there was a monkey inside our villa on our last night in Bulgari and making such a loud noise ( right above our bed) that we couldnt sleep. We were initally told by the staffs to go back to sleep and they tried to convince us its a crab. In the end, we moved to another villla at 4.30am. Our plan to watch the sunrise and photographtaking was ditched as we were tired after this monkey drama. We stayed at villa 57, not close to the cliff at all. We had same issue that some of the reviewers had - the door is quite hard to open! We also had one of our best spa experiences here. I would conclude that my overall stay at Bulgari Bali was lovely but there is room for improvement. I will come back again providing that there is no monkey invading my villa.

title: Stunning View
review_text: The standout feature of this resort is obviously the location and view from the resort. Whilst the service staff tries their best, the overall standards are not quite perfect, though they are really minor issues such as late arrival of the buggie during checkout, we had to call the butler to re-confirm a beach trip instead of him calling back etc. The resort is getting old but well maintained. Breakfast was average. Beach remained closed but a nice trip to Finn's Beach Club was arranged by the resort. Overall a very lovely stay. But just not perfect.

title: Beyond highest expectations
review_text: This is my first review on trip advisor because I could not NOT highlight such an outstanding hotel. We have stayed in few of the best hotels in Asia i.e. several Aman resorts, 4 seasons in Maldives, Capella etc... but our stay at Bvlgari was just different from what we have encountered before. We stayed with my wife and 2 kids at the Bvlgari hotel at the end of March 2014. We had a cliff view villa with a spectacular ocean view lying 100m below our private infinity pool. The rooms were big and very comfortable with BO TV systems and with a nice large outdoor living room. The location is stunning with only 55 villas in total. We were expecting to be in a beautiful place but what was really different from anywhere else is the service and the friendliness of the staff. - 1) We felt like we were almost the only ones in the hotel while the hotel was 100% full. All the staff seemed to know us and call us by our names and were always willing to help with anything. - 2) My wife fall sick with sore that and fever on the 2nd day, while she was calling to book for a massage, the person at the reception asked her if she was feeling ok. When my wife told him she was not feeling well, Bvlgari managed to get a doctor coming into our villa 45min after the call. - 3) With 2 young children of 7y and 9y old, we decided to go to Finns (a beautiful pvt beach nearby). Every single time we asked to be driven there, the car and a butler accompanying us were ready within 5min. The butler came all the way down to make sure we would get good sits and asked Finns staff to take extra care of us. - 4) The top management of the hotel came to greet us everyday and took the time to talk to us about their personal experiences of living in Bali. Overall we felt like we were extra special guests at the Bvlgari and never encountered such quality of service/hospitality anywhere else in the world. We would highly recommend this place for if you are looking for experience of a life time.

title: Extremely disappointing
review_text: When I found out I was going to Bulgari Bali for my friends wedding, needless to say I was ecstatic. Unfortunately, the luxury branding of this resort is merely a name and the resort certainly fell very short of all it claimed to be. Why? 1. Difficult liaisons between administrative staff with long waiting periods between emails. 2. I almost missed my flight when the lobby staff gave away my taxi to someone else even though I had been specifically told 5 mins earlier while sorting out my account that my taxi had arrived. There was no apology by the staff , and even a long argument that it was not my taxi. Finally, the lady who gave my taxi away liased with her colleague and realised that she had indeed made a mistake. I then had to wait 45 mins for another taxi and stressed about time. Traffic and the weather was bad taking more than an 1.5 hr to get to the airport . I arrived 50 mins before my flight ,and thankfully my airline still accepted me as 60 mins is usually the cut off time .Had I missed my flight I would have been very peeved as everything had been a disappointment from day 1. 3. My account was wrongly charged . 2 out of 3 rooms where overcharged- ensure you check the amount on your bill ( in usd) and your credit card if you are paying upfront. 4. Although I had booked for a consecutive night, the front staff still rang me twice to ask me when I was checking out. This was even after I had rang them to tell them to clean up the room and reminded them that I was staying another night. 5. Poorly maintained villas - we had two areas in our villa that was leaking . As December is a rainy period, our room was constantly leaking . The staff were good to put towels on the floor , but for a luxury hotel- this should definitely be attended to. 6. The breakfast alacarte menu was pretty dismal. The nasi goreng and mee goreng was tasty , but the Japanese breakfast had salmon that had gone bad . Again , another unacceptable occurrence . However , when we did request for mangosteens, the staff did th their best to accommodate us and whipped some up for us! 7. The beach was closed . Oh dear . Need I say more . My friends and I spent more time complaining about this resort than raving about it. However, in their defence , the butlers, staff driving the buggies, waiting staff, were all incredibly hospitable and friendly . I believe that they always tried their best to provide assistance wherever possible. The villas were well sized and tastefully furnished. Our welcome mochis were also delicious and a nice touch. Unfortunately , the admin and reservations staff did disappoint us greatly. The management should also look into putting more effort into maintaining their beautiful resort. I will probably not return to Bali Bulgari , as many have recommended other places such as Alila or St Regis , where you will have felt that your dollar was well worth it.

title: Over rated !
review_text: The scenery is really fantastic. But that is just it. We were there for my cousin wedding. We booked weeks ahead and requested a twin bed, but upon check in, it is not available. They were kind enough to offer extra bed in the room. But looking at the bed they brought in, the bed must have been older than the hotel itself (Should have take photo of the extra bed). Dirty, holes, and with springs that can give you back pain. The room is in great need of renovation. The wooden floor creaks when you walk, some of the wood is rotten and maybe is due to the rainy season, so the wood kind of expand and you need to give the door an extra kick to open it. The worst is at night, i must have killed over twenty mosquito and yet they still keep coming. In the morning, was awaken by monkeys jumping on the roof of the villas. It's something new i must say. Around and in the hotel, there is pretty much nothing to do. One final word, if you are not here on honeymoon, better find another hotel.
insider_tip: Bring your own mosquito net. The amount of mosquito is just unbelievable.

title: Its like paradise on earth
review_text: Stayed at Bvlgari hotel and resorts and was in awe upon arrival with the views and the excellent welcome team at the hotel. The service from check in till check out was really commendable. The villa that we stayed was just beyond words especially the views and the in room decoration and amenities. The view was also to die for. But one comment I have is that their bathroom is beautiful and huge but there were no doors and we were three friends sharing a room as we went a group so it was a bit inconvenient in a way. Other that that, there were lots of mosquitoes in the night so you need to bring lots of insect repellent. All in all it was a wonderful stay and would highly recommend this hotel for honeymoon couples or just to relax and get away from it all! Two thumbs up !!

title: One of the best hotels in the world!
review_text: We travel A LOT and definitely Bvlgari Hotel is one of the best hotels in the world. I know that the fee is high but you have to understand that the quality of EVERYTHING here is the best. We came from Brazil to spend 4 days in this beautiful hotel, one week before our arrival we received a very nice email from Septi, the guest relations, explaining all the process to arrive in Bali, sending the restaurant menu among other things. She was incredible and answered all my emails very quickly arranging all we need to dive in Bali, she also offered to find anything we need during our visit (tours, restaurants, taxi, etc.) and to help us anytime. isn't it amazing? When we arrived at the airport there was a guy waiting us to make our immigration. I strongly recommend this option cause the lines are huuuuuge and it's cheap, around 30US per person, after this the transfer from the hotel (BMW X5) was waiting for us with bottles of water and delicious cookies from the hotel bakery. We arrived at the hotel and did our check in very quickly. The employees of the hotel were wonderful and they took us in a golf car to our room, inside the room they explained about the room and it's facilities and about the hotel, 24 hours room service :) I mean not only sandwiches but everything! you can have your breakfast in your room no extra charges, the room is exactly what you seed in the pics, the size is good and the amenities are perfect! they have everything, even beach bags, hats and flip flops! All the employees knew our names, they want to make it personal, they want you to feel home and they make all the necessary effort to realize all your needs. We went to the Italian restaurant all days: IL Restaurant, there is a italian called Carlos how surprised us every night with his great sense of humor and his excellent knowledge. Every day they left in our room some art craft as a gift. I have to say that we were spoiled a lot, it's great when you have so many ppl working to make your stay the most incredible as they can, you can see that they have passion about working in that hotel and this is nice to see. I strongly recommend the SPA and the yoga classes during the morning. The place looks like a temple, you will definitely love it! The only thing that I have to say that is not 100% is the location, not because of the perfect and peaceful ocean view but because it's far from most of the places you want to visit... but hey? what I am talking about? This is a hotel to forget about the world and live every single minute enjoying its facilities.
insider_tip: Choose the cliff view villa, the best view of the ocean :D anyway all the villas are perfect, you will be fine

title: Too many Mosquitos won't come back again
review_text: We were living in a two bedroom villa. There were many Mosquitos inside the rooms and everywhere. We couldn't sleep well due to that. title: Simply Amazing.
review_text: It was my first visit to Bali, Indonesia and I have to say, soon I'll be heading there once again thanks to the extraordinary experience I had from staying with Bvlgari Hotels & Resorts Bali. From the moment I got off the airplane, a representative from the hotel was already waiting for me a few feet away. Upon greeting me & my family with a beautiful smile and after going through the immigration formalities, she then escorted us to our dear friend and driver Made who's been waiting for us outside Ngurah Rai Airport to be transported to our villa. Upon stepping out of the limo to the main lobby of the hotel to complete the check-in process, I was taken by surprise, by the peace and serenity of the area. It was so tranquil you can literally hear the sound of a pin drop. The check-in went smoothly and off we went to our villa, in a nice buggy. Now the villa, it's impossible to explain with words how beautiful it is. You must see it, feel it, to believe it. My favorite part? Everything. It was a blessing that my room's located just a few steps away from the hotel's Spa Centre. In totality, I've never had a better Balinese massage experience than the one I had here. Thinking about it, I'm starting to miss the spa treatment a lot! Food here is amazing too. I would highly recommend The Sangkar Restaurant which offers a more local cuisine. I managed to try nearly everything on the menu here so I would recommend that you try the Bebek Betutu, Pork Ribs Kimchi, Satay Lilit, okay everything on the menu is awesome so try everything. Be sure to try the Manjari Hazelnut Cream jam too. Additional advice? Sit outdoors, enjoy the breathtaking view ;) If you want to discover the true meaning of world-class hospitality service, come to this restaurant to discover it. Special mention to Graha Satria, Diva Alvita and the entire Sangkar restaurant team for making my dining experience such a great pleasure, every single time. The Italian restaurant here Il Ristorante is outstanding as well in terms of food quality, service and restaurant environment. Would highly recommend the Barrumundi main course and Truffle Pasta. Perfect for a romantic dinner for two. The swimming pool in this hotel is really breathtaking too. Very suitable place for a nice picture. Just a stroll around the resort is enjoyable too. Just don't forget to bring your camera along and snap away. As a hotelier myself, the experience I gained from here taught me a lot. Sincerely look forward to return, in the very near future.

title: Bad Experience
review_text: Stayed one night for my friend's wedding last weekend. I arrived at the villa around 5:45 PM and I was in hurry to attend the cocktail reception at 6 PM. Since I needed an iron and iron board urgently, I called the guest services, and she said that "we run-out of the iron and iron board". At 6:15 PM, she called again after discussed with her manager, the option was to send my shirt for pressing and charged me. I told her that it was too late and I asked to speak with the manager. At 6:20 PM, the FOM called (with an attitude) and offered a complimentary pressing which was too late since I was about to leave the villa. I did not want anything, I just wanted to have my iron and iron board at the villa, which both of the staff did not understand and they were keep saying "what could we do?" and without resolution. It was not cheap to stay at this property which I expected to have a good service which they failed to provide and no apologise was given during my stay. To be honest, I wasn't feel the Luxury!, many defects at the villa. Breakfast was not Bvlgari at all, the orange juice was not fresh squeezed and cold cuts were not fresh and dry.

title: Fantastic Experience
review_text: What can we say other than "thank you" Bulgari for making our wedding the most spectacular and memorable experience ever. From the planning stages through to the execution on the day, and the stay after, we can not fault the tireless attention to detail that the staff went through to make everything special for us and our guests. We opted for the Water wedding which in all honesty totally exceeded the marketing materials .... finding ourselves standing on a floating transparent platform atop an infinity pool overlooking the Indian Ocean at sunset, a white carpet of rose petals perfectly offset by an azure sky with hints of orange coming through as the sun set was something that we and our guests will never forget! The whole day was fantastic with great food, entertainment and attentive friendly service, cannot recommend highly enough. The villa was an immaculate Bali inspired thatched ceiling bungalow, comfy bed, slate bathroom with sunken bath and indoor and outdoor shower facilities. Amenities were high class including Bang and Olufsen in room entertainment centre, topped off with outdoor living area with private plunge pool with a view over the Indian Ocean. Fantastic.

title: Spent two nights being dripped on by leaking AC, potholes in floor of 'luxury' villa - stay at Ayana instead!
review_text: I have honestly never been so disappointed by a five star hotel in my life. We left the Ayana (gorgeous, amazing service) and checked in to Bulgari to get married (I fell in love with the idea of a water chapel wedding and photos on the beach). I've seen all the reviews that talk about how luxurious Bulgari is, and maybe I am just spoiled, but Bulgari cannot compare to any other 5 star villa resort that I have stayed at in Asia. Even in Uluwatu there are much nicer places with far better service that you can stay at for less spend. No one with any impact at the Bulgari resort seems to care whether you are satisfied or not. The guys that ferry you about by buggy, the waiters, even the chefs are so sweet but the management is just so nonchalant. The arrogance of the place is insane. 1. I am two months pregnant, and yet when we checked in to get married there was no one to meet us to check us in to the hotel we were getting married at! On my wedding day I had to haul suitcases and my wedding dress out of the taxi and actually go wandering around to look for where to check-in. Sorry, but even if you go to a 5-star hotel for dinner there's always a bell boy there as standard to welcome and help with any bags?! 2. First impressions of the villa - the varnish on the outdoor floor boards is peeling and faded. It just looks tired. It costs nothing to sand and revarnish floor boards?! I would never let my home floor boards get to this kind of state - and Bulgari is meant to be in the business of hospitality and luxury... 3. Speaking of floor boards, getting ready for my wedding, I fell down a small pot hole in the floor of the 'luxury' villa by the heel of my shoe (pictured). It might sound petty - but this is meant to be five star and the place is just worn out. There were black stains on our curtains, too. It was just depressing! 4. My biggest issue was the AC and the lack of concern of anyone in the hotel to fix it. On my WEDDING NIGHT I didn't get a wink of sleep (and not in the good way either!) because the AC dripped all over me - not in a little way - look at the photo of filthy brown water dripping on to the bed!! In the morning I complained to house keeping and the butler, who promised that an engineer would come and fix it. We returned to the room at around 6pm and it was still leaking, the duvet was soaked through. I called to complain again about the leak and the soaked bedsheets, and when we came back to the room a few hours later the sheets had been changed byt the AC was still faulty and dripping all over the new sheets - they hadn't done a thing to fix the AC. Beggars belief, I know. I complained again - begging someone to fix the AC so that I could get some sleep. The engineer's idea of 'fixing' the AC was to place a board over the vent. Sure, I wasn't getting dripped on from the bed anymore, but the bedroom was then sweltering hot - literally no AC (not even a basic fan out of courtesy) in a so-called luxury hotel for the whole night?!!! I'm pretty sure that in a tropical climate, that there is no hotel - of any star - that would do this to guests. Considering that this was meant to be the wedding of my dreams, it's almost as if Bulgari did everything that they could to make it miserable. To make matters worse the water that would have dripped on me re-routed to the left hand corner of the room - all over the beautiful jewelery my new husband had gifted me on my wedding day. It was just disgraceful. 4. The breakfasts were nice, and I will say that the coffee is good. Note that if you stay in Villa 25 you will need the caffeine to get you through the days after those humid, uncomfortable, sleepless nights. 5. The events coordinator is a vile woman who is more unhelpful than you can imagine. I only wanted to get married in Bali for the water chapel wedding and the photos on the beach. The beach was closed - had been for weeks, with no plans to open anytime soon. But no one thought to tell us this when we were paying the upfront, pretty hefty fee for the ceremony. My wedding planner hadn't been informed either. I only found out when I was asking the photographer on the day to take us to the beach for some nice shots on the sand. Instead of offering helpful alternative beach locations, the Bulgari lady just adamantly insisted that it wouldn't be possible. I was reduced to tears by this woman an hour before my ceremony. Luckily the photographer was a very kind Balinese man who took us to a public beach the following day to take some nice photos. 6. Heavy rain fall meant that my water chapel wedding - the only reason I wanted to get married at Bulgari - didn't happen. I'm not blaming Bulgari for the rain fall, and the team did do a lovely job of making the alternate venue, a gazebo, as nice as they could. The team comped a spa treatment for us because of the rain, which was nice. But Ayana (where we had stayed for the five days leading up to the wedding) also comped us a couple's massage just as a celebratory gesture to get married. I'm not being ungrateful - but comping spa visits is pretty standard when you are spending the kind of money that these resorts charge, especially when you are getting married, anniversaries, honeymoons, etc. Rant over. You won't catch me staying at Bulgari ever again, and we are regular visitors to Bali, especially Uluwatu. The grounds are very pretty and well maintained, but the villas are worn out and can't compete with the other luxury resorts in Uluwatu. Plus, the commitment to guest satisfaction is bordering zero. Stay at your own peril, people!
insider_tip: Save yourself the stress and stay at Alila or Ayana.

title: Overrated over priced
review_text: This perhaps is my lowest review on all the 5 star luxury resot I ever been, Lets star with the great. The view and location is amazing and breath taking, sitting on the cliff side facing ocean, every inch of the property is a pleasure to stay and relax. All units are villa style, with private pool and patios. Bvgalry brand tolietry and decor are stylish and chick. Staffs are always smiling and friendly, greeting you by your Last name. Then it stops here. We requested a Ocean view Villa, but stuck in a Villa with view on bushes and roofs. Took 6 calls and 3 hours later to tell us they wll try to accomodate us the next day on new villa. The buttler handles 20+ customer , so dont expect to see him too often, we often had to explain all over to a new person on the phone or in person. The SPA and beach are under maintanace, so my spa was done in a shallow corner in other side of the property--neverthe less not impressive with the service, shower didnt have the towel to use. the fire alarm went off in the middle. since the beach was closed we were arrange to Hyatt property for lunch. When we call to reschedule 30 min later, the conciere couldnt find our arrangement-- just after we end up ordering room service..they call back saying they were waiting for us to take us over ( 30 min drive). Only two restuarant at site, and the food is very average compare to other resorts in the area.* pricy too**, One of our Villa has broken phone, broken faccents so they couldnt use the bath at all. The villa is heavy infested with mosiquito & ants inside bathroom. I would strongly suggest management to provide repeller lamp, no the spray that dont work. Perhaps we are bit harsh on them, but if I pay 1500 a night, you do expect bit more on the service and quality of your stay. Unfortunally there is lots of choices in the area with similar or better pricing same amazing view. They did not wow me at all, exept the time I saw all those monkey on the roof top. Sorry, not coming back Bvlgary, Keep your day job: jewls.

title: A hotelier's review of another hotel
review_text: Went to Bulgari for 4 days 3 nights and I must say it is one of the grandest most spectacular resort in the world...but none of it will be important compared to the warm hospitality and diligence of the great people who work there. I booked for an arrival transfer and was wowed when the greeter quickly ushered me through the crowd and got my passport stamped for me, effectively jumping a few hundred people's queue. The limo had nice towels and glass bottled branded water. A short trip of 40 minutes got me there, to a remote dry area near a cliff next to the ocean(not sea). Security is tight at Bulgari and you will love it, as it keeps the exclusivity of the resort. Check-in was smooth and I was given a minty welcome drink and before I knew was in the buggy to my villa with Putu, my butler. He knew when to be there, and gave follow up calls to ensure I was well served. Loved his service and sincerity. Next the villa, is gaga jaw-dropping. It has a outdoor living room next to 2 sunbeds with a quite deep plunge pool(more than 1.6m I could do thread water as exercise in the morning). The indoor area had a extremely high thatched Balinese roof above the large comfy bed, making one feel like a sultan. The bathroom was stupendous with a sunken bathtub, separate rain shower, and outdoor shower as well. All stocked with luxurious soap and even shaving cream. Spa was best I had. I've probably gone through more than 59 therapists in my career with familiarization trips, complimentary sessions etc. The therapist Nitha(I hope I got her name right)could stretch out all the knots, and I have a twitchy sensitive back, which she managed to knead without me feeling ticklish. Throughout the session there was that assuring pop sound of revitalized muscle, tendon and bones.Her strokes were sure and strong, excellent for a weekend athlete like myself. In the end I requested for therapy from her again the next day. I love Italian food and ate there at Il Ristorante all the time. The staff there know me by name so well. I was highly tempted to drink the supertuscan saccicaia but as I already had a haut-brion in my room, I settled for some other unique varieties of Italian wine like my favourite Soave. I ate so much there oh my god. Then I had a bright idea, on my last evening. I asked the chef to create me a linguine with my favourite foie gras as the highlight and he made this really memorable linguine with foie gras which was perfectly al dented I swear god must love me so much to bless me with. I spent my days in the infinity pool, hot/cold jacuzzi, exercising in the villa(yoga), scuba diving...it was great. It's so exclusive there is no competition for resources here. No noisy kids running around, no irritating tourists or tour guides. At this level of achievement, the guests definitely know how to behave well. I had an early flight out so I booked for a in-room breakfast. Got my breakfast fully customized. I wanted a whole pot of coffee, freshly squeezed green apples, mangosteens, and a pasta and there it was. Secretly the in-room breakfast was my safety net to ensure I woke up to catch the flight, as I told them to get the captain to enter my room to ensure I was awake. It was nice to be served by Sonny, Putu, Anthony, Ong Kim, Jessica(restaurant host) and look forward to see them again in a few moons. Now I am writing this from home, I miss this place. My maslovian needs are all fulfilled here.

title: Heaven!
review_text: Amazing amazing amazing! This is probably the nicest resort in Bali! Everything is perfect here- the food, the service, the perfectly manicured grounds, the location, the views, the villas etc! We stayed at this property for 5 nights and I cant wait to go back. Our villa was huge with beautiful views of the Indian Ocean… an outdoor living and dining room and a beautiful private pool. I hardly ever wanted to leave the room! The bed, bedding, toiletries etc were all so top notch. Gold flatware and beautiful Bvlgari dish ware as well. The food on this resort was some of the best I’ve ever had in my life. Usually room service food is sub par- but the Bulgaris room service menu was amazing! Had dinner at their Italian restaurant Il Ristorante twice and loved every single morsel of food. It was awarded ‘BEST HOTEL RESTAURANT IN THE WORLD by International Food & Beverage forum and it completely lives up to that claim. We enjoyed our share of lobster, wagyu beef, fresh pasta and seafood while staying here and everything was expertly prepared every time. Cocktails were extremely unique and made with top shelf liquor, fresh pressed juices and exotic fruits. On to the spa- I had read a ton of reviews boasting about how unbelievable this spa is but unfortunately while we were there is was under construction. I was super bummed about this because I utilize the spa every single day on vacation. However, they relocated it to one of their private residences on site (which was selling for 7m US) for the time being which was an enormous mansion with beautiful views. Also, the moment I expressed the slightest bit of dissatisfaction about this spa scenario, the manager offered my husband and I a complimentary 90 min couples massage in the privacy of our own villa showered in rose petals! (a $350 value). I thought that was really classy of them! We really appreciated that! Turn down service was great- they always left little gifts like aromatherapy bath salts and candles or a really nice lavender eye mask for sleep etc. The gym was the nicest hotel gym I’ve been in. The main pool and bar are so breathtaking I wished I could just stand there and stare at the views forever. Service was 20/10. Our butler was superb and every staff member we walked by on the resort called us Mrs + Mr Barber with a friendly smile. At a price tag of minimum $1,400US for the lowest standard room (keep in mind we are in Bali here), not including any food or drink. this hotel carries a hefty price tag… BUT its so luxurious and worth the experience if you can afford it! Thank you Bvlgari Bali for making our Balinese vacation unforgettable! Will be back for sure!!

title: Wow Factor with Style and Charm.
review_text: The Bulgari is all about the wow factor, style and charm. While I would really recommend this for couples we had our 9 year old son with us and we all received fantastic service with loads of smiles. My other recommendation is to pay the extra for the ocean view because with this hotel it is all about the view. However, if you can’t afford the extra spend you can always hang out at the pool and bar area and get your view fix there. As for eating, don't miss Il Ristorante, the high end signature restaurant which serves modern Italian with fresh Balinese ingredients. It was fantastic silver service dining but we did not like the fact they allowed smoking. We luckily avoided a couple of smokers who were seated next to us just as we finished our meal. Admittedly, the restaurant is open air but having smokers next to us when we were eating would have really spoiled the experience. Breakfast is also a highlight, especially on the terrace . For a special occasion or a treat a couple of nights here is an amazing experience.

title: Beware of the monkeys here!
review_text: We planned a trip here and thought it would be a dream vacation. It turned out to be a nightmare. This resort is beautiful, and is in an incredible spot. However, we did not realize it was inhabited by monkeys, as the resort is built on a cliffside of a former jungle that has been home to the monkey population forever. We rented a cliffside villa, #11, for the stunning views. It was unfortunately the favorite hang out place for a large clan of monkeys (around 20). They invaded our space, opened the sliding doors, trashed the place and ate all the food (coffee packets included!) We had to keep calling for help to chase them off. It made me so uneasy, and we felt like prisoners in our own villa. If you don't lock the doors (all the rooms are separate spaces) they will come in. One brave rather large monkey followed us right into the bedroom to retrieve some fruit, and then proceeded to eat it on the doorstep! You cannot move freely about this type of villa without being invaded and feeling very vulnerable. The staff there are lovely people, but there is very little they can do other than assist you and say they are sorry about the monkeys. My complaint is that we had no warning from the hotel, except a little card on the bedside table alerting us to the "local inhabitants" and said don't leave your belongings outside, as the monkeys will take things! I had no idea it would be the most stressful trip of my life. These monkeys can be aggressive and have been known to bite people for no reason. There are other villas where this is not as much of a problem. But, if you are renting cliffside, you have been warned! These monkeys also enjoy invading the restaurant at breakfast time. It was comical to a degree. The staff are used to chasing them off. Glorious end to our stay was when the brakes failed on one of the buggies (golf cart) used to cart us up the steep roads to the entrance of the resort. We nearly missed being thrown over a wall because the driver hit a wall as we were hurling backwards. Nothing like a bit of whiplash and a few scrapes to end your 5 star vacation. I cannot recommend this place!

title: 'The Bar' .. I though I'd died and gone to heaven
review_text: This hotel is absolutely breathtaking. Security is stringent on arrival. At reception we were taken by buggy to ‘The Bar’. As visitors we were welcomed to the resort. This resort is built on a cliff face, everything is spread out so you need to catch a buggy almost everywhere. The grounds are lush and immaculate. The Bar is absolutely amazing, it has a beautiful trendy Balinese/outdoor feel – it’s situated on top of a cliff, looking over an infinity pool and the cliffs. I immediately decided that I wanted to stay here forever. This is the perfect place for an intimate lunch or cocktail. We started with a drink and were offered a selection of complimentary nibbles which were delightful. We then had coffee and cake this again was divine. We didn’t stay for lunch as we decided to go to the Alia in Uluwatu (wish we had). This was the highlight of my trip to Uluwatu - I will definitely be back.

title: Experience of a Lifetime
review_text: Got the chance to stay in this hotel. The name itself spells LUXURY. When we arrived at the airport, we were welcomed by a Bvlgari Staff and assisted us in the immigration, got a VIP treatment if you are staying at the hotel. On the way to the hotel, the car service (provided by the hotel) had refreshments and fresh towels ready to use, this gave me the impression that i will be having a great time in the hotel - and it was true enough all troughout our stay. We were welcomed by all smiling and very friendly staff with a drink called Virgin Mojito (non-alcoholic drink) which was superb and very well presented - and the view from the reception was - BREATHTAKING. We were transported to our villa using a baggy by our assigned Butler Putu - he was very helpful even offered to tour us around the area before settling our things in our villa, and yet again i was astounded by the scenic view and amenities they offer. Our villa is the best yet so far in entire existence, we had a mini pool with two pool beds onothe sides, our own dining area, inside the room we had a very luxurious bath tub and shower, complete with all amenities (Bvlgari perfumes, lotions, shampoo, etc) and the king size bed where you just want to sleep all day and the tv and speakers which am very particular was Bang and Olufsen, again spell the BEST! The best thing there is they also offer in room dining too which was amazing. We had breakfast, lunch and dinner at the hotels resto which was called Sangkar - food was great and service was excellent (the best so far). The second day we planned to take a dip in the pool. When we arrived at the pool area, we were again greeted by their very friendly staff and Ari (i guess he was the supervisor), again welcome drinks were offered and a tray of sunblocks and tanning lotions with different SPF were offered - amazing service. We were also treated with satay and lychee flavored ice-drops. The following day we were supposed to visit the beach however, it was was under construction so they offered an alternative instead, the hotel arranged everything from car service to food and drinks ) heading to their partner hotel - again it was another awesome experience. We stayed at the hotel for 4 days and indeed it was an great experience. The service i believe is the best. They were very warm and even try to call you by your first name. I didn't find anything negative to write about the hotel. It twas all about pleasure and luxury at its best matched with excellent service. I miss Bali but it was Bvlagri Hotel and Resort that made memories to last a lifetime.

title: The best resort I ever stayed at !
review_text: There is nothing I can fault this resort on. I will list the things that impressed me the most: 1. All the staff were friendly and helpful. It was a good touch to be addressed personally when attending dinner and breakfast. 2. Room service was the fastest I have experienced. We requested a bucket of ice and within 5 to 10 minutes it was delivered. 3. I asked for 2 pieces of clothes to be ironed. The long pants had 2 small holes in them, before I sent them off. Room service phoned me to check if indeed they were damaged. Any other hotel would have not bothered. 4. The food and wine in the restaurant was of the highest quality. 5. The Villa was amazing Yes it's expensive but you MUST experience this resort ! The only negative was that the lift down to the beach was under maintenance.

review_text: It's their wonderful hospitality that keeps me coming back especially to their restaurants. Their receptions, waiters/waitresses, security guards even the buggy driver are so friendly. review_text: Bulgari is a good choice to stay in Uluwatu, strikingly beautiful and delightfully less dense part of Bali. You can trust Bulgari be a Bulgari so it's a good choice to stay: the best food on the island , rooms are moody and sleek with industrious maggiordomo, the finest stationary, stylish resort bags and robes, selection of delectable treats with turndown service, great private yoga under cruel tutelage of an Indonesian version of Pai Mei (thumbs up). 4 stars for such niceties? The inclinator to the beach was out of order both times in 2007 and 2013. Nonetheless I'm tenacious and will for sure come again, at least for lunch.

title: Great experience ...
review_text: Stayed here with a group of friends on holiday in September 2013. This review is being made keeping in view the level of expectation one expects from the Bulgari brand. First the few complaints. Service for food takes more than normal time, not the standard 30 minutes fare in the villas. The check in could have been faster. I have tasted much better and tastier Italian food in many many more hotels and restaurants. The villa in which I stayed did not have the straw slippers which was provided in my friends villa (not that I needed them). The dry fruits provided in two bowls in the living room did not get replaced or removed for five days even after they were opened, same with the sweets in the bedroom. The other villa did not get the dry fruits. The housekeeping can definitely improve as the mini bar never got refilled. They also left an ugly crate of drinking water bottles in the middle if our living room throughout our stay. The above factors did not in any manner effect our stay, but as I mentioned earlier, this review is being made in view of the highest level of expectations from a brand like Bulgari. Another disappointment was our inability to access the beach. The hotel communique said that the beach has been closed because of the rains (surprising) but we later realised that the lift that connects to the beach had been out of order for weeks. Another disappointment was that we requested for a late check out. We left at 8 pm. But this was not accepted and we had to pay for an extra day though we never stayed an extra day. Now the positives. The service provided by the butlers were almost impeccable. They walked more than the extra mile for all our needs and demands. The food is good and more to an international palate and not as Balinese as in restaurants and in the street. The breakfast were excellent and no matter what time you walked in, you will get a full breakfast. The spa is perhaps one of the best I have ever been to. The open spa rooms that open into the ocean was truly relaxing. The therapists are amongst the best in the world. The Balinese hot stone massage is a must. The swimming pools are great. All pools are infinity pools including the one in my villa. The ocean cliff villas we stayed in (19 and 11) had magnificent views of the ocean. Full marks to the butlers. The resort is designed in a very natural environment and the are no signs of noisy modernity like cars and machines. Only golf buggies are used for transportation. We enjoyed a full roasted suckling piglet for our last meal. Though not part of the menu the chef prepared an extraordinary meal which was out of the world. It was brought down to the villa and the chef was on standby to cut and serve us. There are some interesting stuff to pick up in the gift shop. I bought a few things which were too big to fit into my suitcase but the butlers got them packed and ready and sealed without any problems. The butlers will do anything for you, from getting you SIM cards, paying your phone bills, hiring a hatch, anything. All in all, a wonderful holiday. A great place for rest and relaxation and for a honeymoon. I will definitely go again if the opportunity comes. And I also recommend it for all my friends. The resort is located in a village and the road to the resort is in very poor condition but that is perhaps not the resort's fault. The free gifts at the spa and the wooden toys were a nice touch. If you are on a honeymoon or on a rest and relaxation holiday, this is the place for you as you are away from it all. The Bose music systems are welcome. A holiday I will remember of a long time. Thanks Bulgari.
insider_tip: Look out for the "local residents" the monkeys. Keep your villas closed and if eating outside keep a watch. They even come and grab food from your plate.

title: Perfectly Picturesque
review_text: The Bulgari Resort was absolutely stunning. My husband and I stayed here for the final 3 nights of our Honeymoon. We booked the cliff-side villa. It was worth it. The views were incredible and the space was completely private. We were in our own little paradise with a infinity pool right outside our door on the edge of a cliff overlooking the ocean. The resort was very tranquil and the service was excellent. The staff was extremely responsive to any request. The beach at the hotel was unfortunately closed, but we were provided free transportation to 3 nearby resort beaches and a Bulgari staff member stayed with us at our beach visits to make sure we were attended to for food and drinks. The villa itself was beautifully designed and combined traditional Balinese architecture with European elements. I must advise if staying at the resort to be sure your doors are closed and secure at all times. We must have left a door unlocked on our trip to the spa and a monkey made its way into the room and ate a platter of fruit. Luckily it had its snack and left immediately because nothing else was touched.
insider_tip: For the price of the hotel, it's worth the extra cost to experience the cliff-side villa. The views are amazing and the outdoor space is completely private.

title: Decent Hotel; Stay elsewhere
review_text: I stayed 3 nights for a friend's wedding in Bali. Location is good; beach was closed so had to share a beach with the Marriott property. Resort itself is pretty, the villa was nice. Finishes were spectacular but a little over the top i.e.bang and olufsen TV is not necessary. A large Sony seems a lot more practical. Bathroom was nice and spacious, private dipping pool was small but functional. I feel like I paid a lot for the "bulgari" name rather than the actual hotel. A lot of it seemed a little gimmicky or obnoxious i.e. checking vehicles for bombs at the entrance gate of the hotel. One thing that was undoubtedly spectacular was their service. My butler (forgot his name) arranged everything I wanted in my suite to perfection. They also threw in a free gift everyday which was kind of nice. Two disappointing things about this hotel: Laundry service: laundry was pretty bad. Melted some of my buttons of my shirt and made a new ironing line on my trousers. Breakfast was not very good. Would not stay here again.

title: Full Occ Not an Excuse for Slow Service
review_text: I just came back from Bvglari , Uluwatu and had a lovely last evening but there were a number of hiccups and this is not to blame entirely on the management, but there was a lack of consistency in SOP with the staff. Some things can be let go of, but for the price you pay, I know having been to smaller resorts in Bali, you expect Value Exchange. 1.The room was beautiful and clean, but 2/3 times with the Do Not Disturb sign on, our room was entered anyway. 2. When I asked staff for a missing room coffee filter for the loose Coffee to strain, the staff had no idea and neither attempted to find out and get back to me. 3. We ordered the golf karts to the lobby , and 2/3 times they were VERY late and we were nearly late for the wedding in the estate across from us. I felt they should have let us know a time frame, so perhaps we could have walked to the lobby instead. It took 30-45mins. 4. Ordering items like cigarettes took just as long, 30 misn and we had to call at least 3 times. 5. At breakfast I was refused in the buffet area with a bikini top and shorts, which I understood, so we were told to sit at the bar just below, which in fact was a lot quieter and a lot more intimate, which we really appreciated. The next day we asked to sit there again, dressed appropriately this time, but different staff, said different things with allowing us and we were left hanging a bit too long, so with lack of consistency in training, they should be able to tell us off the cuff what we can or cannot do. We ended up eating there any way. 6. I had a mini ordeal with my mobile dropping into the pool, so I asked if it was okay to have a tiny bowl of uncooked rice, which we honestly thought after all the waiting we would not expect to be charged , so we were surprised when they wanted to charge us for the price of cooked rice. 7. The last day at dinner , my partner who enjoys drinking Coke cola ordered a glass and upon drinking it , he let the staff know this was diet coke or either Pepsi, which he does not like . I tried it and let the staff know they made a mistake, fair enough. But they came back and insisted it was coke. We ordered a bottle of still water on the side of our meal , and they filled our glasses once and we never saw the other half of the bottle. When the bill came, the order said "Diet Coke", and it was obvious they HAD made a mistake and charged us for a full bottle of still which we would have drank if it was left on our table. We had voiced our concerns to the Director of Rooms, Marcel, who was a fantastic guy who understood our frustrations at the small things, but to be honest we had only wanted the name of the MOD when we asked the bar staff and they ended up calling the BAr manager who then called the D.O.R and we really wanted to eat our breakfast in peace, but they ran around calling all the managers when we JUST wanted a name. We explained none of them were in trouble we just wanted a Name. 8. Lastly consistency in breakfast was average. The first day we had a lovely Eggs Benedict on bread with potato , fried tomato, with chicken sausage and bacon, the next day, there was no sausage or no bacon with the meal?!? Well overall , I still would like to go back, I just wish they would take care of the smaller details for the price that you pay.

title: Amazing location
review_text: We stayed here for two nights as part of our honeymoon. We had high expectations for the hotel and were not disappointed. title: Very good but not quite great.
review_text: We stayed at the Bulgari hotel in Sept '13. This is our fourth visit to Bali (previously stayed at Bale, Ritz Carlton, and Four Seasons) and as before thoroughly enjoyed our visit. The hotel villas are very nice and very private. They're a little dark for my taste but I think this is more of a brand-Bulgari choice rather than a fault. The TV/audio are high spec and have good channel access for the late night vegeing when back from dinner. For those who like to keep in touch with the world the wifi is excellent. The food in the Italian restaurant and the cocktails at the bar are of the highest quality but a little pricey when compared to what you can get 20-30min away in Jimbarin. The staff make the difference between most other hotels I've stayed in here in Bali with them meeting every unusual request I had. Serious thanks to them and they're a credit to the Island the hotel's management. The hotel's plus points are; 1. definitely one of the quietest hotels i've stayed at in Bali. heard zero noise, music, etc. for our neighbour villas despite being relatively close. 2. amazing views 3. very friendly staff 4. very private The negative points are; 1. mosquitoes (five or six flying around in the spa changing room) seemed more prevalent than other hotels I've stayed in. Granted this is pretty subjective. 2. food quite pricey and very little options
insider_tip: The cliff Villas have the most amazing views but the Ocean villas are much cheaper and although have a slightly less impressive view and are a little smaller are definitely the best value by far.

title: Over Promise, Over Deliver
review_text: We added a few days at Bulgari, at the end of a 2 week holiday to Bali, following visits to both of the Bulgari restaurants on previous holidays.If you can afford the rate,just do it, worth every last cent ! Ocean Cliff villa, very spacious, indoor/outdoor everything it seemed. All classy amenities,Bang & Olufsen sound, classy check-in service 'in the room', not at the usual 'check-in desk'. In both restaurants top quality food, presentation and service fantastic. For something different, the beautiful 'StarLight Dinner'. A seven course degustation menu at a table for two on an exquisite private terrace with the lead-up path adorned with rose and frangipani petals.One of the most 'romantic' dinners on the planet. The Spa was excellent, we managed a treatment everyday, was all perfect and as good as you'll find anywhere in the world.The overall hotel service also delightful.Our names and our F&B preferences at all times seem to be remembered. We've found there's so much to be gained from proactively sharing this information with staff, if only it's the "extra salt" with your margarita.Wine list is more than adequate, local wines are worth a try but always fun watching fellow travellers practice their 'continental' wine tasting skills on wine's they're clearly not always seemingly at ease with. Location isn't relevant as once ensconced at Bulgari, you won't want to leave. Just can't state highly enough that this property is stunning and our stay only rivalled a few nights on an exclusive island in the middle of the Zambezi a few years ago. To cap it off, the complimentary snacks and sun care products around the pool were excellent, the gym terrific and the main pool area lovely. Extended access to our villa on check-out day was made available, great if you have a late flight, we did. If you're visiting Bali and are only staying at Bulgari,you don't need an extended wardrobe, unlike the Banyan Tree, you won't need long pants for dinner attire. We'd stay again in a heartbeat.
insider_tip: All rooms have stunning views but if you have the choice, request a villa closest to the main pool, spa,restaurants and bar area. Walking distance is an advantage.

title: Great view, Great Italian Food, Luxury Villa
review_text: We were staying in the Ocean Cliff (1 Bedroom) Villa with unobstructed view of the ocean. View is stunningly beautiful. Bulgary amenities are first class, high quality furnitures and sound systems. The bed is huge and heavenly comfortable. Personal butler service is good but seems like there are many butlers are taking care of us. Which makes us a little confusing and less personalized. However, it wasn't an big issue and we were still very well taken care of our needs. We highly recommend to dine in iL Ristorante. Very romantic restaurant for dinner comes with good selections of wine and italian meal. House wine are well choosen by the sommelier, and the high compliment goes to the italian chef. Meal was beautifully plated and taste heavenly. Highly recommended. Another recommendation is the "In-villa dining" (not room service). They set up a romantic in-villa dining in your villa, table surrounded by flower petals and frangipani. You could enjoy full-course of wonderful dinner (by the same italian chef from il Ristorante) in your own private villa. Great experience. Spa was a little pricy but worth every penny. Recommend any spa with hot stone. It did make us feel rejuvenated and full of good energy after the treatment. One of the best ! Location is far from everywhere. But if you are spending big bugs to stay here, just switch off your phone and enjoy the beautiful view, great food and spa. After all, that's what holiday is all about isnt' it.? Truly relaxing.

title: Stunning setting let down by inadequate service
review_text: For such an expensive resort, it is only fair to judge it on a strict scale and for that, Bvlgari in Bali is only Average. The view is stunning and the amenities are top notch, but the service and other small details that usually separate a good hotel from a great one were all lacking during my stay. Room: The room is nice. Having stayed at Banyan Tree in Ungasan, I would prefer BT's whole set up and pool. However, Bvlgari amenities and B&O equipment are hard to beat. The rooms are quite clean. The let down for me would be the patio area and the pool. It's just on the small side. The villa was aging and you can tell from the damage on the wooden doors and walls. The bed is really soft and for those who prefer harder mattresses, you may find this slightly uncomfortable. Facilities: My favorite part of this resort is the main pool. The view is stunning and the two mini inifinty pools off to the side are great for couples and friends to share. And where else can you take a shower outdoors while enjoying unobstructed views of the ocean? The gym was not that big, but also not a huge concern for me. Breakfast: The spread is small. You get the bulk of your food from the ala carte menu. The quality is not that great. Again, considering the brand and the price that you pay at this resort, it's fair to expect something great. Unfortunately, I was quite disappointed. Service at breakfast was also slow. It needs to be more proactive. I understand that things tend to be slower in Bali as it should be, but slow doesn't mean inefficient. Service: Overall service at this property is ok if this were a regular 4 or 5 star property. As one of the top hotels in Bali, I expected something mind-blowing. At Banyan Tree, almost everyone remembers your name whether it's the buggy drivers, waiters/waitresses, or the pool attendants. At Bvlgari, you still get kind and genuine service, but it doesn't feel as personal. And again, not always that efficient. Some staff were wonderful, but the consistency just wasn't there. Check-in/Check-out: No major concerns. Fast and no problems with the bill. Would I return? Maybe. It depends. Overall, I enjoyed my time at Bvlgari. You just can't beat that view. It would be nice if they improved on the service and food.

title: Luxury brand hospitality at its best.
review_text: The sweeping vistas and views of this resort will take your breath away. Location, location, location. Edge of the cliff villas spectacularly created in local architecture but awash with the most luxurious amenities and electronics available. The bang and olufsen tv /dvd/sound system was fantastic. They seem to have covered everything even the note on keeping clothes inside and not leaving food stuff out due to the inland's other primate inhabitants was thoughful. The villa was huge and tastefully furnished with local accents and decor. The spa was fantastic as well and extremely well organized. One of the worlds best resorts for sure. Prices are high but you definitely get what you pay for and more. Love this resort.
insider_tip: Do remember to keep your belongings inside the villa prior to calling it a night. The monkeys on the island tend to roam and randomly take things left outside.

title: Paradise in Bali - Wondrous Luxury worth your dollars
review_text: Location - Up amongst the cliffs facing the Indian Ocean, its paradise on earth. Definitely not accessible via public transport but the resorts would have arranged a pick-up service for you to get up there ;-) Getting into the comfortable car, the friendly but professional driver would share with you a bottle of Bvlgari mineral water and the free Wi-Fi available in-vehicle. Already impressed. Decor / Ambience - Soothing, peaceful, wondrous....Its black & white Balinese decor villa-style. Security at the entrance is stringent - knowing that you are safe within the resort definitely helps in the overall experience. Upon reaching the lobby facing the ocean, you would be asked to rest in the comfortable cushions while enjoying the cool ocean breeze off the rising hills around it. The staff would serve you a refreshing mojito while you wait for check-in. Service & Room - Your personal concierge assigned to you after checking-in would then take you on a guided tour round the estate and then to your personal villa. The villa with its private plunge pool overlooking the ocean blows the mind away as you wonder about the possibilities of staying here....forever. The living area and bath facilities are spacious and there is also an outdoor bath shower in case you are not interested in the indoor bath tub....we probably need to take 4 baths a day to enjoy it all. Amenities are all Bvlgari-branded and you could not resist smelling them. We were already distracted even though the butler tried to guide us within the villa ;-) Eventually, we were left to our own devices knowing that we can call our personal concierge anytime for service. The room is also prepped with a local fruits platter and sweets on arrival. There is also a kite left in the cupboard in case you like to do so....Excellent service. Travel within the estate is via your personal buggy with your personal concierge but who really wants to leave the villa? This private villa is more than enough for you to enjoy a romantic day and night with your loved one. Spa / Facilities - So much to write about but being massaged and scrubbed while listening to the crash of the ocean waves against the rocks is......something we've never experienced before and we fell asleep in this haven of nature. Restaurants - We had both dinner and breakfast during our stay (definitely needs a separate review later...) and the amazing views over the cliff to the ocean is enough for you to thoroughly relax and enjoy your meal. ********************** We also wandered around the villa and headed down to the private beach via a mini cable car / escalator which is accessible only by the villa guests). Its hard to describe it but the only word that fits is "Paradise". It feels like your little piece of heaven and very private. Would love to go back again if ever we head back to Bali. The price - USD1500 per night - is definitely worth it.

title: The Bar at Bulgari
review_text: Visited Bulgari for some refreshing cocktails after our morning visit to Uluwatu temple. The Bar is located on the edge of the cliff therefore you are shuttled from the resort lobby by a golf buggy. It receives a beautiful breeze which is perfect on a sunny hot morning and is set out with nice décor and comfortable seats and day beds. The pina coladas made by our bartender were the best we have ever had! The long island ice tea also packed a punch. We have found the staff at the lobby and bar to have been very friendly and welcoming on both occasions we have visited. They are also more than happy to take photos for you. The uniforms worn by the staff at The Bar are also quite stylish with Italian flare. The resort grounds are immaculate and lush with flowers and trees. The water pond adjacent to the bar with its transparent glass bottom is a stunning area to transform into an altar for a wedding. There have been many beautiful weddings at this altar previously! Overall The Bar at Bulgari is a great place to visit for drinks if you are in the Uluwatu area. We are always happy to return anytime we are in the Uluwatu area.

review_text: When deciding to go to Bali, we really wanted to spoil ourselves, so we booked a night at famed Bulgari Hotels and Resorts in Bali. This place is a once in a life time experience.

title: Such a disappointment
review_text: We checked into the resort with high hopes of having a luxury holiday for our family. Such a beautiful and picturesque place. We arrived with our two children 2 and 4 booking a 2 bedroom villa. Unfortunately we weren't able to use our second room as it was in a completely separate structure separated by the plunge pool. All three rooms (2 bedrooms and living area) had subpar cleaning with cobwebs throughout the rooms. We actually requested the cobwebs cleaned in the living space since with was throughout the high ceiling area and was rather unsightly. Both bathtubs had staining and mold around the drains which were cleaned properly when requested. Definitely not what you would expect from a $1800 USD a night resort. The food selection was limited. There was one pool with limited service available there. The beach, while beautiful, was a hike to get to and once you did had a beach that was not swimmable. It was actually quite dangerous. We planned a 3 night stay but left after the second night. When we checked out no one ever asked how our stay was or apologized for the cleanliness. When I asked if there were accomadations that were continuous so we could be in the same structure as the kids but still have a separate room we were told to get a sitter to stay with them in the other room so they could be on their own. We were sorely disappointed and would not recommend this resort to anyone - family or couple.

title: Villas, villas and then there are villas
review_text: We have now stayed at Amanusa, Bulgari and St. Regis in Bali. While we would return to the other two (see reviews), we will not return to Bulgari. Admittedly, we are regular Aman guests so this was our basis of comparison. But given that the pricing and the business model are the same (villa style resort in unique or picturesque locations), we believe the comparison is fair. First, the service was good but not great as in Aman resorts. At Bulgari, a number of requests needed repeating and a few requests were fulfilled incorrectly due to misunderstanding of the request. Second, this property is seriously aging and while there are quite a few maintenance staff visible, the quality of workmanship of the repairs is very visibly substandard to the original construction, providing an ugly contrast...especially since there are so many visible repairs. And then there are the issues that others have written about...with the "band aid" repairs, they can't really fix the problem. And example...we had two leaks in our bathroom, one coming from the sink drain and also coming through two different doors from outside (from the air conditioning condensation, so it was constant). They repaired the sink and it started leaking again two hours later. And they didn't even try to fix the leaking doors...just wiped up the extensive water from the floor that reappeared in an hour. These are just examples...such issues are visible throghout the property...replaced stones with mismatching stones, replaced wood that doesn't quite fit in fit and finish, replaced materials that subsequently fell off and remain broken. Throughout our stay we commented to ourselves that this resort is not worthy of the Bulgari brand and Bulgari should be ashamed to have their brand on such a (relatively) shoddy facility. We have stayed in Aman resorts that are of similar age, but they are kept up to date with quality workmanship. The positive? An exceptional location...

title: Will return every chance we get!!!
review_text: I'm a travel agent. I've never written a trip advisor review after staying at hundreds of hotels worldwide. The experience we had at the Bvlgari Uluwatu has prompted me to write my first ever review and I can't say enough amazing things about our time there! From the original reservation through to checkout, the service was seemless!!! Upon arrival at the airport, the fast track service through customs leading into our cushy Toyota transfer with recliner seats the comfort of business class was above and beyond. I'd explained the visit was a surprise for my partners birthday and both staff members kept the secret perfectly until we sat in the car with complimentary Evian where the Bvlgari sign offering free in-transfer wifi indicated where we were going! My partner was blown away at the surprise! We arrived in the incredible lobby to a refreshing virgin mojito and a generous welcome from our amazing Butler who took us on a guided tour of the property in our golf buggy before arriving at our Oceanview Villa. Worth. Every. Penny. The bedroom, bathroom, private plunge pool overlooking the ocean, living area were all mind-blowing! I couldn't wipe the smile off my face as we were guided through the villa by our butler and left to our own devices knowing anything we needed was just a quick phonecall away! There was a lovely welcome letter, a fruit platter and local sweets for us on arrival - watch out for the local cheeky monkeys who take advantage of an open door to steal the bananas!!! At dusk, a plate of profiteroles with gold leaf were delivered as an appetizer before our dinner. It's one of those places you never have to leave - your own private world is enough to bask in! The in-villa service was wonderful! We had lunch and dinner delivered to the villa with an intricate chocolate cake with personalized Happy Birthday chocolate plaque delivered with dinner as a surprise! The bed is indescribably comfortable!!! Like all of your lifelong wishing-well wishes put together into bed format! The pillows and robes were just as devine along with all of the Bvlgari toiletries throughout the villa! Breakfast the next morning was wonderful with a lovely view over the cliff to the ocean, and options of Indonesian, American or Japanese breakfast. We loved both the American (great poached eggs!) and Indonesian options! We had the lovely Hotel Manager personally approach us to ensure we'd had a lovely stay - I couldn't compliment his hotel and staff enough! The escort down to the private beach (only accessible by Bvlgari clientele) was almost choreographed! We had the beach to ourselves for a time and had towels on our daybeds before we even walked across the sand to them! Once we'd caught the gondola back up to the property, we had a golf buggy waiting for us to take us wherever we wanted to go! The cocktails we ordered were tasty and well priced - about AUD$15 each with tax. All the food and drinks in fact, though pricey for what people might expect out of Bali were much better priced than what you would find in Melbourne for a much lesser hotel so we were pleasantly surprised not to have spent more! I'd go back as much as I could afford to! The service, the property, the location all far exceeded 5star! I can't wait to send my clients here and have them blown away as much as I was!!! Thank you Blvgari Uluwatu, can't wait to come back!!!!!!!!!!!
