title: Personal staff, unbelievable views from the villas, Bvlgari-esque quality
review_text: I wanted to keep the best reviews for last, but I felt compelled to write this one. My wife and I were on a honeymoon and chose Bulgari to be our first of 3 hotels in Bali. We took a taxi from the airport and it took us about 20 to 30 minutes to get there. It was truly an adventure for newcomers and first-timers to Bali because as the taxi took us away from the city and into the quiet small and curvy road, we were finally led to the main gate. It was highly secured with well-trained security staff who checked the trunk and hood of the car (and this became a normal practice for top and well-known resorts in Bali). We were dropped off at the outdoor check-in and were greeted by the friendly staff and - our now favorite - the herbojito! It is mojito + herbs but I think it was uniquely Balinese, if I can safely claim that. We later realize and began to understand that the resort is huge. Your common transport would be the elongated golf cart, or I think they called it the "buggie." Your personal butler (yes, your personal butler) takes you around and will arrive within minutes upon a quick phone call from your villa. There are only villas at this hotel resort and they are very private. The villa consists of a patio with comfortable sofas and a wonderful shade to block the sun, a private pool that is always warm to lounge and sip on your cocktail, and a very sizeable indoor villa that consists of a large bedroom that has a quick access to the outdoor to the pool/patio area via moving window doors, your own small office table located next to the entrance to the indoor villa, two separate closets for you and your lover, and the other highlight of our villa - the restroom. I don't know how to describe this "restroom." It literally is a room that you can rest, shower (love the rainfall shower head with the regular shower head), bathe in the protruding encased bathtub at the center of the room, do your makeup (for my beautiful wife), and an outdoor shower right outside. We ate on our first dinner at Sangkor Restaurant, which deservedly needs a separate review on Tripadvisor later. All the in-room dining that we can order from our villa was perfect. They even provide you with local fruits (like, fruit of the day) everyday in front of your bed. Rental DVDs for your villa can easily be accessed by a phone call and your butler will deliver it to you. We watched 3 movies and they were all good. Was it the movie or the ambiance of watching them in your villa? That's the undiscovered secret of staying at Bulgari - to enjoy even the simple things like watching a movie in the convenience of your room and eating fruits - become amplified at this beautiful hotel resort. Our special thanks to Nana (Sri Murtana) for meeting us at random times during our stay at the hotel resort. He made us feel very welcome, sincerely inquired about the pleasant of our stay, and became a good friend, which you wouldn't find at any hotel resort. We both look forward to meeting him again in our next trip to Bali. Terima kasih.
insider_tip: All are villas with your personal butler and personal pool. Highly recommend Sangkor Restaurant, which is located at the end of the resort and beautiful view of the ocean and the infinity ponds on the side of the restaurant that look quite amazing at day or night. Also, learn Indonesian and even Balinese! It will carry you and your memory of Bulgari - forever.

title: Less for More
review_text: On paper, this seems to be the perfect romantic getaway...a landscape touched by gods, a world-class luxury brand / hotel management company and bricks-&-mortars designed by Antonio Citterio. Bulgari, Bali, almost could have been the perfect resort...except for one "minor" fault...service. While the service is not offensive, it lacks the personable and customized ambience that other top-notch resorts offer. You get about the same amount of attention as if you are wearing shorts and sneakers browsing through a Bulgari jewelry store. If you pay this amount of money for a stay, you expect much more. During my stay, I filed a minor complaint on housekeeping. I repeatedly (3x) asked for a response...if not an apology. None was offered. Since this resort is blessed in so many ways, it definitely attracts the wedding crowd. If you enjoy privacy, then cross your fingers on not meeting the wedding entourage. The packaged weddings seem to be a big draw here. It's great for the newlyweds and the wedding party; not so great if you are on your special romantic getaway. I do have compliments for this resort. The cliff-side view is breath-taking. The 75 degree tram down to the beach was spectacular. The Italian restaurant, Il Ristorante, was fantastic (BTW...if you want Indonesian food, try Warung at Alila not far away). The villas in a typical Balinese garden setting were beautiful...though the dark furnitures in a humid tropic weather do not age well. I might sound overly critical. However, I am entitled at the rates they charge. There are simply many other resorts on this beautiful island which offer more for less.

title: My absolute favorite hotel in Indonesia!
review_text: I travel a lot and this is by far my favorite hotel in the world for an amazingly peaceful vacation. The beauty of the hotel and its surroundings plus the incredible service make it really an otherworldly experience. The view of the ocean from the cliff is stunning, the beach is beautiful, the food (at both restaurants) is delicious, the staff is constantly there, attentive and polite. The villas are your small little sanctuary within the hotel, fluffy beds, huge closet space, fantastic bathroom with a beautiful jet tub and an outdoor shower, and your own yard with your own infinity pool! title: OK. BUT DECEPTIVELY ADVERTISED.
review_text: At first glance, our villa was pretty, if awkwardly-laid-out. But upon closer examination, there were many shortcomings. Door-knobs fell off in my hand, the toilet flooded in the middle of the night, and we found 10 toenail clippings scattered around the main living area. When we pulled back the sheets on one of our beds, we observed a number of tiny bugs. To top it off, the beach was closed. We felt that the website was misleading and made it look like guests could relax and swim on the beach. You can never swim on the beach, and on the day we were there, we were even unable to walk on the beach. There also appears to be a serious fly problem. Flies were swarming around us in our villa and at the restaurant. On the plus side, the staff was extremely pleasant and tried to be accommodating. Every employee we encountered was professional and dedicated. The person who welcomed us, and the butler who was assigned to our villa were amazing, and deserve to work at a facility that reflects their hard work. The property manager was extremely professional. The views were stunning, and the staff was very hospitable. Ultimately, however, we paid for a 5-star experience and got a 3-star result.

title: Great location with hideous villas
review_text: Awesome location/view and the best pool ever. HOWEVER, extremely outdated villas, which is just a complete shame given the level of luxury associated with bulgari resorts. I'm not talking about major face lift here but small stuff that could make a huge difference. Falling door knobs, ever-clogging sinks, worn down chairs and tables, hideous lamps and lighting, phones from the 80's. for the money they charge, definitely under-invested. Also, seems like they don't do anything in containing flies and Mosquitos. Every meal we had, we were sharing our food with tens of flies that often end up swimming in our drinks. FYI, ended up moving to another resort nearby, and we had no fly/mosquito issues there. Staffs. Friendly yet not professional. For luxury resorts, smiles don't and shouldn't cut it. When we checked in, we were escorted to our villa but no explalation as to facility locations, etc, which is at, all times, provided at othetluxury resorts. We weren't even given the resort map... Beach. Was closed during our stay. We were aware but would expect the resort to keep the guests posted on when it would reopen. We had to ask everyday and was provided different answers/dates on a daily basis. Very annoying. Foods. Overall, very good but breakfast needs some revamping. Very limited selections vs other resorts we've been to. And pls contain those flies!! Pretty sick to see flies swarming over anything and everything laid on the buffet tables! In sum, one of the most beautiful resorts we've been to only to be completely tarnished/offset by hideous villas that are significantly underinvested. We couldn't stand it and moved to another resort nearby. For the management, would seriously recommend you to take a good look through the villas and invest accordingly / at par with the level of luxury/comfort the bulgari name commands or should command.

title: Heaven on Earth
review_text: We are seasoned world travelers. We stay at the finest hotels in all of the countries we visit; France, Italy, Russia, UAE, Egypt, Morrocco, etc. The Bulgari was the best hotel amongst all in one of the smallest countries we've traveled. It is a treasure, and a somewhat hidden treasure at that. It is located further south, off the beaten path of the majority of hotels. We made many day trips to ruins and cultural sites while visiting Bali. Returning to the Bulgari at the end of each journey was heavenly, especially when greeted by the warm and professional staff. The hotel is organized by villa's. Each villa has an infinity pool attached to a large 30x30 guest room, which is also has a large, 20x20 room with a semi-sunken bath and a separate shower. The toilet closet is outside of the bath "room". Outside of the guest room is a large open-air sitting and dining area under the exquisitely thatched roof. Each phone call to guest services was returned with an immediate response, which was impressive for a hotel of its size. The only advice a guest must have is to lock their doors when not around, otherwise their welcome basket of local fruits will be stolen by the monkeys; which was our amusing experience. The food was delicious for all of our meals, although the menu at the lounge for lunch is a little too light. The outdoor spa was very relaxing, although I should have told my masseuse to dig a little deeper. The weather in July was sprinkled with short rains, but not daunting. Each rain passed quickly, without lingering humidity in the sunshine.

title: Could have been the best, but not quite.
review_text: Well, I certainly had high expectations for this resort, especially having been to Ayana, both Four Seasons, and St. Regis. I can say this resort has a stunning setting. Oceanfront villas have better views than Ayana and Four Seasons Jambaran. However it's really close to the edge of the cliff versus Ayana has a really nice grass area. Pool is bigger than Four Seasons but smaller than Ayana. However it's empty and we had the whole pool to ourselves for most of the day!! Now, the rooms. I have to say my room was not really maintained well. We had lights with burned out lighbulbs, front entry door not functioning properly, pool was not cleaning properly, bathroom toilet that's hard to flush, phone needed to be replaced, and flooring that's really worn. Worst of all we found out after we got here the beach area is close due to the beach access lift needed maintenance and will be closed until end of month. No noise and no monkeys though, although we really wanted to see some monkeys. This place is secluded and out of the way so don't expect to dine out like you do in Nusa Dua. Breakfast was mediocre, not as good as others. In general their restaurants are weak.

title: A sanctuary
review_text: There are no words to describe this hotel! My husband and I stayed at this hotel for 3 nights which were the last nights of our 21 day honeymoon.. We've been through various places in the Far East , and we've planned the last week to be in Bali .. But when we arrived to Bulgari hotel , it has made us decide to stay at the hotel , explore every part of it and enjoy everything about it... The employees at the reception were so nice and they welcomed us with a cool lemonade and a cool lemon grass scented towel, then they took us in a golf cart to show us the hotel premises and finally we parked at our villa.. It was a villa with the cliff view and a private pool... The villa was spacious , very clean , the bed had crispy white pure soft linens.. The outdoor pool was fabulous , the outdoor searing area was amazing!! title: Perfect Hideaway from Bali's Maddening Crowd
review_text: I've been to Bali several times and it never fails to deliver every time. The Bulgari Bali raised the bar though, and didn't fail to impress in every way. It's away from the touristy areas of the island- just the way I wanted it. The ocean view villa I stayed in afforded a magnificent view of the ocean, whether from the lanai, the plunge pool, or the bed. and the seclusion and privacy of the villa gave the feeling of being in your own island. Toiletries from Lanvin- not sure why they weren't Bulgari, but no issues. The tub, situated in the center of the huge bathroom, gave that extra sense of indulgence. The spa rooms also took in the ocean view very generously and provided that additional touch of relaxation and rejuvenation. Service was great, as one would come to love about the Balinese! The resort isn't much for water activities; the beach was nothing to write home about either. But then again, you don't go to this paradise for the regular stuff that you'd find anywhere else. You go to Bulgari Bali for the experience!

title: Number 1 resort in Bali without a doubt !!!
review_text: We had a fairy-tale wedding ceremony at this awesome resort and was not disappointed at any instance. Although we were worried about rain that might occur during the ceremony, the sky was indeed kind and weather stayed dry and cool the entire day. This led to a spectacular water wedding at sun set which was stunning!! Dinner outside the pavilion was equally sumptuous, where guest were left raving particularly on the bread basket and the desserts. The resort compound spans several hectares and the villa was absolutely gorgeous. The bathroom was impressive and I can attest that it is even bigger than the bedroom itself! Though the resort was expensive, it certainly was worth every penny. Everything exudes luxury and class, from the Bang & Olufsen TV console and sound system to the Italian amenities provided where toothpaste provided was from Marvis. Service was first class with a 24 hours butler service at your call with the press of a button. When we urgently required a steamer to press away wrinkles on gowns and dresses, the resort was accommodating and the steamer was immediately loaned to us at our villa. We were also greeted with a cheerful "Hello" and "Morning" by every single staff we met and they never fail to remember our names. That personal touch impressed me particularly and felt like everyone at the resort was our companions. Lastly, a BIG shout out of thanks to firstly our butler Edi, who made us felt at home in Bali and was accommodating to our every request big or small. Next, we can't express our heart felt thanks enough to Desy, our wedding coordinator, who helped arranged for an unforgettable wedding and obliged to our every wish and desire (all that while being 8 months pregnant!!) Wishing Desy a happy birth of her dragon baby !!!

title: Almost heavenly, but not quiet
review_text: Let's get all the good stuff first. The view from our room was just sensational! We had room service for breakfast and it was excellent. Service was very good, although not as top-notch as some of the other luxury hotels in Bali. What I found to be the low point of this resort is its design. title: Absolute luxury
review_text: We were lucky enough to stay at this resort, it is expensive but for that special treat it is well worth spoiling yourself. As you would expect each villa is fitted out to very high specs and the service is exceptional but what surprised me was the views. The resort perched on high cliffs and the view fom the welcome area is spectacular but they also have a vernicular railway down to the beach which l think is the best in Bali. It is so isolated that only the Bulgari guests can access it via the railway down the cliff face. We absolutely loved our stay here. It is a couples retreat so not suitable for children. I did go to the spa twice - the room opened out to a view overlooking the cliffs - literally breathtaking and as you lie having your massage all you can here is the crashing waves
insider_tip: As close to the restaurant as possible as it is built on a steep hill and the pathways are cobblestone which is difficult when wearing hills

title: Excellent if you are willing to pay
review_text: I think Bvlgari Hotel Bali is more suitable for couples on honeymoon. Stayed there 2 nights for business a couple years ago. The bathroom is massive - excellent. Love the bed and all the Bvlgari amenities. Love the 6 bottles of Equil provided inside the room every single day, the dessert provided in the room as complimentary. title: Amazing!
review_text: We stayed at the Bulgari for our first 2 nights in Bali before moving to a more central hotel in Seminyak to be closer to shopping/ restaurants and to be next to a beach where we can swim as opposed to the beach by the Bulgari which is beautiful but you cannot swim in it. We now regret it because the beach was horrible, the hotel terrible and the town of Seminyak really not great, very dirty and in need of sidewalk and street repairs ASAP. We should have stayed secluded at the Bulgari. Our welcoming at the airport was smooth, we were assisted and quickly went through to obtain visas. We arrived at the resort late at night, we were greeted with a really good mojito to freshen up. We were then driven to our villa and wow! It was gorgeous! Great outdoor area with sitting and dining area as well as private infinity pool and lounge chairs overlooking the ocean with shades should you want to close off the area more although very private. The bedroom was very confortable, the bed linens amazing. We were welcomed with a private message from gm, fruits and dessert. The closet space was very spacious, his and hers separate closets. We were offered a beach bag and beach hats. The washroom was divine! Amazing bathtub and shower and an amazing outdoor shower. You wanted to shower more than twice a day! The amenities offered were so many from insect repellent, nail filer, brushes and much more. The Bulgari products are very good quality and the blow dryer was elchim. The only other hotel I know that elchim is the Petit Hotel in Montreal, an elchim blow dryer is a great touch for women. The breakfast was excellent, food quality, choice and service. The pool was very nice with amazing view and lovely flowers which were also all over the resort. You felt like you were in Italy! The service at the pool was excellent - amazing large towels, great drink service, got free bouchees every hour, sun products were also offered. The washrooms by the pool were also gorgeous. The beach is located low and you can get there by a great lift. The lounge area is very nice with sand. You can go on the beach, however it is not advised to swim. We took a long walk in the morning but at a point lage waves came and we had a hard time escaping them. The only thing this resort is missing is a great beach to lounge by all day. It is a gorgeous place to be, it would be only better if Bulgari built this hotel on an island with an amazing beach to complement this amazing resort. Bali was the wrong location for such a beautiful resort. We did not enjoy Bali and found it very overrated. The monkeys all over the resort were cute until they robbed our villa. We made sure to lock the windows and doors at all times, yet during our absence, a monkey made its way in through the roof which was not properly closed. Engineering and housekeeping immediately came to repair and clean up, however the incident took about an hour to be resolved so we did it get a chance to enjoy our pool as planned before dinner. Also, I did not find that the hotel did anything to compensate for the unpleasant experience given that the monkey got in due to an opening on the roof. On a negative note, we did not enjoy our massage at the spa. The welcoming was nice, however the bathrobes offered did not seem clean, they seemed to have been reused and the bed linens were not white, they had a pattern and did not look clean nor did they smell fresh. The treatment was not great - we were very surprised because they had received awards. We booked a 90 min treatment, but it really lasted 60 to 65 minutes maximum. Overall, this is a resort to stay in at least once however the spa experience needs to be improved to be up to par with the resort according to our experience.

title: The Honeymoon We Never Had...
review_text: After 12 years of marriage, this was the perfect place to go on the honeymoon we never had. We spent two nights here and two nights at Four Seasons Jimbaran Bay. Our apologies, but Four Seasons were a huge disappointment after our first two nights spent at Bvlgari. We have been to many nice hotels, but never one as nice as this one in every sense of the word. It's expensive (but I think everyone should experience this place at least once in their lives) and here are reasons why it's worth every penny: - Villas with beautiful views of the Indian Ocean. Nice shades you can pull down in the outdoor living/dining area in case there is some romance there. - Romantic bathrooms that make you want to take a bath twice a day, everyday. I have never seen such a nice bath tub pillow. Bathroom has every amenity you can think of...including a nail clipper, loofah, incense, candles, hairbrush and hair comb. They also provided a huge umbrella, insect repellent, beach bag, and beach hats. B&O stereo system throughout villa, including in the bathroom too. All Bvlgari bath products. - Very nice welcoming letter from the General Manager, fresh fruit and dessert on arrival. - Stunning "elevator" ride down the cliff to the beach with such friendly, genuine service at the beach. The staff immediately prepared a chair for us and brought us iced water. - Nice breakfast with buffet portion as well as menu portion. Other reviewers mentioned wild monkeys on the premises, but we never once saw a monkey at the restaurant or in our villa. - Many well-decorated bathrooms with showers located throughout the resort. We didn't use any of them, but visited them to see how nice they were. It seemed like there was a person in charge at each bathroom to keep it immaculate. - The spa area is the nicest I have ever seen! The individual rooms, especially the open rooms where you can see/hear the ocean were the best. My husband had the best massage of his life there. While we were were there, we saw preparations for twp weddings. I must admit I wish I could have gotten married there, on the glass platform at the infinity pool overlooking the Indian Ocean. It makes me smile every time I think about it. Maybe I'll just have to get married twice...

title: If you're looking for that "perfect resort" in Bali, look no further.
review_text: Well I don't know where to begin!! :) Bulgari, Bali has that perfect combination of style, comfort, innovativeness, a 'blend of modern with the traditional' feel and much more. Located on a cliff-side, it offers unparalleled views of the sea and has the most amazing sea-side bar. So here we go: For starters, Bulgari is located in the Uluwatu area which is about 45 mins away from popular areas like Kuta and Seminyak. So in case you plan to party / eat out / shop often, Bulgari is probably not where you'd want to stay. Unless you do a few nights at Bulgari and a few nights at a Hotel in Kuta/Seminyak. (See my review of Oberoi Hotel, Seminyak). The Resort - Is simply unbelievable! Manicured-tree lined pathways lead you to your private villa. The villa itself has been kept simple in design but has that chic element that you'd expect from Bvlgari. The surprise for us was the cute little sit out with a mini bar, next to the private pool. The bathrooms are spacious with a tub and outdoor/indoor showers. The private pool overlooks the sea and you cant miss sitting out by the pool during sun-set. Tip: At the time of booking / check-in ask for a front/ sea view villa, although the prices may vary. The Infinity Pool - Is where we spent most of our time relaxing. Overlooking the vast expanse of the Indian Ocean, the Infinity pool at the Bulgari is one of the best in Bali. There are separate Hot and Cold pools too and well set loungers for couples/groups. The Beach - Since Bvlgari is located on by the hill-side, the beach can be accessed only through a lift. (See pictures) It is a rather small stretch of the beach, however, very scenic because of the lush green hill-side next to the beach. Mid-way to the beach you come across this well-thought-upon bar, set in a cottage like structure. Sip on some cocktails after you've taken a dip. Tip: The lift is operational only till 5pm so the beach cannot be accessed after that. The Main Bar and Restaurants - Whatever you do at Bulgari, do not miss booking a table at the sit-out overlooking the sea at the Main Bar. It is the perfect place to spend your evenings and watch the sun-set while the attentive and cheerful staff serve some exquisite cocktails and appetizers. There are 2 restaurants and although they may not be the best in terms of ambience and decor, the food is good. Recommend trying the local Indonesian cuisine esp. the Nasi Goreng. Bulgari leaves you with a longing to go back and as happens with some of the best resorts/places, the whole feeling of it sinks in much later. Like I mentioned earlier,if you're looking for that perfect resort in Bali, really, look no further!
insider_tip: Ask for the sea-facing Villas located right up front.

title: Bliss
review_text: Once you check in, you do not want to leave! The Bulgari grounds are beautifully manicured, and the views are out of this world. Upon checking in to our villa, we were pleasantly surprised. We were expecting a lot, and our expectations were met! The private dipping pool is small but sufficient for a quick dip. And the apartment is very luxurious, from the huge bathroom, wonderful his and hers wardrobes and beautiful outdoor couch/dining area. Our bed was honestly the most comfortable bed I have ever slept in. We had a couples treatment at the spa.. this was seriously wonderful, definitely worth it. The food at Bulgari was good, however let the resort down slightly. There was nothing wrong with any of our meals, but the restaurants just lacked the 'wow factor'. We probably enjoyed breakfast the most, delightful banana pancakes with an unbeatable view.. the perfect view was complete with butterflies fluttering past us. The service was wonderful, aside from a pool attendant who was obviously bored and wanted to chat constantly about where we were from etc, but he meant well. Whist there seemed to be a high occupancy at the resort, it never felt crowded. In fact some times we were the only ones in the main infinity edge pool. Near the main pool is a separate hot and cold spa - also very nice to use. Don't expect to be able to walk along the beach.. although it is nice to take the gondola down to the beach, it is often closed to guests as it is unsafe.. you can however, sit on recliners near the beach and watch the huge waves roll in. We would go back in a heartbeat. Amazing.

title: Breathtaking Views, Intimate Remote Resort
review_text: This resort is luxury at its finest. Carved into a former rainforest, the resort is nestled atop a mountain with sweeping and dramatic vistas overlooking the beach. One must take a vernacular down to the private beach and bar area to access the serene and private each and Indian Ocean. Very remote and secluded, this resort is designed for those who desire to get away from it all, relax and stay within the confines of the resort. A butler escorts you around the property via golf cart and they discourage walking about as it is very steep. The grounds are pristine which is the opposite of most of the rest of the island. Staff was continuously sweeping and cleaning falling flower blooms and keeping common areas tidy. It wa sso clean and immaculate it almost didnt feel real. The bar/ restaurant was exquisite and the staff, food, and beverage offerings were of the highest quality. We lingered for 6 hours one day. The native monkeys remain on the property and become quite aggressive around food. The staff was not completely successful in shoooing them away. One even quickly snatched a breadstick off my plate. I was surprised to see this but one must remember it was built on a former rainforest and monkey sanctuary. It is a gorgous place and very romantic.

title: One of the best stays ever
review_text: The minute you step out of the cab and into the reception area, you feel like you're in heaven. A citadel sort of structure with breathtaking views of the ocean. title: 5 stars hardware 2 stars services
review_text: I stayed in Bulgari Resorts in September 2010, as I was impressed by this location and it's branding and expecting the level of services they could offer. However, I find out that the way they manage the hotel is more on corporate standard rather than personal touch. Butler services is not personal, I didn't get the name of the Butler who drove me to my villa, he just asked to me to press 1 for any assistance, then pressing 1 on the phone actually connected you to the call center and then they despatch a *butler* or staff to help you. They introduced all in-villa faciliates to me during the check-in, I was impressed by the free wi-fi access and trying to connect the Internet when I settled down. It didn't work, I then press 1 to call the Bulter and then they connected me to the helpdesk, they then send *IT Support* or Techican to my villa to fix it, eventually I found out the staff do not know anything about how it works, I eventually rolled up my sleeve and fixed it myself in front of the staff they despatched. I also found out the helpdesk is in Manila. They spent 40mins on it. What a globalized way of managing a 6 stars luxious resort. Location-wise, Uluwatu is kind of remote than any part of Bali, it offers you privacy but also costs some time to travel when dining out. I'd give credit to all the in-room hardware, B&O TV sets, washroom setup, your own private garden in the villa for your workout, cliff side endless swimming pool overlooking Indian Ocean. However, all these setting must first come with the highly professional services and personal touch. With the room rate I was paying, I could enjoy similar level of environment and excellent customer services by COMO Hotel group. I can still name a few bad experience of how poor the bulter services e.g. missing the promise of pickup....at the end I found out the ratio of Bulter to Villa to 1 to 10, given there are 50 villa or more. That was in 2010. I hope they improve.

title: Spectacular facade - finer details severely lacking
review_text: We were recently married at Bvlgari. If you would like details about our wedding, please send me a private message. This review is in regards to the accommodation only - we stayed in a 1 room villa and a 2 room villa. Positives: location is unbeatable with prime views of the Indian Ocean. Service is also generally attentive with prompt responses. Negatives: 1. The air conditioner in the 1 room villa did not work when we arrived (it was VERY HOT) and it took 2-3 hours for maintenance to fix. 2. Both toilets in the 2 room villa did not flush/were blocked throughout the day. This made the toilets pretty disgusting, especially as we had many people around on the wedding day. 3. The electricity did not work in the living area of the 2 room villa. Maintenance had to be called to fix. 4. Air conditioning in the living area of the 2 room villa was either faulty or broken. With all doors closed, you could still barely feel the air conditioning. 5. There was a Nespresso machine in the 2 room villa. However no pods were provided so the machine could not be used. 6. The keys to the villas were difficult to use and constantly jammed in the doors. Unfortunately all the negatives ruined our stay. Ultimately at this price range, you are paying for premium service and the finer details to be perfect. While I believe the service is quite good, the finer details definitely leave alot to be desired and the resort as a whole is in desperate need of better maintenance. I would not spend the money on this resort again until they improve their maintenance as for the amount of money, you could go to many other better places in the world.

title: Hard to leave, great staff
review_text: This is one of the most expensive hotels I have ever stayed in but it's well worth the price tag. Perfect for a honeymoon or romantic escape, the beautifully-designed villas and spectacular setting on cliff top overlooking the Indian Ocean at Uluwatu make this the perfect retreat. Our villa was larger than I expected with large plunge pool and enormous bathroom and outdoor dining/seating area. Even with a private pool, it's hard to ignore the allure of the main pool and classy bar area. The beautiful private beach below the hotel is accessed by a dramatic fernicular ride. We got to experience the Bulagari at its most peaceful when the hotel was not busy, often having the main pool to ourselves, but were also there on a weekend when the resort hosted two weddings. The staff were fantastic even by Bali standards, incredibly professional while friendly and open at the same time. I had been worried the Bulgari would be over-priced and pretentious but after staying there we would definitely go back. I'm surprised at the number of negative reviews here. It is probably time the Bulgari went digital, however, and offer guests access to more movies and music which were reasonably limited (the DVD library did not appear to have been updated since 2006). There was no library either although plenty of magazines available by the pool. Food was superb and the little touches such as complimentary apertifs at the pool and bar really make a difference.

title: Disappointed, doesn't match up to the price tag
review_text: With this price tag, one would expect great linens, clean and comfortable. On our first day, we decided to take it easy and had an afternoon nap. To our greatest shock, the bed had sand all over... Got them to change the sheets. But bed linens were rough. Totally not what I would expect with this price tag. On our second day, while we left our villa for the spa which was just seconds walk from our villa, for a mere 2 hours, the monkeys decided to invite themselves in for a feast, despite the fact that all the doors were shut. My iPad and my husband's cameras had "paw" prints and fruit juices all over. His glasses case had teeth marks... Spa - I would give a 5* for the spa treatment received - both my husband and I had very good therapist. One feedback - please change the in-spa slippers - very uncomfortable to be wearing a pair of room slippers outdoor enroute to the spas on grass and tiles. Especially when it's raining, the room slippers with very thin sole gets wet and it's very uncomfortable to walk with on hard and uneven grounds... Apart from that - congratulations - your spa deserves a 4* for overall experience. F&B - Only had 2 restaurants to choose from and 1 other one by the supposed beach which only serves lunch. In the blistering hot and humid days, it's impossible to even consider dining by the beach... The 2 restaurants - 1 serves local food and 1 Italian. To be honest, we didn't try the Italian - thus, no comments. The local - disappointing. Selection of local food was not great, some items in the menu may even be a tad too fancy. While people who visit the resort are generally fairly well-to-do, it's simply unnecessary to have fancy stuff like lobster and wagyu to make a simple fried rice or noodle, not unless you can do it really well. Balinese sushi in the menu - interesting twist but the seaweed used was not fresh. Tasted like those I have at home which has past the sell-by date. Breakfast was ultra disappointing. A hotel/resort with such price tags - served fruit juices from the bottle - just like those you would get from any 3/4 star ones - worst of all, no labeling - four types of juices served - orange, mango, pineapple and apple - and in a not so brightly-lit place, it was quite a challenge to identify. Chinese congee was lumpy while the little dish of curry chicken was fairly tasty. Although I've asked for late check-out, I don't think I wish to stay in this place much longer. I got up at 5am due to the noise on the doors caused by strong wind but I later got worried wondering when the monkeys will come visit again... The hotel staff are generally quite friendly and nice but I've met 1 or 2 who couldn't quite understand english - 1 example - when I called to ask to transferred to a concierge, to help with restaurant recommendations on the phone, she told me that she didn't understand but if I wanted, she would make reservations for me...

title: the price of peace in Bali is - priceless
review_text: I organized an incentive group of high level business people, 115 people in all, where we took over residency of the whole of Bulgari Resort in late May 2011. These days, it appears that usually people are very quick to write something negative on TripAdvisor in the hope if receiving recompense, an upgrade or "forcing the hotelier under fear of a bad review" to give in to unreasonable demands, so I felt compelled to write about our experience. The logistics of planning, managing, housing, feeding and watering such a large group for 5 days, along with entertainment, internal and external cannot be underestimated. With infinite and detailed planning, and meetings as detailed as I have ever encountered, I can say the teams and staff at Bulgari did not let us down in any way, either by minor errors, or because of unplanned events, or due to lack of attentiveness, process or product. The old adage, "you get what you pay for" could not have been more true. We paid top dollar, and we got exceptional experiences, service and an unforgettable event - that happily, was also enjoyable to manage. F&B, housekeeping, maintenance, senior management all contributed to the success, and the enjoyment by all. in tropical climates you will always find bugs, live with it or stay at home. Wear and tear by relentless weather and water of these climates, combined with the salt corrosion and damage from being literally at the shoreline (just ask any boatie) is a constant maintenance nightmare and can show major signs of wear and tear overnight. Being so close to nature you have the wonder of wild animals, not the wild animals you encounter in Kuta and who lets face it are infinitely more dangerous, an unspoilt beach means just that - no plastic bottles, no plastic bags, no straws, preserved naturally just as mother nature presented it in the first place. Wooden tables carved from locally sourced trees, and stunning displays of craftsmanship in heavy Indonesian teak chairs were on offer, if people took the time to recognize this from a modern cushioned plastic chair. The Resort does show signs of having being used in places, it is not new, but it also oozes peace, tranquility and understated elegance. discreet service is just that - there when you need it - not constantly being pestered Being able to retreat from the madness of other parts of today's Bali was for our group, priceless.

title: Best in Bali!
review_text: My daughter Diane and I spent close to 6 weeks in Bali during July & August 2011. Of the six properties we stayed at, I enjoyed my time at the Bulgari the most. The beach is simply the best at the Bulgari! The service delivered was extraordinary and I felt like I was in paradise. My room was cleaned twice a day and we did not have any problem with bugs as I had in other resorts. The food was delicious at all of the restaurants. I especially loved the beach The setting is natural despite the fact that an elevator crosses the mountainside to carry guests down to the beach. I also stayed at another upscale property along the same stretch of beach and climbing up and down that mountain to get to the beach is quite challenging. The manager of the beach-club seemed to everything about the wildlife surrounding us. He helped us find beautiful shells along the shore. It's different from any other beach I've ever visited. We loved the swimming pool and hot-tubs. Our room was gorgeous and left little to be desired. I'm really glad that this was one of our final destinations because I would have felt let down if we had started out at the best hotel.
insider_tip: This location should be a top destination in itself. Don't expect an exciting nightlife or crowded resort environment. Relax and enjoy the beauty and luxury of it all. Spend time at the beach.

title: Stunning location & villas but dubious food...
review_text: I have read reports in this website complaining about the villas and the food.. hence, I was hesitant about making a reservation especially during the wet monsoon period. Yet, I did so as Bulgari is flexible about the minimum number of nights stay (some top resorts in Thailand, like Six Senses & Trisara insist on a 10 day minimum in peak periods giving you a USD 50k bill!).. and I love Bali anyway... I felt that the criticisms in Tripadvisor about the villas was totally unjustified. Some complain about the design of the villa (one review below talks about the lack of a covered walkway to the villa door entrance, but that is absurd as there is such a device called an "umbrella"..) and others complain about the wear & tear (another reviewer talks about the scratches on the bedroom floor but guess what? Wood scratches!). In reality, the villas are intelligently designed and well built. We stayed at a premier ocean view villa (number 45) and the sea-view was magnificent. Having stayed at all the Amans in Bali as well as the 4 Seasons Jimbaran, I would say that Bulgari's are the best beach villas. The layout maximizes the use of space both for the interior as well as the exterior, and the plunge pool is usable and attractive. All in all, the villas are superb (so please readers, don't whine about minor shortcomings!). Having said how much I loved the villas, I must also admit that the food was pretty disappointing. The breakfast buffet is rather pathetic with limited options. And why have 2 tiered pricing for those who want it with eggs (American) and those who don't (Continental)? I also ordered the breakfast nasi goreng and they used brown rice which is very bizarre (having lived in Asia, I know my fried rice and rice HAS to be fluffy and white). We also stayed for New Year and food there was similarly uninspiring, especially for the hefty price of the New Year's eve event! Thus, overall, I agree with the criticisms on this website about the quality of the food and the hotel really needs to look at a new F&B team - as the hotel is a long drive from anywhere else (apart from the Alila, which has better food) you don't have many other dining options. Also, the wine list is TOTALLY horrific in prices.. it is totally unjustified to charge guests hundreds of bucks (minimum) for a bottle of wine, and even more for champagne!! In spite of the criticisms of food (and cost of wine), this place is a "must see/stay/do" in Bali. The resort is well laid out, amenities are great (even the non-swimmable beach is pretty), service is excellent and the management team are very nice! Uluwatu is an authentic part of Bali that has not been destroyed by mass market tourists, and the Bulgari is in a stunning location. Combine it with a stay at the superb 4 Seasons in Ubud/Sayan and you have the perfect trip to the land of Eat, Pray, Love!
insider_tip: Stay in a Premier Ocean View VIlla as you won't get a view of rooftops in front (and won't have to pay the crazy price for an Ocean Cliff Villa!)

title: A stay that will always be dearly missed
review_text: My family and I stayed at Bulgari last Christmas and it was amazing! Our villa was a two bedroom villa with a huge pool and living area. Here are the things I'd like to highlight: 1. The villa was excellent, with a lot of space. 2. We really enjoyed the spa, the atmosphere, the location, the service, the amazing cliff rooms and the treatment itself. My mom and I have been to lots of spa appointments and it was one of our favorite. 3. Fantastic view, just stunning! 4. We like the fact that we were showered with gifts, with different turndown amenities everyday. 5. Compliment for the bed, it was by far one of the most comfortable bed I've slept on. 6. A lovely Christmas Eve dinner, although it rained for a while. Food at Il Ristorante was delicious especially the truffle and leek amuse bouche. 7. Service was good but could improve. Overall, our stay was great and we were so happy. Thank you Bulgari for a wonderful Christmas vacation!

title: Paradise yet somewhat lost.
review_text: Well another trip another hotel. Sorry I forgot to take pictures. My wife and I decided to go to Bali for New Years. We were booking with AMEX points and the Bulgari Hotels & Resorts Bali was one of the hotels that would could book with points. The photos seemed amazing (and yes the hotel, grounds, and rooms are stunning) so that is the hotel that we choose. Before arriving at the resort I emailed the hotel and they were very accommodating with arranging a pickup for our late arrival to the hotel. In fact we were greeted before we entered customs by a hotel staff member that walked us through the visa on arrival, walked us through around the customs line and had us processed by some higher up why we waited for our bags then got us to our car. On arrival we were taken directly to our room where they checked us in so that we did not even have to go to reception so late. Thank you. Below is my Pro's and Con's list. For every hotel I think you should always try and see both sides to everything so that people reading the reviews can make there own choice based on both sides. PROS: - The View. You can not go wrong anywhere in this hotel from the rooms to the restroom from reception to the pool. This is just fantastic. - The villa. We had a wonderful room king bed room. The bathroom was larger that the bedroom practically and the bedroom was not small at all. This was a small house basically. A wonderful outdoor shower as well as an indoor shower (please see the shower con below). The plunge pool was actually warm. We have stayed in hotels with plunge pools before. The problem is the hotels never heat them they rely on the sun. This one just perfect. Last but not least about the villa is the patio. It is wonderful and really big. There is no lack of space in these accommodations. - The staff. These are some of the nicest people out there. Good day and Roberto especially. These guys were so helpful and kind. - The food in the Indonesian restaurant was really good and the service was great. The italian restaurant that is supposed to be this great restaurant that has won awards was a let down. CONS: - The overall lack of attention to detail. I do not blame the staff at all. I think something like this is taught from the hotel management. My suggestion would be to steal some managers from the Four Seasons. - The shower. It seems like most people complain about this in the reviews. The floor is so rough that it feels like you are walking on the bottom of the sea with rocks on it. - Leakage. Yes with all of the rain our room was leaking from the ceilings and it looked like it had been happening for a while. In addition to this the moisture was coming through the walls and a great deal of this was around the electrical outlets. - Cleanliness. Since we checked in late we jumped into bed and went right to sleep. We woke up in the morning and the bed sheets looked like there was leftover room service stained on the sheets. Yuck. We took the sheets off and had the replaced. Three nights later it was the same thing again. They changed out the sheets and they were dirty again. This should never happen one time let only a second time. - The pool service. This is an example of lack of attention to detail. Once get to the pool you get the towel service and the water. After that other than a snack later you really never hear from anyone again not even if you want to order a drink. I would think a hotel would want to sell sell sell. The towels were pretty old and frayed. - Breakfast. This is a really odd one. When we checked in till about January 2nd the hotel was packed. The choices for breakfast were 4 things on the menu and some pastries, fruit and yogurt, and cereal in a buffet setting. It was not bad but what was really weird is that once the hotel emptied out we were greeted at breakfast and told that we could have anything we would like to have made for breakfast. This is not really a con it was just really weird. It seems like the hotel can not handle a large number of people (management). SUMMARY: This was a hard place to review actually. On one side it was just amazing and we walked out of the hotel loving our stay but we paid with points so the money is not a factor really. I think that if we had paid the huge sum of $1k per night we would have really been bummed out. There is a hotel that we went to for dinner called the Alila Uluwatu. This hotel is not the same style. It is stunning but very sleek and modern. It was very nice to walk around there at night and the food. WOW!!! it was awesome that is all that I can say. The staff were great as well. WOULD I STAY HERE AGAIN? Probably not. I would not be bummed if someone else paid for it but I would not pay for it. I would try out the Alila.

title: Very Very Disappointed
review_text: This hotel is overrated. The villa was generally very dirty. We found dead bodies of the bugs and insects on the bed and shower area when we first checked in to the room. They were not removed after our four days' stay there! The pool in the villa was exceptionally small, the public one was small compared to all other resorts in the same class. The private beach was a joke. Need to take a lift there. All the way were full of ants! The people there had no knowledge as to what time to arrange hotel car to send us to airport! At this price, we feel cheated!!

title: Not what you think..
review_text: I didn’t like my stay at the hotel. I will start with the cons. 1. Unnecessarily expansive. I paid over $1,000 a night for the room. Nothing in the hotel made me believe the hotel was worth a $1,000 a night. 2. Cleanliness. For what I paid I expected there not to be any bugs in my bed. One night we grabbed 6 ants and three spiders from the bed! And yes the door/window were always closed. Plus the entire villa had one trash can! 3. Service. Depends on who you were dealing with. I appreciate why hotels would have non-locals working at the establishment, in this case they had Japanese and Chinese staff members to deal with their Japanese and Chinese clients. But they should at least be able to speak English! I was also very disappointed by the Chinese lady at the concierge desk. When I asked her of good restaurants outside the hotel for dinner, without even making eye contact she handed me a little booklet of different places to visit from her top drawer! 4. Beach didn’t serve any beverages. Pros 1. The hotel probably has the best view in the world. No kidding the view from the top of the cliff can’t be described. 2. Food was great, and I really appreciated the fact that the included breakfast meant I can order it from a menu in my room. First time. 3. Monkeys. They came to our villa, a dozen of them, it was the most exciting thing about this trip. The hotel sits on a jungle-like cliff with plenty of monkeys. 4. The ride/adventure down to the beach. You have to try it to understand the highlight of our trip. 5. SPA was great. All in all, I would not stay at this hotel again, however I do recommend honeymooners spend at least two nights there.

title: Overated!
review_text: For the price you pay to stay at the Bulgari I think there are much better options in Bali, such as Alila, St Regis and The Four Seasons. For this level of hotel I expected much more. Aesthetically the resort was absolutely gorgeous and the room was beautiful. The fit out was of a very high quality as you would expect. Here is where i was let down: Upon arrival we had to wait 30 minutes to get into our room, we arrived after the check in time and i would have expected the room to be ready right away. The pool in the villa was cloudy , has dead worms in it and was dirty, In the evening we arrived back from dinner to find our room swarming with flying bugs, we called and had a man arrive, followed by another 3 staff who sprayed the bugs and informed me we had a nest of them at out front door, it was at this point that i would have expected a room transfer. Instead the room was left for us with all of the dead bugs on the floor and not cleaned up. We continues to have them invade our bed throughout the night which made for an uncomfortable and unromantic night. The hotel cannot control the bugs but they could have moved us to another room with no nest or at least cleaned up the dead bugs. The next morning we went to breakfast that was underwhelming, the eggs were cold and the yoghurt was warm, the service however was excellent, Upon return from breakfast i would have expected the bugs to at least be cleaned from our room but they were not. What I did like was the beautiful resort itself, the service from the butler and the restaurant staff, the bed was absolutely amazing, the most comfortable bed, doona and pillows i have ever slept in, the view was lovely. All in all I would not go again instead i would go to the Four Seasons which is at a better location, has better service and food or the St Regis where I could also say the same.

title: Very Disappointing
review_text: This hotel in NO way resembles the Bulgari in Milan - with regard to design integrity and quality. It feels like a tired old Marriott (or similar) resort... Your first experience of the hotel is from the cliff top pavilion that houses the concierge. Reception is halfway down the cliff. You are greeted by complacent staff, arrangements of stale frangipani and views to the ocean over the rooftops of the cascading villas (dotted with multi-coloured building tarpaulins). Perhaps management could have at least chosen standard black tarpaulins throughout if they were at all concerned that building work would detract from the ambiance of the resort. We booked an Ocean Cliff Villa for $USD1200 (plus taxes of around $240) and received on arrival, as part of the deal, an "upgrade" to a two bedroom Ocean Cliff Villa. This villa comprised of a walled entry garden with one enclosed living pavilion with two adjacent bedroom pavilions, an oceanfront plunge pool and exterior seating arrangements . One had to navigate an obstacle course of dirty pavers, located in a water feature, between the entry gate and the living pavilion. There was a standard shabby and dirty jute door mat at the entry of each pavilion - a lovely welcome. Entering the living pavilion there was a bowl of fruit and a welcome note to Mrs Elysia, from the GM, no mention of Mr.?? We were at once taken aback by the very poor quality of the joinery (minibars, wardrobes etc.), door joinery/hardware and soft furnishings. The minibar unit in the living pavilion and the other built-in joinery were particularly poorly constructed. Only a few of the doors to the pavilions were in working order. Many of the door handles were falling off the doors so it made it difficult to know which door to use to access each pavilion. The decking, surrounding the living pavilion, was in poor condition with sections of dry rot. On entering the small plunge pool one quickly noticed the mould around the edges of the pool. Make shift plywood roof boarding was clumsily retrofitted to link the pavilions (presumably to shield the rain). Yes there were B&O TV's in the bedroom pavilions. But in the main Living Pavilion the view of the ocean was completely obliterated by an extremely large basic LG TV. The bathroom had a shower stall lined with a sandstone type of material on the walls and floor. This sandstone was filthy - marked with mould and dark black streaks of dirt. The shower smelt dank and I needed to wear flip flops to actually stand to enter it. On turning on the water two small brown frogs jumped out of the shower floor waste and hopped around my legs while I showered. Disgusting! I had no desire at all to use the sunken bath as the slip proof stripping on the base of the bath was a dirty grey/brown colour. We weren't able to access the outdoor shower adjacent to the bathroom as again the door handle didn't work. The exterior shower wasn't too appealing either. The sheets and bedding were good quality. We couldn't understand why a large emergency lighting fitting appeared to be retrofitted under the bedside tables. Had the designer or contractor forgotten to include this essential life safety function in the base building design?? The majority of the glazing in the sleeping/living pavilions were covered by metres of pinch pleated block out curtains that dragged onto the floor. There was a coffee machine that had been used, by a previous guest, and not cleaned by housekeeping. An opened can of soda was also in the fridge (from a previous guest). There were NO nibbles of any kind provided - such as nuts, biscuits, chocolates etc. The majority of the shelves in the minibar units were bare except for a layer of dust. A kettle was located on a small stool adjacent to the joinery unit. The villas were generally very dusty. A sad little orchid with two drooping flowers was placed on the desk. We tried three times to order a bucket of ice from room service. On the third try we received a crude timber bucket of ice - no tray, napkins or glasses - just plonked onto the timber table. So horrified at the state of our villa we asked for a tour of one of the ocean cliff villas that we had actually booked. None were available so we were taken to one of the standard ocean view villas - higher up the hill. Although slightly less grotty, than our upgraded villa, it was similar in design and quality. We also decided to take a look at the beach and "beach club". It was a sweaty five minute trip in the inclined elevator. The beach bar looked like a Dai Pai Dong from a back street in Southern China - with some basic chairs and tables on two levels. We took a quick look at the beach. It consisted of a small strip of sand with some sun loungers and guests grilling themselves in the sun - without the possibility of cooling off in the inaccessible surf. The one common area pool, by the other restaurant and bar outlets, was rather small but it did have some nice covered cabanas around it. The weather was very hot and sunny. I can only imagine what it is like here when it rains. The whole resort is on a very steep slope and walking around on very uneven rough cobblestones was not a pleasant experience when it wasn't raining. The golf buggies that the staff drive around also showed signs of wear and tear. On returning to our villa we entered the sleeping pavilion to be greeted by a large mess of orange peel and flesh littered all over the floor by the bed. Obviously one of the resident monkeys had pushed open the closed (but not locked) door to the the room and enjoyed the facilities. We rang and advised the guest relations officer and quickly two chaps came to clean up the mess. They left the remaining oranges, that the chimp hadn't eaten, in the bowl - just incase we were a little peckish later?! So each time we moved between the living and sleeping pavilion we had to ensure that we locked the door. Before long there was a procession of chimps climbing along the seafront wall in front of the plunge pool. Viewing these cute creatures was the only highlight of our stay. Although my heart sank as I realised that they probably also enjoyed the plunge pool as well as the fruit bowl. After a couple of hours, of being watched intently by the chimps and feeling like we were the "animals in the zoo", we decided to leave our villa and have a drink at the main bar and an early dinner. The Italian restaurant had a private function so we booked a table at the Sangkar - the "all day dining" restaurant. Before leaving we rang and asked to meet with the General Manager in the morning in our villa - not mentioning the reason for our request. The Sangkar dinner menu was a mix of "international" items. We settled on the local indonesian fare. The food was good - not outstanding. Our fellow diners (infact all the other guests in the resort that we saw) appeared to be on package deals. The GM approached us during dinner and we had a quick discussion about our disappointment of the quality of the resort. We would have much preferred a chat with him in private. Breakfast was dismal. A really standard buffet with poor quality pastries, over-sweet yoghurt concoctions, sweaty cheese/meats - standard, standard fare - buzzing with flies. There was a small standard menu of congie, eggs etc. cooked to order. We didn't have any appetite to try these options. On leaving, the lock on the sleeping villa fell off the door and fell onto the ground...priceless ! We left early in the morning despite a guaranteed 4pm check-out. When we checked out we were advised that the GM had "comped" the room and all expenses (due to our displeasure at the standard of the product). I'm sure the clientele that we observed may have enjoyed their stay at the Bali Bulgari - probably only interested in the cachet of the Brand Name - and oblivious to the standard of a true five star hotel. There are many excellent, world class, five star resorts in Bali. In our opinion the Bulgari isn't one of them.

title: Dream come true.....Spectacular in every way
review_text: Bvlgari Resort - Bali is the most spectacular resort on earth. The service was outstanding, you name it they had it. (with in minutes) Staff were excellent, very efficient and always ready to please. We went for a friends 40th birthday end of April 2011. Staying in the Bvlgari Villa, our every need was attended to in an instant. The staff at the villa were always around though not in your way at all. title: The best of the best....
review_text: We have stayed 4 times in the ocean cliff villas since the resort opened. It is exceptional. Stunning location, exceptional service and unforgettable sunsets. The staff are extraordinary and remember our names every year we visit - nice touch. Staff anticipate your needs daily with small lovely gestures. The most exceptionally trained and mannered staff I believe that I have encountered. Great english. Rooms - Absolutely amazing. Bang and olufsen TV / sound system. The most luxurious bathroom I have ever seen...... excellent outdoor sala plus infinity pool overlooking the breathtaking ocean views. Waking up each morning in their devine beds is only made better by the views of the ocean from your bedroom. Beware of the monkey factor..... We enjoy their presence and are used to it now, however it can freak some guests out. Clearly the monkeys have excellent taste in location - they've occupied the area of Uluwatu for hundreds / thousands of years. Excellent resort facilities and the most amazing spa with killer views. Two restaurants on site - both excellent. Recommend getting a driver and going outside resort to Banyan Tree and Ailila for dinners and a long lunch at Karma Kandara (the most amazing view from a restaurant in the world ) Excellent bar - take advantage of this uber cool set up to enjoy the sunsets with some cocktails. Love this hotel - we will continue our yearly trips with confidence.

title: Honeymoon Happiness
review_text: My husband (newleyweds) and I travel a great deal and we were floored by this hotel. I rarely take the time to write a review on a hotel unless it's a poor one. The grounds and setting of the Bulgari are so spectacular. Upon entering the property, you will be instantly wowed. Our favorite part about this hotel is that every room is exceptional. Each room is it's own villa (with a pool), with plenty of room and a great view. Our least favorite part of the hotel are the high prices ($30 USD for sunscreen and $250-500 meals!). Once you are in the doors, it seems as though the hotel feels they can rip you off from everything like food to incidentals. Needless to say, it was an amazing place to experience our honeymoon. Don't expect to be immersed into Bali culture. If you're looking for culture, spend a few days in the jungle areas of Bali near Ubud then come to Bulgari. This is definitely a lay around and do nothing kind of resort. P.S. Disregard any reviews you read about bugs and lizards. . . there are bugs and lizards EVERYWHERE in Bali. I hate lizards and it's just something you have to deal with - ha! But we didn't have any in our room, thank god!
insider_tip: Request a room fairly close to the pool and restaurant areas. Every villa has a "Buggy" (golf cart) that will cart you around but sometimes you will find yourself having to wait for one to become available. With a room closer, you can walk.

title: Sunset Cocktails & Canapes
review_text: So we didnt have the pleasure of staying at Bvlgari Resort, Bali but my partner & I did go there for cocktails & canapes while watching the sunset. From the minute you drive through the gates and arrive at the impressive reception, you really get the impression of luxury & exclusiveness. We told the staff we were there for the sunset and cocktails, and without any hesitation, we were escorted to a golf buggy where a very pleasant staff member took us down to the bar. The golf buggy ride down was an excitement all of its own! The big sloping grounds filled with lush gardens and villas gave us a taste of what it would be like to stay there ... once at the bar, greeted and treated with impeccable service, we had 3 rounds of cocktails & shared a delicious tiger prawn pizza. The other bonus was the Italian restaurant next to the bar, would send out tasting canapes (to try to lure you in for a meal, I'm sure) which was a really nice touch. The infinity pools looked amazing, and I'd wished we'd worn our swimmers under our clothes! The sunset was truly beautiful, especially as your view is that of nothing but the expanse of the indian ocean. I highly recommend visiting Bvlgari Resort, even if just for the cocktails!

title: The only hotel worth staying in Bali
review_text: Last year I had diner at the Bulgari Italian restaurant and promised myself I will stay there next time I come to Bali. This is what I did this year and I do not regret any single dollars (and you need a lot) I paid to stay there. It is for me the best hotel in Bali. The sea view from the Uluwatu cliff (where the hotel is located) are just stunning. I seldom had a comparable view. The suites and great. Do not hesitate and book a cliff view room (even if yoy think it is expensive). Breakfast is great, as are the 2 restaurants. There is a liftvto go down to the beach, but you usually can't swim as the waves and current are very strong. The hotel has montain bikes you can use for free (but be prepared to work hard as the hotel is on a cliff). You can also rent a small motorcycle (preferable solution) and get the freedom to go outside the hotel. I will definitely come back.
insider_tip: Cliff view. The most expensive but definitely the best.

title: Spectacular resort, excellent service, loved it
review_text: The Bulgari in Bali has lived up to my expectations - and they were high. Our Ocean Cliff villa was great. Villas - beautifully designed by Antonio Citterio's firm - have great views because the resort sits on the cliffside as if it were an amphitheatre with the sea as the stage, but you will see other villas' roofs if your villa is higher up: choose an Ocean Cliff in order to see nothing but the sea. We enjoyed an excellent level of service at the beach, the bar, the pool, and the spa. The staff is competent, friendly, and eager to please. Overall, I recommend this resort without any hesitations. On the minus side, here are a few suggestions for improvement. Lesser resorts have complimentary yoga classes every day, and with much better teachers: I have had many yoga teachers in my life, and the one who taught the Thursday morning class at Bulgari was in the bottom quintile. Housekeeping in the villa showed a certain stinginess with the supplies: tea was not consistently replenished, and we had to ask for tea twice in four days. Also, why only one bag of bath salts in the bathroom? We had to ask for another bag if we wanted to take more than one bath. We took a trip to Ubud and everything went well, apart from the fact that our driver and guide got a bit lost towards the end as we were looking for the residence of the Bali Healer we were meant to visit at the end of the day's program. We ended up visiting a different healer, Tjokorda Gde Rai instead of Pak Cok Rai, and this was a bit unexpected, although we were told he was from the same family. A very interesting experience.
insider_tip: The feeling of solitude in the Ocean Cliff villas is worth the extra price.

title: Stylish resort in a beautiful setting
review_text: We recently spent 4 nights at the Bulgari resort and didn't leave the resort once. We stayed in a cliff top villa which had beautiful views over the ocean. The room was very well appointed and we had our own plunge pool which was very private. It is quite a long way from the busy shopping and restaurant areas of Bali which can be good or bad depending on the type of holiday you are looking for. The beach restaurant had lovely fresh seafood in an idyllic location. The Italian restaurant in the resort also served beautiful food. There were some very nice touches in the service department. If you sat by the main pool, you were regularly served water and light snacks without feeling any pressure to buy food or drinks. The only slightly irritating aspect to the service was that when walking around the resort, every staff member you came across asked you what room you were staying in which gave you a feeling of surveillance. I'm not sure if this was done for security reasons or just to keep a track on when the guests were.
insider_tip: The cliff top villas are definitely the best rooms but they are expensive

title: Lacking the exquisite delight of its Milan sister (and its price bracket)
review_text: Lacks a unique edge that its above-premium price, Bulgari branding and the reputation of its Milan sister-property (where I've stayed) all demand. Not a bad resort but there are 20 other better and/or better priced alternatives in Bali and nearby. Villas are spacious and well-appointed, although the private plunge pools are all overlooked by other villas. Resort is well laid out but could ANO 5+ star spa; makes little use of the only unique feature: a private cable car to the beach. But since the beach bar shuts at 5pm, there's little point venturing down for a romantic sundowner. Service is friendly and polite, but (again) lacks that premium edge you'd expect and could be any other pleasant similar resort in SE Asia. Probably the biggest disappointment is the F&B - given the distance from any alternative dining venues, I expected more. What you get is an average 'International' restaurant, that served some of the poorest Asian food I've had in the region, and an Italian restaurant where the mains average US$70+ -- acceptable perhaps if you have a Michelin Star, or even if you're serving amazing food, but for tiny portions of same-same pasta, I felt cheated. Overall a pleasant place, but very overpriced and trading off a brand that it doesn't deserve.
insider_tip: Book into one of the other dozens of boutique spas

title: Picturesque view for chillaxing holiday
review_text: I stayed at the hotel with my family, we are lucky as we made the booking early before the trip and got one of the few family villas there. The villa has 2 spacious separate bedrooms with both indoor and outdoor bathrooms at each room (master bedroom has a decent size study area), 1 spacious living room, and a small edgeless pool. The room has a very nice scent and toiletries in the bathroom are all Bvlgari products. Customer and butler services are excellent. However as with many Asian countries, due to the humid weather you are likely to find insects/bugs in the villa then again they do provide guests with insects repellent. Have a walk around and explore the villa, it has a picturesque and breathtaking view. Go to the restaurants to have a peek at the views the villa has to offer. Take the inclined escalator (it's at about 45 angle and few hundred meters down the cliff - fun fun fun!!) to enjoy view of the Indian ocean and sea breeze. Restaurants and bars at the villa are indeed expensive but which other luxurious hotels/resorts are not. If you are after a luxurious holiday, it's worth a try staying in Bulgari Bali for 1-2 nights and just spend your time at the villa. If you'd like to stay longer in Bali, then you may want to stay at a different place for the rest of your stay. Overall, we had a great stay there and definitely love our chillaxing time there! Piece of advice: The villas are at the cliffs and there are wild monkeys from time to time. Usually the monkeys don't interrupt the guests, but to be safe close your bedroom and living room doors at all times especially when there are food in the room!

title: Fantastic views but our villa leaked when it rained
review_text: With sweeping views across the ocean, and perched on top of steep cliffs, the location can't be more fantastic. Service was very friendly and we have a great experience in the restaurants, the gym, and the facilities. There was, however, one recurring problem when we stayed there. The villa leaked but we were not bothered very much and we were not staying for more than 2 nights so we did not report the problem. But I am sure the staff would have been able to sort this out. There are, however, design issues with the villa itself which I doubt the staff can help. The living room area is not connected to the bedroom area with a covered canopy. They are just 2 steps apart but when it rains, one does get wet a little. Of course, one can use the umbrellas provided, but this is so troublesome.

title: The best anniversary ever - thank you Bulgari!
review_text: My husband and I were celebrating our wedding anniversary and decided on the Bulgari Italian restaurant - Il Ristorante - for our anniversary night out. This is an absolutely stunning hotel and is pure luxury. Rafael kindly gave us a guided tour of the property and the villas. The villas are beautiful and decorated with only the finest materials and linens. Stereos and TV’s are all B&O. We started our evening at The Bar with cocktails. The view is absolutely stunning and the service exemplary. The canapés were excellent and the relaxed atmosphere made for the perfect start to the evening. We watched the sunset arm in arm whilst overlooking the beautiful ocean and sipping cocktails. We then moved to Il Ristorante for dinner, which I would have to say is one of the best Italian restaurants I have ever eaten at. Having travelled extensively throughout Europe, Asia and the USA, I am extremely choosy about fine dining. The atmosphere is elegant and the restaurant is designed in an open-air outdoor bale style. As it was our anniversary we always have caviar and champagne. We had requested caviar in advance and the Bulgari graciously obliged. The hotel special ordered it for and it was fabulous, accompanied by the best and freshest condiments and blinis. All the ingredients at this restaurant are of the absolute highest quality. The restaurant also has an excellent and extensive wine list and the sommelier made an excellent recommendation for the perfect wine that went with our dinner. Having eaten until we couldn’t manage another bite – Wagyu Beef Carpaccio, Fois Gras Terrine, Slow Cooked Lamb Rack and Mandarin Marinated Salmon Fillet – we had a quick walk around the gardens in order to make just a bit more room so that we could try the desert! Il Ristorante is a fantastic restaurant and I highly recommend it for special occasions or if you just want some of the best Italian food in Bali. We had a very romantic and fantastic evening. Thank-you to Alejandro and his team for making our anniversary one of the best ever!
