title: When you want to get away from everything. High Luxury.
review_text: After reading the reviews here, we were concerned about the Bulgari. While I'm sure Alila is wonderful (and just down the road), we were duly impressed with Bulgari. To begin with: The location. After Ubud, the silence of the ocean was wonderful. When you arrive by car, the view at the Welcome Area is stunning. The architecture of the resort integrates into the environment naturally. Even if you don't get a massage on resort (cheaper alternatives are well documented on TA), ask for a tour of their facilities. It's amazing how natural moss can become "wallpaper" that enhances the minimal aesthetic! A few thoughts: - Check out the gym. Amazing and they even have spare clothing if you forget it. - Breakfast was delicious. The the Mi Goreng was well prepared...ask for the chef to prepare it spicy. It helps. - Yes, the restaurants and bar are expensive, but you are at the Bulgari! Choose carefully. Note: the organic beers on the menu are the best deals - This is not the resort to go to for a beach. It's more rocks than beach with a steep tram that desends to the ocean. - Expect to encounter quite a few bees. They love the flowers throughout the resort and hang around the food. - The Bulgari amenities were excellent and well stocked. - The pool is larger than it looks in pictures and we always got great chairs. - And the monkeys: Yes, they take some getting used to. When eating at the bar, circa 4pm, pass on the complimentary breadsticks as they will grab them. While annoying, the helpful staff reminded us "We're guests in their homes. The resort was built on their part of the island…" They now feed them on the other side of the resort, which has reduced their aggressiveness. But be prepared, they are common. And we were instructed not to eat outside in our villa, which was a bit of a bummer. - Regarding complaints that I've read on TA about bugs in our room strikes us as a bit strange. When you're in paradise, with a room that has inside and outside environments (and individual plunge pools!), bugs are part of the deal. - When taking taxis from the resort, make sure you state your rate before leaving (reception can help with estimates)…being on the resort immediately ups the perceived value of their passengers, so taxi drivers "adjust" their rates. - One miss: they didn't completely service our room when we arrived and we had to ask them to do so multiple times. Their only real miss. So, in short, we loved the resort. We really didn't want to leave, even to explore the island (we did that earlier in our trip). If you want to get away from everything in pampered luxury, this is it.

title: Not befitting Bulgari Name, Overpriced Given The Drawbacks
review_text: I was there for my honeymoon. Paid USD$1k per night. The location was very remote. Which is ok for a honeymoon and the views from the villa were spectacular and dramatic when we reached the hotel for the first time. However, this initial excitement wore thin when we encountered a number of issues with the hotel room. There were several power outages at the hotel. The electricity went off frequently. Also, whilst the villa had a pool, the pool was not clean. There was clumps of dust on the floor of the pool. Very bad. Spa was good but did not use Bulgari products. They did not replenish the bulgari frangrance at all. All we got was one 5 ml sample! Apart from the measly toileteries, the only link to Bulgari was an overpriced boutique store. Service was ok and the italian restaurant was good but generally very disappointing for USD$1k per night. SHould have stayed at the Four Seasons. There does not appear to be any proper management in this hotel. They did not even make any special arrangement such as a bottle of wine to make up for any of the glitches and the fact that we had indicated that this is a honeymoon for us.

title: We still can't stop talking about that view!
review_text: The Bulgari was our last hotel stay of 3 on our 2 week honeymoon to Bali. It easily had the best view, dare i say best view of any hotel we had ever been (if you like staring at clear blue ocean from atop a very tall sheer cliff). The villa was very nice, very big bedroom with a super comfortable bed and views out to the Indian Ocean. How amazing it was to wake up every morning to that. Huge bathroom with lots of nice ammenities and very soothing rain shower. Excellent service from the hotel staff as well. All that said the cost of the food almost ruins it...it's prohibitively expensive! Even for a high end resort and for someone who has eaten in some of the finest restaurants. I agree with the reviewer who said $40 for nasi goreng was ridiculous (basically fried rice). Yes it has lobster but it's just as good with chicken and you can probably slash the price in half and at least give your guests another option. And yes, we realize wine is expensive in Bali but we saw no bottles under $300 USD on the menu and that was the cheap bad stuff. On our last day in Bali we ventured over to the Alila for lunch and it blew away the food at the Bulgari...and was MUCH cheaper too. Beautiful hotel, would stay again but try to eat somewhere else unless you have very very deep pockets.

title: Overpriced, Nothing Special
review_text: My fiance and I stayed for 2 nights at the Bulgari before we moved to the Alila Villas for 3 nights (also see my review on Alilia) as I couldn't decide which one to go for so I decided to check out both. Turned out Alilia was far better than expected and Bulgari is a bit disappointed. It is the most expensive in terms of the room rate and the food is just ridicously overpriced. A bowl of noodle soup is 40 usd and it doesn't even taste that good. I have been to some of the best resorts in South Africa and I was expecting with a brand like Bulgari, I could expect it to be at the same level but it clearly was not. It is similar to say a Four Season in Koh Samui but much more expensive, even the food tasted much better at a Four Season. The service is good and not much surprises. We had breakfast in our villa the first morning and got surrounded by 20 monkeys trying to steal our food, we called for help to clear out the food however it took them another 15-20mins to come over, so my fiance and I had to grab whatever tea or fodd that we wanted to go back into the room watching the monkeys took over our table outside and eat all the leftovers. It was an interesting experince with nature i suppose but help could have come a bit quicker. The room itself is nicer and big however again not much different than any high end tropical resorts and the design doesn't have any "Bulgari" feel. It's not posh or modern, just typical Balinese dark wood style. The Alila looks more Bulgari than the Bulgari...! They do have B&O TV however a Philips alarm clock? and no Nepresso machine although it's an italian hotel?!! The villa pool is also tiny, although it was funny watching the monkeys dipping into it. The main pool is nice with gorgeous ocean view but again not so big. Food selection is so limited and overpriced, and worst doesn't taste so good. We even tried the Mozerralla pizza for a snack and it was bad, come on, this is again an Italian resort right? The food is 3 times as expensive as a normal resort just because the Nasi Goreng comes with Lobster tail not chicken...but I don't like lobster! Nicest thing is probably that they have a lift to take you down to the beach (so save wathcing the thousand steps) and they also have a bar and lounge chairs there so it was nice to check out the beach although there were more bugs and the waves were fierce. The Spa was good, similar to the one at Alila, they use Espa from the UK and Bulgari amenities which are better than the Alila brand, but again treatments are rather expensive. Given there are so many other new choices in Uluwatu and I love the Alila so much more, I definitely won't go back to Bulgari.

title: Nice, but not exceptionally nice
review_text: We stayed there for a 4-day trip. Everything was amazing -- from the moment you stepped out from the plane to the moment they sent you back to the airport. Once we stepped out from the plane, we were picked up by 2 random men who escorted us to the immigration and did the visa for us. I have no idea where these people from since our holiday package includes 'pick-up from airport' but we didn't expect they would literally pick us up from the plane and did all the visa/luggage for us. Soon after, we were greeted by another woman outside the airport and a luxurious 7-seater car was waiting for us. There're two bottles of chilled acqua panna plus cold towels -- I felt like the Queen of England. We arrived the resort after a short ride. We waited a while in the reception area -- although with the breathtaking view, I guess the check-in process could go quicker. We checked in villa no.59 and were assigned with a butler. The villa itself -- nice but smaller than I expected. For a start, there's no living room so we spent most of our evenings lying in the bed because there's no sofa indoor. There's a pavilion with a dining table and sofa but our food got eaten by monkeys (they did warn us, but I underestimated the monkeys' intelligence, they actually ripped the cling film and ate the fruits!) and there're too many bugs around so not possible to chill out in the evening. The pool -- nice but again small. I ended up going to the main pool for a proper swim -- but if you are not a keen swimmer and simply looking for a pool to stretch your limbs, I guess it's alright. The bathroom -- something I can't complain. It's HUGE and so lavish. The bath tub was placed in the middle of the bathroom and I felt true luxury -- no better place for a self-indulgence retreat. There's a B&O TV in the room and we went down to their DVD library to pick few movies. Sad thing is that their DVD library stocks what I suspect pirate copies of DVD and the machine could not play 90% of the DVDs. As such I guess I can't blame the resort's management because we went down to the mall in town and the DVD shop sold something like pirate copies there as well. So perhaps it's difficult to get genuine copies in Bali. FOOD -- I still miss every dish they serve and would definitely recommend everyone in Bali to go there and try. We had almost every meal with the Sangkar restaurant and a splurge meal with their Italian restaurant. So nice that you have nothing to complain about. Words can't describe how nice the foods were -- you just have to try out by yourself. We didn't try out the spa because it's pricey. We didn't go down to the beach either because we saw the beach being quite rocky so preferred staying next to the pool. I would recommend the resort to my friends but have already planned to stay with the new banyan tree (the ungasan resort) and the Alila Uluwatu for my next visit to Bali.

title: This place is a dream!
review_text: My wife and I are no stranger to luxury villas in Bali. In fact, on our last trip we got married at Ritz (before it became Ayana) and spent a week in one of its villas. This time we decided to try Bulgari and immediately fell in love with it as soon as we reached the welcome pavilion. The view is simply breath-taking! So that's the reason they built this resort in Uluwatu (middle of nowhere). We got an ocean-view villa and found it to be beautifully designed. Indian ocean can be seem from our bed, and the spacious bathroom is surrounded by natural light and greens. The open-air living & dinning area is very comfortable, but in it I was confronted and almost attacked by a large monkey (the resort is full of them) who came in and robbed our food, and peed on the floor before he left. The butler really should warn people about it. The in-villa plunge pool is small but the trendy public pool more than make up for it. The bar and Sangkar restaurant have absolutely spectacular views. All food were quite enjoyable and the large variety and portion they served us for breakfast is very memorable. The resort staff were very friendly and polite, but a bit more efficiency would be appreciated (perhaps we went during a busy week). The resort's private beach can be reached by taking a small inclined elevator. The sands are so soft and it really is quite amazing there, unfortunately not many people bother to visit. This resort is a tad pricy and remotely located, but if one dreams of the perfect balance between elegance and relaxation, then this place is it!

title: Thousands of Bugs crawling all through the bedroom
review_text: My partner and I just came back from an OK dinner at the restaurant and walked into the bedroom to find thousands of bugs and insects everywhere. Sure is romantic - even the bathroom has thousands of bugs in it - so i cant even wash the darn things off. title: Superlative service, even to non-staying guests.
review_text: Visited the Bulgari with my family of seven for dinner at Sangkar in Feb 2010. Dinner had been booked for us by Alila Uluwatu, at which we were staying during our annual trip to Bali. Unfotunately, at the end of our dinner, my 87 year-old grandfather experienced breathing difficulties and had to lie down in the middle of the dining room. I got the staff at the restaurant to call for an amublance to take him to the hospital. Throughout the entire experience, the staff members who assisted us were quick-thinking, helpful and genuinely caring. They transported him through the resort up to where the ambulance was waiting. title: Our favorite resort stay EVER!!!
review_text: I'm writing this review because it's actually been 2 1/2 years since we stayed at the Bulgari Bali, and we are STILL talking about it. Every resort we have stayed at (Bora Bora, Maldives, etc.) we have compared to our night at the Bulgari. Absolutely spectacular in EVERY way! My husband and I traveled to Bali to celebrate my 30th birthday. We stayed at a timeshare resort that was at no charge to us, but traveled around to the Ritz, Four Seasons, and finally the Bulgari for dining options, as we wanted to explore the luxury accommodations we were passing up. After seeing the Bulgari and the *breathtaking* views of the Indian Ocean, my husband snuck off and booked us a night for our last night of our vacation as a surprise to me. What a surprise!!! We stayed in a cliffside villa, and it was SO WORTH IT. WOW, WOW, WOW! Our room was still the nicest we've ever stayed in, and the infinity pool overlooking a 300 ft cliff to the waves crashing below - INDESCRIBABLE! The dining was spectacular....the restaurants....the in-room dining...and the butlers were so amazing. Yes, we had 2 butlers waiting on us hand and foot. We have never felt so luxurious in our entire lives. We still keep planning our trip back to stay at least a week, but since we have 2 kids...we're still waiting. It will definitely be a resort we will return to, no matter how long it takes. This is our ultimate in pampered accommodations, bar none.

title: World-class experience, my new favorite in Bali
review_text: Spent a mini-vacation at the Bulgari and it was GREAT!!!! Sent my wife there for an afternoon of spa treatments whilst I finished some business and then I met her for spectacular sunset drinks. She absolutely raved about the spa itself and the quality of the treatments. We then settled into the room (which has an incredible bathroom, and an amazing B&O sound system, both in the bedroom and bathroom, love it) It's a great and relaxing room that I would have happily spent many more night in. Dinner in the restaurant was a fantastic experience (Asparagus soup with Caviar, yum) and both of the chefs and the restaurant manager were extremely accommodating and helpful. Lunch or In-room dining option has to be the Wagyu Burger. I consider myself a burger connoisseur and this one is perhaps the best I've had. The views are breathtaking and there is a hill tram to take you down close to the beach where you can enjoy light drinks and light food. We happily forgot about the rest of the world whilst sitting here. It really is magical. I am frequently in and out of Bali and I look forward to many more of these mini-trips at the Bulgari.

title: Posh resort
review_text: Security at the resort is very high, as soon as we got to the gates, our car was checked by 2 guards and our luggage by watch dogs! We thought this was too much. Other than that, we thought the resort was incredibly beautiful, luxurious and extravagant in every way. Ocean Villa Our villa faced the sea, however the Spa opposite to our villa blocked a portion of the view (make sure your villa is unobstructed). The villa is furnished extravagantly with white and black being the dominant colors. The bed is the most comfortable bed you'll ever sleep on; the sheets and pillows were of the highest quality too. The bathroom is very modern and there is an oversized bath-tub in the centre. There are indoor and outdoor showers with Bvlgari toiletries. There is a complimentary beach bag for you and your spouse including a straw hat and a kite. The garden in the villa is well landscaped and contains a lovely seating and dining area. The private pool is nice but small and the water was a little too cold to swim in. We didnt feel that our villa was private as people passing the hill behind the spa can look into our pool and tanning beds. However, it was still amazing to sit and tan all day long with an incredible view of the sea a few meters ahead. And the great thing was that we didnt see a single bug, which was amazing as Bali is a tropical island. Service The service was good and the staff were friendly, knowing that we were from the middle east, they provided two middle eastern music CD's in the room specially for us. Although it is mentioned that there is a host to every villa, we didnt hear or see much of our host. We had to make our own restaurant reservations, arrange the buggy and call front desk to inquire about activities and places to visit. Thus we didnt feel the service was personal. Food The food was amazing, really tasty! The breakfast was great and we also had the in-villa dining a couple of times which was fresh, and really tasty. I tried the Balinese food so cant comment on the Italian or others. Spa The Bvlgari spa is the most beautiful spa I've ever seen from an architectural point of view. I just had a hair cut there, which turned out well but took too long. All in all, we had a wonderful stay but thought that the rooms were grossly overpriced, USD1000 for the lowest category room was definitely steep. Also, the overly posh atmosphere had us on edge sometimes and so we werent completely relaxed. I would only go again of they reduced their rates by half or had really good offers although I still do recommend this hotel for a couple of days but not more.

title: Service and views extraordinary
review_text: We just returned from a two-night stay and had a wonderful time. We stayed in villa number 21 which I would highly recommend, as it is situated next to the "Bulgari villa" which is their version of the presidential villa. This makes the villa more peaceful than the others. Now I should say up front that the service in Bali in general is excellent, and at this resort this is certainly the case. Others have mentioned the Four Seasons Jimbaran in comparision to this resort, and I would like to add my thoughts here. Please realise that in Bali there are several different styles of resorts that are located in several different locations - you can have beach (Jimbaran, Nusa Dua, etc.), forest (Ubud), and you can choose to stay on a cliff (Uluwatu). So comparing Bulgari to Four Seasons Jimbaran really makes no sense. The setting is totally different. Seasoned travellers to Bali will know what I'm talking about. We really enjoyed the view from villa number 21. I would not go there and get anything less than a "cliff view" villa, as if you do you will see roofs of other villas. The cliff view villas have just that - a cliff view, unobstructed. So you have been warned in advance. The villas themselves are gorgeous. They are Balinese in design with the extravagance of the Bulgari brand. Now, they do not have wood carvings and statues scattered throughout (if this is how you define "Balinese", but being Asian I think I do have a competent appreciation of this culture). There is a small courtyard surrounding the bathroom, full of plants and tasteful outdoor lighting. The resort makes liberal use of the colour black, the bathub is a blond marble tub with black marble surround that is set in the center, as this is pulled off beautifully. It is actually a perfect fusion of Balinese and contemporary. The outdoor lighting is absolutely brilliant in design - a modern take on Balinese tradition. The facilities are also lovely in design. The pool and outdoor areas are very well done. The meals were good but quite expensive at times - but just go there expecting this and you won't be surprised. The Italian restaurant was the most expensive, but who goes to Bali to eat Italian? I think most of the people eating there were not actually staying at the resort, but rather came from elsewhere to get a glimpse of the place. One of the best "secrets" of this resort is actually the beach grill, we had our lunch there both afternoons and this was the highlight of the stay! The grill is located at the base of the cliff, so you have to take a funicular (slanted elevator) down to it. It is steps from the beach. The view is gorgeous - a panorama of the ocean and absolutely worth the time and price. We had their "daily special" for US $29, which included a meat (lamb or fresh fish), starch, and freshly-made salad. They have a very limited menu and do serve drinks as well. Remember that this is located HALFWAY DOWN A CLIFF, and they prepare the food freshly there on the grill. This means they have to hand-carry everything there. So please take things in perspective. About the beach - you cannot swim as it is full of large rocks, but you can walk about and just enjoy the views, sounds, and multi-coloured sea shells. The staff on the beach are very doting and will get your chair ready, will bring water and face sprays, etc. as well. You can also order from a drinks menu. Soft drinks and fruit drinks were $5 and alcoholic drinks were $12 - very reasonable considering the location! The staff were very friendly and always available. Service is excellent and they are very doting. Once you rang them, services were delivered promptly. The buggy service was offered frequently but we declined it, prefering to walk about the grounds to see the flowers and resident monkeys. The only complaint we could come up with was perhaps the villa is a tad dark at night, and could perhaps use an additional lamp or two? Otherwise, we can complain about nothing.

title: Only stayed for 18 hours - bugs and no running water!
review_text: To be fair it was not one single person's fault that our stay at the Bulgari went horribly wrong. The employees are very well intentioned but they have not been trained as well as say Four Seasons staff. For $1,000 a night we expected perfection. We had already stayed at the Four Seasons Jimbaran Bay and Sayan which were fabulous. The Bulgari is very isolated down near Uluwatu. You cannot walk to town or anywhere outside the hotel and I don't believe there are many restaurants nearby so you are pretty stuck. The views are amazing...cliff-top overlooking the ocean. Rooms were very fancy but not Balinese at all. Huge bathroom and the outdoor space, including plunge pool were great. Now to the bad parts...when we arrived we went down the funicular to the ocean front cafe thinking we would have a casual lunch. We soon realized that the hotel does not do anything casual. It is literally a thatched hut with wooden tables but the only entrees were $45 steaks and seafood dishes. No sandwiches or salads to speak of. Horrible service and the food was inedible. We had dinner at the italian restaurant...food was ok, better service, very expensive. Back to our room where things went downhill...in the middle of the night my husband and I realized we had no running water. Called the front desk and they said they had no idea how long it would be to fix. Then we woke up at 6am and I turned on the light next to the bed and suddenly there were hundreds of bugs all over the bed. I thought at first they had been IN the bed and freaked out. I would post pictures of it here but it is too gross! Our butler came and said he had no idea what it was so we decided to pack up and leave the hotel (later found out it was from the villa roofing which they are supposed to treat for insects). We still had NO running water at 7am when we left and went back to the Four Seasons Jimbaran Bay. To their credit we did get our money back for the one night we stayed there and a gift and apology note were sent to us at the Four Seasons from the Bulgari. We heard that over the next several days they still were having water problems which apparently is not uncommon for them. They looked to be adding some Bulgari Villas but I think they need to get the water and bug situation under control before adding more rooms and guests!

title: Probably one of the top 5 in Bali, but definitely not the best
review_text: Before starting this review, I'd like to say that I have been to Bali in July/August 8 times already, in good conditions, and that I have been to many high end hotels and resorts on the island. I had heard many positive comments/reviews concerning the Bulgari and I was rather excited to stay in this hotel for 4 nights. However I left with a contrasted impression due to several points I will now develop. Pros: -The situation is pretty decent, as the view is definitely breathtaking (one of the nicest in Bali), and the hotel is located less than half an hour away from the International Airport. -The hotel itself is design and, as it is only 34 months old, it is still in good condition. The decoration is modern and more tasteful than at the Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay. -There are two very nice boutiques located within the hotel (they are pretty expensive though). -The breakfast, which is a hybrid between "all you can eat" buffet and a la carte, is delicious and offers a sumptuous view. -The bathroom is beautiful, spacious and clean. It contains 2 showers (outdoor and indoor) and a large bathtub, plus two sinks. Cons: -The service is OK, but definitely not worth the price. Service at Four Seasons Sayan/JImbaran Bay, Alila, any of the Aman hotels is clearly better, personnel is more attentive. A few examples: the buggy (which is indispensable to any movement in the hotel) once took 20 minutes to arrive at my room while "5 to 7 minutes" is announced; the staff at the restaurants is not as professional as in Mozaic, Cire or the restaurant of the Amandari for instance; we had booking issues at the restaurant because our butler forgot to make the reservation for us... -The food at the Italian restaurant, which is supposed to be the best of its kind on the island, is overpriced and not that great. -The beach is nice but it is almost impossible to go swim: the ground is full of rocks and the waves are more of the "surfing caliber" - high and dangerous. -The 2 rooms I've been into were infected with armies of tiny ants. I found some in my bag, inside my laptop... Some came under my keyboard (annoying), and BEHIND my screen (very annoying). In total, I probably killed around 40 before giving up and asking for some bug spray. -The cliffside villas have a great view but the ones that are located higher offer a pretty mediocre view. -The rooms are poorly lit. Huge windows compensate during the day, but the room is somber after sunset. To conclude, overall Bulgari is a classy and sumptuous 5 star hotel. However I would not recommend it to a friend as some other comparable hotels that offer relatively similar prestations are slightly better... and cheaper. I am thinking about the brand new Alila which is extremely design (almost making Bulgari look traditional), offers a better service and serves tastier food for a couple hundred dollars less per night. I would also recommend the Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay, which is very similar to Bulgari (although older) but offers a truly professional service at a lower price.

title: Fine hotel but not worth the hype
review_text: I have been reading the glowing reviews of Bulgari and suspect it is from people who do not frequent Bali a lot. In short, it is a fine hotel but nothing particularly spectacular in virtually any respect (rooms, service, location, etc.). We had a two bedroom villa with a central livingroom. It was fine. The view was spectacular. The restaurant was typical for Bali with a mix of local and international cuisine - all fine. The service in the restaurants was quite attentive to the needs of our children. The beach (although really not swimmable) is something to behold - we were there with the family for an hour without seeing another soul! In short, everything was fine but it is not so different from the Four Seasons Jimbaran but about 2X the price. As a family that returns to Bali every 3 months, we would not return to this hotel. Check out The Samaya in Seminyak (cool and trendy), the Four Seasons in Jimbaran (great for families) or any of the Amans.

title: Luxury at the end of the World
review_text: The Bvlgari Bali has a spectacular location on the cliff of the southern end of Uluwatu. You are greeted at the airy "lobby" and brought down to the actual resort by small golf cars. The grounds of the hotels are perfectly designed. Everything thaughtfully planned, without looking to perfect. The common pool is so beautiful, with several daybeds and sunloungers spread around. To get to the beach you need to take a small cable car, quite steep, but when you get down to the beach, you are standing with on one of the most picture perfect beaches in the world!!! The villas are beautiful, with a good layout and beautiful decorated with furniture by B&B Italia. Each villa has a small plunge pool, all with the same spectacular view. Only thing is that the villas are a little dark inside... I was a little disappointed of the villas, you can experience the same at several other resorts around the island, to a much lower rate. The three restaurants served gourmet cuisine, and it was memorable to dine on the edge of the cliff with thousands of stars above you and the waves rolling on the beach 100 meters below. I missed my special one sitting there alone..... The spa treatment was one of the best ever! Among other treatments, I had a 4 hour session in a private open air pavillion, truly perfect! The spa lady had the special touch that separate mediate massage from the top notch! The treatments are pricey, but worth every rupiah! You go there to experience one of the finest resorts ever build, to worship the sun and get the perfect tan, to have fantastic spa treatments, to eat great food and to pamper yourself. The rate for my villa vwas around USD 900,-. There are no complimentary pick up at the airport, they charge you USD 115,- each way. The Bvlgari is also quite isolated, and you need to hire a driver to get around. Then you can start the discussion, is it worth it..... For a stay of 3/4 nights just to have the experience of the place, absolutely! For a 2 weeks holiday, no.....

title: For complete relaxation, best hotel in the world
review_text: I have traveled to quite a few luxury hotels and this hotel was by far the best. A step above al other luxury hotel chains, which I never thought could be possible. You could be sitting around the pool with 15 other people around but you would feel like you are the only one there. I had never been more relaxed in my life. With all of this said, if you are traveling to Bali for the first time, I would not recommend Bulgari (or at least not for your entire stay in Bali). I believe there are other hotels in Bali that embrace the culture better. The truth is, although the Bulgari hotel was a large part of the best two weeks of my life, I really could have been anywhere. I only with there was one closer to home.

title: This is the Best Hotel in the World -- Bar None
review_text: My wife and I just came back from a 7 night stay at the Bulgari Resort in Bali. Let me preface this review with the fact that we are hotel snobs and have stayed at the finest hotels in the world from Amanyara in Turks to Goerge V in Paris to One & Only to Las Ventanas to countless Four Seasons and Ritz Carltons around the world. Bulgari Bali beats them all and frankly its not even close. We also went to the Four Seasons in Jimbaran Bay and Sayan in Bali to check them out and the Bulgari makes them look like a Holiday Inn (to be fair the one in Sayan is in Ubud and in a very different location and the surroundings are also spectacular). I also should say I have never written a review on a hotel.... The View; Common Areas; Rooms (choose the Ocean Cliff Villa its worth the extra $$); Pool; Food are all amazing. The view from the resort is sort of like Big Sur -- the hotel is perched on a huge cliff overlooking the Indian Ocean. When I say amazing, I am not just referring to a general attention to beauty which you can find in other places but I am saying that down to the coaster you place your drink on the design is top notch on a level we had not seen prior to this. I am not going to get into every detail because really the part of this hotel that takes it to the level of Best in the World is the service. You tell one person one thing and it gets done -- end of story. It gets done with a warm smile and efficiently. If you are walking through the resort you will be greeted by every member of the staff with a smile and they will do just about anything to make your stay comfortable. You have a butler -- ours was Ronni and he was better than most executive assistants I have had and took service to a level that again we had not seen. (go to the bar everyday at about 5 pm for sunset and you will get tiny samples of the most amazing food for free...) I wish I had the time to write about every experience but just go; make your own experience here and it IS worth the travel time -- go in June and July when the weather is best!

title: great location
review_text: The Bulgari resort at uluwatu is located atop a cliff overlooking the indian ocean. the design of the property is unique and it borrows heavily from local- traditional architecture elements and blends it with a contemporary design. The bulgari black theme/colour is very prominent, and as a slight downside to the property, can be quite a challenge to navigate and find your steps in the dimly lit atmosphere post sunset. That apart, there seems little else to complain about. The food is great and though the menu may seem a tad limited, the chef is always happy to accomodate your personal and perhaps sometimes demanding tastes and requests. The meals were something to look fwd to. The staff are friendly and pay great attention to the small details. The rooms/Villas are very comfortable and spacious. Theres a funnicular to take guests to the private beach area down below. There is also a Bvlgari shop on property.

title: Perfect, perfect, perfect!
review_text: Just returned from a week long trip to Bali with our friends. First 3 nights we stayed at the Bulgari and last 4 nights we stayed at the Four Seasons Sayan. We all wish we would have stayed the full 7 nights at the Bulgari. Everything was perfect in every single way possible. The service, the room, the food, etc., etc.. We have stayed in many top hotels around the world and I can honestly say that the Bulgari Bali and the Bulgari Milan (also stayed) are our two top favorite hotels in the world. They are hotel perfection in my mind. And no, I do not have any affiliation with Bulgari whatsoever. Wish I did, discount would be nice. Its seems to me the reviewers that do not like the Bulgari really just like to hear themselves type (226 reviews...really?). I assure you, if you want to kick back and relax you will not be disappointed. However, it is a little bit secluded so if you want to venture out everyday you will need to hire a driver, which is not expensive, but kind of a hassle. I'll miss you Bulgari...til we meet again.

title: Impeccably Bvlgari !!!
review_text: We had a company outing at the resort & even though we had a large group of 30 plus the resort did an excellent job in organising it right to the finest details. The attention to details started right from the airport pickup & the wow factor began the moment we stepped into the resort where we were very warmly welcomed by the General Manager. Cocktails plus canepes were served & we had the opportunity to savour the beauty of the surrounding sea from the welcome lobby. The resort had no trouble catering to a large group at all & they even organised paint ball & golf sessions for us. What better way to soothe our tired muscles after some sporting activities than to go for a tailored spa session which left us so relaxed & rested. We also had a pool side barbeque dinner that included unlimited serving of lobsters ! I must say the setting was amazing with the tables set around the pool side & the chefs serving up the food right next to you !! All the staff we encountered were extremely friendly & helpful & a few of us were so impressed that we have gone back to the resort again over the past year. I would certainly recommend this resort to groups or even couples as the place is large enough to ensure you have enough privacy & yet intimate enough for you to feel the pampering of the staff !!

title: A place to get away from it all!!!
review_text: We have just come back from the Bulgari after 4 fantastic days doing almost nothing!! Overall, we had an excellent vacation, from stepping off the plane and having express immigration clearance provided by the hotel, right through to 6pm checkout on the day we left. On arrival, we were 'upgraded' to a cliff top villa, which was fantastic for the view, but not so great with too many monkeys around! The villa didn't have the'wow' factor that maybe we were expecting, but it was excellent all the same. The best things about the vacation were: Service - fantastic, the staff were exceptionally helpful and efficient, right down to pre-empting our request for a late checkout. Food - fantastic, particularly the Asian cuisine Spa - My girlfriend is the queen of Spa treatments, but says that the treatment she had here was the best of all time!! It must have been impressive!! Scenery - Stunning. The coastline is fantastic to look at, and within the resort itself, the the walkways are immaculate with beautiful flowers The only real negative comment would be on the price of the wine. Whilst the selection is ok, it is far too expensive, with wines on average 3-4 times more expensive than Singapore. A reasonable bottle of red wine is approx $200 USD. The resort is very isolated too, which is ok if you just want to stay in the resort and relax, but it's almost an hour into Seminyak We would definitely go back, if we need another completely relaxing break.

title: Bad Villa Condition and Sloppy Housekeeping
review_text: This resort supposedly represents the pinnacle of luxury in Bali as well as in Asia and have spectacular views. Indeed this hotel / resort is unlike any I have stayed before. The Bulgari chain only have two hotels in the world, and strive to deliver cutting edge luxury with contemporary design and rely on the drawing power of the Bvlgari brand. One thing you need to realize first is that this resort is very very expensive, the most pricey in Bali with rack rates starting in the range of thousand dollars per night. Booked the upper tier cliff ocean villa which has a direct ocean front view. However, it may not make sense to pay for the extras since you can get the same or better view from the resort’s common areas. The villa is divided neatly into outdoor and indoor area. The outdoor area comprises the dinning area, living room, a small ( and I mean very small ) garden and a very small plunge pool ( for dipping only, not for swimming ). The indoor area comprises of the bedroom, closet and bathroom. As expected, the design is modern contemporary and there’s a lot of attention to detail using much custom furniture and fittings. There’s also a stylish B&O TV which I enjoyed very much playing music and watch movies in it. . Floor to Ceiling wood panel windows allow the ocean view to pour into the room. Bathroom is a full ensuite with a deep soaking tub and is nicely designed but a bit too dark ( black is the color used here ). Nice Bvlgari toiletries provided. Bed is very comfy and sheets and bed linens are all first class. It’s also kind of nice to fall asleep listening to the ocean sounds. Unfortunately, there are also too many negatives. Things are not that easy to figure out, starting with the annoying doors that you have to twist the knob and key to a certain position otherwise it won’t open. There are many switches in the villa which took me quite a while to figure out, and I am used to high-tech hotel rooms. The room is also very dark and dim, especially at night with the predominant black and dark brown color scheme. Wireless Internet is complimentary but slow and unreliable. The worse of all is the wear and tear evident everywhere throughout the villa ( see photos ) and this resort is just barely over two years old, the result of using wood extensively which bears the scratch marks over prolong use and is rather unsightly. One standing lamp is so severely stained that it looked like someone just vomited over it. Strange that for the price this resort is charging, it is not doing more to in refurbishing and maintenance and it shows. I have stayed at older hotels and resorts which much better condition. It's a matter of whether the hotel is willing to invest in refurbishment. The wear and tear problem is exacerbated by the sloppy housekeeping of this resort. Dirt and stains all over the villa, cups not cleaned properly and strands of human hair in one of the amenity boxes ( see photos ). Totally unacceptable. Finally, to round up the series of annoying incidents, the aircon became warm middle of the night ( I have to adjust the temperature down to compensate ) and for some weird reasons, the cordless phone gave out a peeping sound too. I did complain about the condition of the villa and the sloppy housekeeping. The resort then waived my F&B charges and promised a complimentary night stay which I shall see if they will fulfilled, more as an evidence that the management is interested to rectify the problem rather than I want to come back to try this resort. Disappointingly, the General Manager whom I was told was informed of this complaint, did not bother to personally apologize to me and instead just sent one of his staff to discuss with me about their proposed compensation. He did sent a email afterwards though. The common areas are very nice, with a nice infinity pool ( but again small ), two restaurants, a bar and a gift shop and even a Bvlgari boutique right inside the resort. There’s a beach but can’t swim in it because of high tide. As expected, the prices for everything here is very expensive. Landscaping throughout the resort is well done indeed and it’s a pleasure to walk around to explore. Service is passable without any major fault but nothing very outstanding as well. Typical of Bali, they will take some time to attend to your request, it’s not immediate. But then this is not a business hotel so perhaps that is to be tolerated. One word of caution. The entire hotel seems to revolve around a specialized group of clientele only, ie the honeymooners and couples looking for a romantic getaway. So only come here to stay if this is your sole purpose, otherwise you will feel seriously out of place and there’s nothing much to do since this place is so secluded. Even if this is your purpose, for the price, there must be better value elsewhere in Bali which boasts literally hundreds of luxury resorts and villas. So in conclusion, this resort is way below expectation. I got a villa in poor condition with extremely sloppy housekeeping and overall, I have to rate this resort a low 2/5. Not withstanding the compensation offered by the resort, all these problems should not have happened in the first place. It’s such a pity because this is frankly a very beautiful resort and if maintain and run properly, has all the potential for greatness.

title: heaven on earth
review_text: i have travelled around the globe, stayed in different properties right from 7 star hotels, villas, resorts to tree houses around the world, but this was one extremely satisfying experience. right from the time we entered to the send off, it was perfect. I was astonished by the brilliance of this property's design, location, creation and sophistication. Every area of this resort is stunningly picturesque. the looby, the pool, the beach, and the bar The Villas are beautifully designed and well thought of, the room was very relaxing and inviting , privacy was definitely protected. The bathroom is bigger than the bedroom with a large tub, choice of out door and indoor shower, plunge pool was cute. The amenities were all well thought. Most holidays are spoilt coz of bad or in appropriate choice of food, in this regard I should say that the bvlgari “a la carte” had everything from Italian, local Indonesian to Indian vegetarian roti, gobi & dhal (my wife being a vegetarian really enjoyed it), and everything tasted so authentic. To top it all the service was extraordinary, the staff were pleasant, polite and helpful, always with a smile. Overall we are very pleased with “The Bvlgari Resort” and look forward to our next visit there. I would recommend this place to any couple, honeymooners, and anyone who’s looking for a fantastic holiday but not cheap” yashwanth, INDIA

title: Very Swish
review_text: We stayed at the Bulgari for the final 2 nights of a 9 night stay in Bali and really enjoyed it. Apart from a little mix-up in which we were given a twin room instead of a king bed, the staff were friendly and attentive! The setting is out of this world and views amazing. The food is fantastic and the bar area great to take in the view and loved being offered snacks for free while having a few drinks. We didn't use the golf buggys at all even though our butler had them at our beck and call, our room was only 2 rows up from the main pool area so we didn't require them. The only dissappointment was the beach as you have to take the stairs and travelator to get down there onky to find it is all reef and you cant swim, you only do that trip once as the humidity and the stairs are a killer. The room was lovely and bathroom was out of this world. I am glad we didn't have a cliff view room as there are lots of monkeys around especially on the cliff so you have to be careful not to leave anything outside the room. The plunge pool was ok, but preferred the main pool area where there was a dj & staff brought you refreshing drinks and snacks for free towards he end of the day. i was glad we were only there for 2 days though as there isn't much to do and my fiance would have been quite bored if we stayed any longer. You need o take a car to go anywhere and there definitely isnt any shopping close by. This place would be perfect for a honeymoon though if you just want to spend time with each other doing very little.

title: Brilliant, as usual...
review_text: I recently spent another stay at this remarkable property, helping to stage dinners and tastings at its recent wine and food festival. I travel through many high-end properties throughout Asia and remain astonished by the brilliance of this property's design and creation. The marriage of an Italian-inspired layout and Balinese shapes and materials is like nothing I have seen before. The wine selection is better than could be expected, given the well- known present difficulties faced by the industry in presenting a wide range of wines in Bali. The cuisine is first-class, especially at the property's open-air Italian restaurant. I am equally impressed by the attention to detail and friendliness shown at all levels by the hotel's staff and management. The highlight is the tiny restaurant three quarters of the way down a cliff to the pounding ocean, which you can only reach via a funicular railway. Once you're there, time stands still. This is a great place at which to stop and do as little as possible. It's not set up for kids in any way, so it's best to use this as an excuse to leave them at home.

title: Breathtaking scenery, impeccable service and luxurioursly comfortable villas!
review_text: Bulgari Bali is one amazing resort with beautifully landscaped grounds that feels like a private garden instead of a large resort. Every angle of the resort is photogenic and picturesque. Villas are generously proportioned (abt 300 sq metres) with a very well designed and large bathroom (probably larger than the bedroom) - with a large soaking tub, outdoor shower (but this has no view), indoor shower and really great amenities (girls - you would like this, they have a powerful professional hairdryer unlike those small little whispery ones). The bedroom has 3 sided floor to ceiling glass doors that is adjacent to private pool and faces the ocean. The bed is deeply comfortable. I didn't care much about for the B&O system. However, the private pool is a little small. Outdoor dining /living areas are well designed and very relaxing to hang out. Service is warm and attentive. There were some small glitches but the staff made up for it with their smiles and positive attitudes Food is of very good quality and both the Cafe and the Italian restaurant serve excellent delicious food that's traditional in ingredients but innovative in presentation. Private beach is nice but a little narrow/short during the high tide. The big pool is very nice and has a great sunset view. Night swims are wonderful too under the stars. Overall the resort has a very private exclusive feel to it and it feels like going to a friend's family villa and not a big resort. We will definitely come back! We booked through CX holidays and it was a pretty good deal too! Thanks for the great holiday!

title: Very over rated!
review_text: I did not stay at this hotel. While I am here in Bali, I decided to visit the hotel for lunch to see if it is worth staying at this hotel in the future. This hotel is a big disappointment. The staff at the entrance appeared unprepared for visitors. They told me visitors are not normally allowed into the restaurant unless they have made appointment ahead of time. They made it look they were making an exception for me. This is not very welcoming at all. The restaurant, Sangkar, is not well maintained. When I arrived, part of the area was still not cleaned of last night stuff. Some of the staff were eating their lunch at one of the tables. The volcanic rocks they used to construct the restaurant turned out to be a big mistake. The rocks absord and release a lot of heat. It is too hot to sit outdoor during day time. Most people opted to sit indoor under A/C. The food was very mediocre (e.g. fish so overcooked it was tough as a board). Over lunch on the patio, I was visited a few times by a monkey which at one stage was within hand reach at my table. The animal was obviously looking for food. The restaurant staff (unlike their colleagues at the entrance) were at least friendly and helpful in getting me a cab back to town. Not sure I would want to spend US$1000 per night to stay at this hotel. Begawan Giri, Four Season, Oberoi which all cost a fraction of this hotel offer better services.

title: Wedding & Honeymoon at the Resort
review_text: This review should have been written long time ago. However it’s better late than never. My husband and I got married at the Resort in May 2007. We looked at private villas and hotels throughout Bali and thought the Bulgari Hotels and Resorts was the best venue for our wedding as it not only could provide hotel services but also provide a very private and exclusive atmosphere. We had approximately 110 guests and it was the largest wedding that the Resort had at the time as the Resort had just opened at the end of 2006. We had Tea ceremony in the morning at the Pavilion, the wedding ceremony in the afternoon at the Gazebo, cocktail at the Pavilion Terrace and finally the reception at the Pavilion both in the evening. The Gazebo was very beautiful, made out of solid dark timber and typical Balinese hut. It situated on the same level as the Pavilion. The Pavilion was even more impressive, the ceiling was decorated with ancient Balinese calendar or so I was told and it opened up to the Terrace over looking the restaurants, the bar and the ocean. The guests were impressed with the stunning views and the beautifully prepared canapés and food during cocktail and reception. The wedding coordinator was very hands on and patience despite minor oversights on the flower arrangements at the reception tables which were not as per agreed drawings and the fact that the coordinator did not coordinating the guests from one event to another. Thankfully we had a family MC who took the role of coordinating. Nevertheless we thought the overall wedding experience was very memorable. I believe since then the Resort have improved their wedding services as they have experienced more weddings. The wedding package also offered a romantic dinner for two at the Gazebo. We thought that was great as it gave us a chance to reminisce over the detail of the wedding day as it happened too fast. We stayed at one of the Villas for the next 4 days after the wedding. The Villa was magnificent, it has state of the art TV and theatre system, huge indoor bathroom, outdoor shower, an individual plunge pool and a open living area. We could have breakfast at the Villa or at Sangkar, the Indonesian restaurant. The Villa came with a private butler and buggy. Each butler was trained to entertain and kept the guests amused. Everyday we received different traditional toys and notes at our Villa. I remember on our first day we had hearts made out of rose petals on the bed. The employees always greeted us by our names and it seemed all of them knew our names. We thought that was quite special. I would recommend choosing a Villa not too far from the main pool and restaurants as you do not want to wait for a buggy every time you want to go anywhere. During the stay we also enjoyed the Spa. The Spa entrance was this beautiful over 100 years old hand craved Javanese Joglo House. The Spa menu offered variety of Asian and European treatments. I had the four hands massage and I thought it was divine. The Bar was very relaxing. In the evening they served complementary canapés and they were different every day. We tried both the Italian and Indonesian restaurant and thought they served excellent food. The infinity pool was stunning especially at night. I would recommend for people who do not want to spend the night to visit the Bar in the afternoon and have dinner at one of the restaurant in the evening as the lighting at night was spectacular and you will enjoy the different atmosphere at sunset and in the evening. Overall we are very pleased with the services and the hospitality of the Resort and look forward to our next visit there.

title: Food, Wine, and relaxation at The Bulgari Hotel & Resorts Bali
review_text: My stay with family at the Bulgari Hotel in Bali was a wonderful experience, a lovely way to unwind and relax away from daily stresses of business and city life. Upon arrival, the staff greeted us with cold herbal tea and refreshing towels that felt like wiping away and detoxing the airplane ride. The villa was very welcoming, clean, and spacious. The bathroom was amazing-bath in center of a spacious room, modern and elegant interior-makes one wish one had a bathroom like that at home, with windows and trees, which gives the feeling of zen. The amenities were all thought out carefully and prepared already for you, the room was very relaxing and inviting , privacy was definitely protected. Most importantly, the food selections were excellent-needless to say, great Italian food (order the ravioli and/or steak) and the Indonesian food was a delight-Nasi Goreng is a definite recommend. Each dish never disappointed-and for wine lovers, you can't imagine their amazing selection, if you don't see it on the list, inquire and I'm sure they have it or find away to get it for you. The service was equally excellent, the staff were pleasant, personable, polite, and welcoming-remember your name (my second visit.) and make recommendations based on your favourite items on the menu. I would recommend this place to any couple, honeymooners, and families-their large suite for family use (first visit) was large enough to accomodate a family, give a feeling of privacy as it has two separate rooms and living room, as well as a larger dipping pool. I've stayed at other great hotels in Bali-the Amans, Oberoi, however, the Bulgari was a nice new habit to make-it feels fresh, clean, quaint, discreetly sophisticated, and the food in the restaurants will complete your stay.

title: Stunning....with a bit of attitude.
review_text: I have to caveat my review by saying that we did not spend the night here. We spent the day here at the spa and then had lunch. We were going to spend a night but were turned off by some of the staff and 'rules' so decided not to. More on that.... This property is stunning. Absolutely beautiful. Probably the most beautiful that I've ever seen actually. The spa is gorgeous and the 'relaxing' area is magnificent. We had the full package spa thing with a private villa. Do NOT pay for the private villa - it is not worth it. The spa services were ok...but the setting is really what you are going for...again, so stunning!!! We looked at a room and they were fabulous - no question. We went to the restaurant and it was fabulous with delicious food, drinks, service - just watch the beautiful sea go by. The pool was magnificent and this is really where the problem came in. We had just spent just as much as a room in their spa, there was 3 people by the pool (the hotel is empty) and when we went to go for a dip after having lunch and drinks (just 2 of us), we were told that the pool was off limits to us. Mind you, we were sitting about 10 yards from the pool. The whole thing just kind of turned us off...I completely understand that there must be policies and if the pool was crowded that this might be an issue...or if we had just showed up for lunch or a drink and then proceeded to camp out by the pool for the day...but, we had just spent almost $1K there and it was 3 or 4 in the afternoon. Anyway, this is a beautiful property and i would recommend one, maybe two nights here if you can afford it (not cheap). More than that would be too much I think.

title: Memorable Honeymoon Trip!
review_text: This trip was our first to Bali. My wife and I had a great stay. She didn’t want to leave when we had to check out on our fifth day. I read the comments written by fellow travelers and just wanted to add my own thoughts: 1. They also just wrote my name down instead of me and my wife. (Also spelled my first name incorrectly). To me it wasn’t a big deal. 2. The TV and Audio in the villas are first class – B&O plasma TV with DVD. They also have a list of DVD and music CD you can check out which I thought was very thoughtful. 3. The plunge pool in the villas are quite small, but the view towards the ocean is excellent. The infinity design is really amazing but I wish the water was a bit warmer. 4. Everything is expensive in the hotel, try to bring your own bottle of wine if possible to save the overcharge. 5. I agree the food in the Italian restaurant is so-so, the grill at the beach is very good. It is not very convenient to go outside the hotel to eat. 6. Four-hand massage is worth it, my opinion, also try to request for ocean view of the spa room. You can actually hear the splash of the ocean waves (what a nice way to fall asleep). 7. Try to go to the main pool when the sun is setting (the pool closes at 6PM), swim close to the edge of the infinity pool and you will find the beautiful view of the ocean. Overall I think if money is not an issue this hotel is definitely worth a trip to if you are planning to stay in Bali!

title: Pure Bliss
review_text: The Bulgari in Bali was the 3rd destination in my honeymoon. I think it's safe to use the cliché "I saved the BEST for last"!! My stay was towards the beginning of Nov, so I was a bit worried about the rain ruining my trip. I guess I was lucky, because the sun was out during the majority of the time, with a few clouds here and there. I truly don’t know where to begin telling you about this resort? I arrived via a hired car my a previous resort in Ubud, and as soon as we got there, the staff was expecting us and greeted us with fresh cold mochitos, and showed us a top-view of the resort (Since the reception area is closer to the main gate of the resort rather than the beach). Mr. Radu Cernia, who then was the acting general manager of the resort gave my wife an I a brief description of the resort and a bit of its history. We were then whisked off to our villa (Ocean Cliff) via a buggy with our butler. The grounds of the resort were amazing, and you get the sense that you are the only person on the resort (While in fact it was about 95% occupied). The villa was extremely nice with very elegant touches worthy of the Bulgari name. The Villa had its own plunge pool, which was a bit small compared to the one in the resort I had come from in UBUD (For those of you wondering, it was the Royal Pita Maha); but the main pool was so amazing you really don’t feel like wasting your time in the Villa pool. There are 2 main restaurants in the resort; one was Italian (Very excellent) and the other was a mix of modern/Indonesian food, which was also pretty good. The Bar area was situated between the main pool and Sangkar restaurant; let me tell you that the bar area is very nice to chill at during the day or at night (Don’t miss their mini burgers and satay). I was very impressed by how Mr. Radu was always around the guests and always keeping sure that everyone was satisfied. In addition, it seemed to us that everyone in the resort knew us by name! The staff greeted my wife and me by our names which I found to be quite impressive. I stayed at the bulgari for four days, and left wanting more! After I checked out, I had a few hours to kill before going to the airport. We decided to relax at the bar with a very amazing view of the ocean. As I asked for the check, they told me that it was on the house, and looked to find a nice head nod from Radu across the other side of the bar, as he came to say goodbye. It was worth every penny I spent there! (Ill try to add my pictures later)

a very amazing view of the ocean as i asked for the check they told me that it was on the house and looked to find a nice head nod from radu across the other side of the bar as he came to say goodbye it was worth every penny i spent there ill try to add my pictures later

title: spectacular setting, but is it in Bali??
review_text: The Bulgari is very well designed by the Citterio Office, it is made in the euro-asian (hip)syle: black and dark woods and stones, finest decor and linen in the villas, Bulgari amenities. The setting is extremely spectacular, the elevator to the beach site and the beach are unique, truely incredible (no swimming due to waves, but doesnt matter)! But: there is not a single sign or hint, showing you being in Bali!Nothing typical, everywhere just anonymous design. It could have been expected, that a Balinese resort refers more to traditional signs, esp. when made by a worldwide renowned design-studio. Only 2 restaurants (Italian and Asian); minus: who (except some Japanese or Taiwanese) wants to eat Italian food in Bali?? Asian food is a bit boring, menu doesnt change. So food and wines were not a highlight like all resorts we been in Bali the weeks before. The "butler-system" is more on a housekeeping-basis, and not really working. Staff is OK, but not too friendly. In our impression, they are trained to be a little formal, not so warm, helpful and frinedly like all other Balines we got to know during our trip. Overall it was a very pleasant 3-days cool-down of our Bali-trip, but nothing to remind long. The gorgeous views and the beach elevator are worth the stay, but dont miss to stay elsewhere in the country (esp. at The Chedi Club in Ubud)!

title: Stylish, slick but not quite what you expect
review_text: My husband and I stayed here for 4 nights in June and had a beautiful stay. The hotel grounds are amazing and the suites are incredibly stylish and well appointed. We found the GM and the other senior managers most welcoming and ready to give a superior service but this didn't quite trickle down to the staff. The Balinese staff are wonderful but their training needs to be more polished. Breakfast we waited 10 minutes to get served, with three other couples in the restaurant, and the inability to get a cup of tea which had left to brew astounded me with the price that the cup of tea costs. Slightly frustrating. For the first two days it was buffet then they decided to change it to a-la carte??? The poolside menu is pretentious, who wants a Wagyu beef burger or a pizza with foie grass on it? Simplicity is so much more stylish. The poolside menu is so expensive it is a bit off putting. Sunset cocktails are a must, the strawberry caprioska's are divine, and with this they serve 7 mini dishes from the Italian restaurant. This is worth it. The restaurants are really nice but again who wants lobster in their nasi goreng, not me. I see their luxury angle but it is a bit lost on me when there are so many good restaurants in Bali which are into simple, organic healthy food. There were a number of cashed of people who were drinking red wine in 90% humidity who obviously thought that this is what the rich do???? I understand champagne,chilled white wine or cocktails but a bottle of Grange in 36 degree heat, yuck. The spa is divine. I was in an ocean view suite and the scrub and massge was really good worth the money. We will go back there but we do get better service at a hotel whihc is not nearly as expensive as has so many restaurants shopping etc around it. It really is a couples only hotel. I would not go there with a group of friends. I hope that the staff have been better inducted and the menu has been simplified when we go back there.

title: pretentious and disappointing
review_text: Me and my boyfriend spent 9 nights in Bulgari resort in Bali, and we were very excited to try out the new resort everyone was talking about. My first impression on bulgari was not good. Online reservation system was not giving much hope, so I left them a message to contact me. Of course, nobody did, so I called their UK number. The girl on the phone couldn’t tell me the difference between differently priced rooms, told me my preferences were noted but not guaranteed, and managed to write down both name and e-mail wrong, even after several rounds of spelling (i found that out after not getting the confirmation e-mail, so called to see what’s going on). Second big disappointment came with an e-mail, asking if we’d like a pick-up at the airport, quoting several different prices for different types of vehicles. I must say it never ever happened to me before that a $1000+ per night resort charges for airport transfer. And what a charge this was! A half an hour ride from airport to the hotel goes from $85 to $100+, depending on the car you choose, in an island where a hotel car with a driver usually costs no more than $20 per hour. One more thing they also offered is immigration assistance, of course, coming again with a fee (must I say that every good hotel in Bali has this service included). My first impression on the resort was – none. We arrived in the evening, and the resort is so badly lit you can’t see anything of it really (need to be careful when walking to your room after that extra glass of wine for dinner). Same goes for the room – we were very disappointed with the design – both aesthetically and practically . Maybe we expected a lot more (being fans of both Antonio Citterio and Bulgari hotel in Milan), so our disappointment was bigger. The overall design is quite simple. The layout of the room is not very well thought off, all the doors are extremely narrow - we’re both quite slim, and yet we were banging our shoulders every time when going through the doors; the entrance to the room is blocked by a writing desk – if one is sitting at the desk, the other person going in/out needs to squeeze in order to go through; a corridor (containing wardrobes) with extremely low ceiling divides the bedroom and the bathroom, which are both quite big. The narrowness of the corridors and the overall feeling of claustrophobia is accentuated by noticeable lack of light, both sunlight and artificial lighting. The villa is gloomy both during the day and night - I’ve had mild headaches from huge effort to see better. Even the villa pool deck is in shade most of the day, so we kept chasing that little sun beam whenever we chose to stay in our room instead of going down to the pool. The welcome note left in the room addressed my boyfriend only, there wasn’t even a slightest hint on the note that they’re aware somebody’s accompanying him, even though I booked the room, and left them both our names and details. This manner was unfortunately utilized throughout our stay so that majority of staff was addressing him and him only – Good morning, sir, goodbye, sir, have a lovely day mr. I – when i’m obviously walking next to him. Wireless internet is available free of charge throughout the resort, and considering you’re in Bali, it’s not even that bad. There’s a TV with a DVD player in the bedroom, and a cd player with radio tuner in the bathroom. In-villa mini-bar offers a choice of sodas and beers, limited choice of fruit juices, no milk (on my first day I asked having milk in the fridge, so they brought some and refilled every day), biscuits only for snack, champagne (no wine – you need to call room service for that), some bad coffee and tea that tastes like weeds (having tried it the first day, I asked to have green tea instead, so they brought me jasmine green tea every day). The resort has two restaurants, italian and asian fusion. We were told by some people that the food is a bit “over the top”, and that’s exactly the right way to describe it. Especially in the italian restaurant – everything was served in such fashion, that I think it would scare even the most formal dinners–black tie-crowd. And yet, with no background for it – we were so disappointed with the food – almost everything on the menu was merely tasteless. Add to this minuscule portions, and you’re bound to stay hungry throughout your stay. With a healthy appetite, you should prepare yourself for the full , 5-course dinner in order to fill your tummy (or go out for dinners). I must say though that the grill @ the beach club is superb. But my biggest disappointment of all was coffee. At least I expected good coffee in a resort with an italian brand behind it. Nope. It was so bad we haven’t finished the cup once. We even gave them instructions on how to make it, after a series of bad lattes and cappuccinos, but the result was still disastrous. So we just gave up and drank tea instead, waiting for some better coffee days to some. Also, don’t expect enjoying your sunset mojito by the pool – we switched to corona after several unsuccessful cocktails. If you’re beach lovers, this resort is definitely not for you. The hotel is positioned high on a cliff, and it takes some stairs, an elevator, than stairs again to get to the beach, which is btw coral beach, so swimming is prohibited. A few more issues: they haven’t found a way to stop humidity from going into pillows and matress, so they were very smelly – i had the impression i could sense the smell of various people sleeping in that bed before me. The resort is crowded with villas – 59 – and you have no feeling of privacy. You constantly hear your neighbours talking and buggies going by. The pool and the pool are are to small for hotel this size – considering the pool in the villa is more like a bathtub than a pool, and that nobody goes to the beach – so it can be a bit crowded. Oh, and they haven’t adopted the wonderful no-signing policy, so you need to spare the extra few moments after every drink/meal to sign the bill. Also, everything is ridiculously expensive. After all these things, I must say the service was almost impeccable. The staff were always friendly and smiling, almost all of them spoke very good english and we almost never called for anything twice. Though I must say their friendliness sometimes bordered with annoying – it can be a bit tiring saying hello and goodbye 10 times/minute. We were often interrupted during our meal - at one occasion our dinner was interrupted 4 times in less than10 minutes by four different staff members, coming to ask if everything was alright. And then, there’s also the “addressing the mr only” thing. I need to say that some details are charming – a kite, a deck of cards and a little black book in your beach bag, great choice of bathroom amenities, fresh fruits and lovely desserts delivered daily and cute little presents left in your room leaves a nice touch. The goodbye present was very nice and original My intention was not to trash the hotel – it’s not a bad hotel, after all, but since it is trying to position itself to the level of aman resorts and it’s probably the most expensive hotel in bali, I feel I needed to comment on it exactly on that level. For this money (rooms start at $1060++) you’d be much better off in one of the amans or alternatively four seasons.

title: Heaven on Earth!
review_text: The Bulgari resort in Bali is utterly amazing. My husband and I stayed for 2 nights in May of 2007 for our honeymoon. Since I had planned the wedding, I decided to let him plan the surprise honeymoon - I had no idea where we were going until we got to the airport! The Bulgari was also part of the surprise...and I was blown away! From the moment we stepped off the plane and were greeted upon arrival at the Denpasar Airport by a friendly hostess and car and driver, it was nothing but first-class service all the way. Driving into the Bulgari compound, it hits you right away how calm, peaceful and relaxing your stay is going to be. We were greeted at the "Arrival Pavilion" by Tracey (the General Manager?) and several staff...all incredibly friendly and helpful. There is no "check-in", rather, you are taken to your private villa by buggy (golf carts) as the resort is quite large. The villas are BREATH-TAKING. OMG, where do I begin. There are some nice, candid photos posted on this site, but still, the pictures don't do it justice. Service is also amazing...the most helpful, friendly staff I have ever encountered. I could go on and on...truly one of the best experiences I have ever had...no...the BEST experience I have ever had, hands-down! It's pricey, that's for sure. But totally worth it - even if you just stay for one night.

title: Incredible Bulgari Lives Up to the Hype
review_text: My partner and I just spent one dreamy day and night at the new Bulgari Resort in Uluwatu. It was the last day of a 10-day Bali vacation; we were looking for one final blissful impression of Bali for the road, having spent a few nights at the Chedi Club in Ubud (highly recommended) and a week at a private villa. We got what we came for, and more. We were greeted by the manager, who invited us and our driver to relax with a welcome drink in the expansive, solid stone entry pavillian. All local black, roughcut stone, this impressive pavillian is complemented by cream colored cusions and huge scale pots. It feels like a temple, especially when you descend the main level to join your butler and "buggy". Although it is possible to walk the grounds of the Bulgari Resort by foot, the preferred mode of transport is by motorized buggy. The hills and angles of the grading are steep! The manager apologized while explaining that some nearby construction on the north side of the estate had created a noisy situation for the cliffside villas. We could hear the clanging, but I had chosen that room category specifically for the uninterrupted Indian Ocean view, so we took our chances with it and were very happy with our decision. We could not hear any of the work from inside our villa, and the view was breathtaking, spectacular, and worth every penny. Our butler made arrangements for us to have complementary spa treatments to offset the inconvenience of the room, so we also got to experience the spa facility. The spa reception is in an antique carved wooden Javanese house. The carving creates an intricate pattern of openings in the walls so that in the evening the whole building lights up like a lantern from the inside. It's gorgeous. The relaxation area where you wait before and after a treatment is across a pond and includes a private pool facing the ocean. Also on a cliffside location, the spa rooms are open air yet private, providing a blissful ambience and gentle breeze. The therapist selected products from the ESPA line based on how we wanted to feel after the treatments and what plans we had for immediately after the treatments. We were both very relaxed afterwards. We stayed in villa 18, a cliff pool villa toward the western edge of the property. The view was completely uninterrupted oceanfront, except for the occasional monkey. The Uluwatu temple is very close to the resort, and the monkeys are used to people, so it's not surprising to have them snooping around the grounds. The layout of the villas is great, with a large outdoor living and entertaining area including the plunge pool. The newness of the hotel accounts for a few rough edges such as a few missing rocks in rock walls, doors and drawers that open and close roughly, and some wiring that is clearly waiting to be hooked up to lighting fixtures. We had missing fixtures in the interior dressing area and in the exterior garden by the pool. We expected small details like that given the Bulgari opened only a few weeks ago. The sheer spectacle of the resort coupled with the excellent staff and facilities (spa, restarants, bar) left a very positive impression with us. I can't wait to go back!

title: Great villas, exceptional food
review_text: We were anxious to see the Bulgari hotel, having stayed at two other resorts in Bali and everyone talking about it. There was a slight concern about the service level since the hotel just opened a few weeks ago. The facilities of the villas are outstanding. Plunge pool, outside terraces and space, GREAT food and restaurants. It has a different feel than the Amanresorts. If you are looking for true quietness, you might be better off at one of the Amans'. However, the Bulgari manages to bring in a stylish feeling to Bali which one probably cannot find anywhere else, without destroying the Bali style and spirit. To me, a perfect mix! The service was good but not perfect as is to be expected at such a new resort. I m not someone who stops the watch to see how quick my breakfast is here...so I had no issues at all. My wife and I spent 2 full days just at the villa it was so nice without leaving it. It is not cheap but if one looks for a stylish alternative to the Aman's go for it !

title: The new luxurious and elegant style--- Bvlgari Bali
review_text: In Bvlgari Hotel and resort in Bali, time seems to stop. Everything is so peaceful. We have been to a number of good hotels, like One&Only Reethi Rah and Soneva Gili in Maldives, Bayan Tree Phuket, Bintan and Seychelles, Amanresort in Phillipine. Bvlgari in Bali provides another different luxurious and elegant style. The quality and food at the two restaurants are quite good, especially the service is excellent. The villas were built on the cliff, it is not private enough for some villas' open spaces. You can't swim in nakedness in your villa. But the public swimming pool is so amazing. Overall impression of the Bvlgari in Bali: Absolutely magnificent. Incredible cliff sea view . By the way, if you stay in cliff villa, please don't feed monkeys!
